1	# Constraints: m45-d1-viewer-route-fix
2	
3	## Must Preserve
4	
5	- Back-compat on `/transcripts` JSON response: `Accept: application/json` and `Accept: */*` both return `{ spawns: [...] }` in the same shape as v3.18.13.
6	- All existing `/transcript/:id` behavior is untouched (D1 does not modify `handleTranscriptPage` or the SSE tail stream).
7	
8	## Must Read Before Using
9	
10	- `scripts/gsd-t-dashboard-server.js` — specifically `handleTranscriptsList` (lines ~179–196), `handleTranscriptPage` (lines ~275–285), and the exports block at the bottom.
11	- `scripts/gsd-t-transcript.html` (no edits — but read to confirm the empty-spawn-id path does not crash):
12	  - The `data-spawn-id="__SPAWN_ID__"` placeholder on `<body>` (line 141).
13	  - The `const spawnId = document.body.getAttribute('data-spawn-id');` line (180) and `initialId = (location.hash || '').slice(1) || spawnId;` branch (730).
14	  - The `pollSpawns()` function — it calls `/api/spawns-index` which returns the full list regardless of the current spawn-id.
15	- `test/transcripts-html-page.test.js` — to understand what's being asserted before refactoring.
16	
17	## Substitution Strategy
18	
19	The existing `handleTranscriptPage` replaces `__SPAWN_ID__` with the URL-path spawn-id via `.replace(/__SPAWN_ID__/g, spawnId)`. For `/transcripts` (no spawn selected), substitute with an empty string `""`. The viewer's `initialId` logic falls through to `location.hash` (also empty), so `connect('')` is the call path. Verify `connect('')` does not throw — it will attempt to open an SSE to `/transcript//stream` which will 404, but the viewer's left rail continues to populate independently.
20	
21	**Alternative**: substitute with a sentinel like `"__none__"` and let the viewer detect it and skip `connect()` on empty/sentinel. Pick whichever is simpler to test. Prefer empty string unless testing reveals a race.
22	
23	## Must NOT Do
24	
25	- Do NOT delete `renderTranscriptsHtml` until the tests that reference it are refactored in the same commit. (If D1 chooses not to delete it, that's fine — dead code is cleaner to remove after manual smoke confirms the new route works.)
26	- Do NOT change the `/transcripts` JSON shape.
27	- Do NOT introduce a redirect (301/302) — serve the HTML directly. Redirects confuse the browser's URL bar and break bookmarks.
28	
29	## Pre-Commit Doc Ripple
30	
31	- Update `docs/architecture.md` if it documents the `/transcripts` route (grep first; only add if the existing docs mention the standalone page).
32	- No new contract to bump — just a row in progress.md Decision Log.

Add fallback: if no spawn-NDJSON modified within last 30s AND a fresh `in-session-*.ndjson` exists, target that instead. Do NOT edit `~/.claude/settings.json` — template is the source of truth.

content.content:
1	# M45 Partition — Conversation-Stream Observability
2	
3	**Status**: PARTITIONED
4	**Date**: 2026-04-23
5	**Target version**: 3.18.14
6	**Domains**: 2 (D1 viewer-route-fix · D2 in-session-conversation-capture)
7	**Waves**: 2 (Wave 1 — D1 alone, Wave 2 — D2 alone) *OR* single parallel wave (see Wave Plan below)
8	**Rationale source**: M45 scope 2026-04-23 (see `.gsd-t/progress.md` M45 "Current Milestone" block).
9	
10	## Theme
11	
12	Make the orchestrator session's conversational turns visible in the visualizer transcript stream alongside spawned-agent transcripts. The `/transcripts` route is also promoted to serve the real viewer (left rail + main + right spawn-plan panel) so that empty-state UX matches what the user already expects.
13	
14	This is a deliberately small milestone — two file-disjoint domains designed as a concrete test fixture for M44 parallelism (both file-disjoint, both provably under M44 D6 economics gates).
15	
16	## Domains
17	
18	### D1 — m45-d1-viewer-route-fix
19	
20	**Responsibility**: Revert the v3.18.13 standalone `renderTranscriptsHtml` index at `GET /transcripts` — serve `gsd-t-transcript.html` (the same viewer used by `/transcript/:id`) with an empty/sentinel spawn-id placeholder so the viewer renders its left rail with all known spawns and no specific selection. The viewer's existing empty-state handling applies when the spawn list is empty.
21	
22	**Files owned**:
23	- `scripts/gsd-t-dashboard-server.js` (`handleTranscriptsList` content negotiation block — text/html branch)
24	- `test/transcripts-html-page.test.js` (refactor assertions for new contract)
25	- *new, optional*: `test/m45-d1-transcripts-route-viewer.test.js` (positive test that `/transcripts` serves the viewer with empty spawn-id)
26	
27	**Leaves alone** (no edits):
28	- `scripts/gsd-t-transcript.html` — D1 does not touch; the viewer's left-rail empty-state handling is already correct. D2 will edit this file to distinguish "💬 conversation" entries from "▶ spawn" entries.
29	- `bin/*` — no CLI changes.
30	
31	**Success criteria**:
32	- `GET /transcripts` with `Accept: text/html` returns the content of `gsd-t-transcript.html` with `__SPAWN_ID__` substituted to an empty string (or a sentinel like `null`) that the viewer's hash/left-rail logic handles without crashing.
33	- `GET /transcripts` with `Accept: application/json` or `*/*` continues to return `{ spawns: [...] }` (back-compat for dashboard JS).
34	- `test/transcripts-html-page.test.js` updated so HTML assertions target the viewer's stable DOM hooks (e.g., `data-spawn-id` attr, left-rail container id) instead of the retired standalone list.
35	
36	### D2 — m45-d2-in-session-conversation-capture
37	
38	**Responsibility**: Capture the orchestrator session's conversational turns into a transcript NDJSON that the viewer's left rail lists alongside spawn entries. Wire a SessionStart / UserPromptSubmit / Stop / PostToolUse hook. Extend the viewer left rail to distinguish in-session entries from spawn entries. Extend the compact-detector so mid-conversation compactions are written to the active in-session NDJSON when no spawn NDJSON is the most-recently-modified target.
39	
40	**Files owned**:
41	- `scripts/hooks/gsd-t-conversation-capture.js` *(new — hook script)*
42	- `scripts/gsd-t-compact-detector.js` *(additive — fallback target selection)*
43	- `scripts/gsd-t-transcript.html` *(additive — left-rail labeling, session-type badge)*
44	- `templates/CLAUDE-global.md` *(additive — settings.json hook block documentation)*
45	- `test/m45-d2-conversation-capture.test.js` *(new)*
46	- `test/m45-d2-compact-detector-in-session-fallback.test.js` *(new)*
47	- `test/m45-d2-transcript-left-rail-in-session.test.js` *(new)*
48	- `.gsd-t/contracts/conversation-capture-contract.md` *(new v1.0.0 — frame schema, file naming, hook entry points)*
49	
50	**Leaves alone** (no edits):
51	- `scripts/gsd-t-dashboard-server.js` — D1 owns. The route changes for D1 are self-contained; D2 does not need a server-side route change (the viewer polls `/api/spawns-index` which already lists NDJSONs on disk; D2's new `in-session-{sessionId}.ndjson` files will appear naturally if the viewer is taught to also list them — see D2-T3 for the index wiring path).
52	- `test/transcripts-html-page.test.js` — D1 owns.
53	
54	**Frame schema** (one-liner, full detail in `conversation-capture-contract.md`):
55	```json
56	{"type": "user_turn"|"assistant_turn"|"tool_use"|"compact_marker", "ts": "...", "session_id": "...", "content": "...", "message_id": "..."}
57	```
58	
59	**Success criteria**:
60	- A new orchestrator session appends `user_turn` + `assistant_turn` frames to `.gsd-t/transcripts/in-session-{sessionId}.ndjson` for every human↔Claude exchange.
61	- The viewer's left rail lists these files with the label `💬 conversation` (distinct from `▶ spawn`).
62	- A simulated `/compact` event during an in-session conversation lands `compact_marker` in the active in-session NDJSON, not a random spawn NDJSON.
63	- All 3 new test files pass; existing 1914 suite stays green.
64	
65	## Shared Files & Conflict Map
66

Extend the compact-detector so mid-conversation compactions land in the active in-session NDJSON (not a random spawn file). This makes mid-session compactions visible in the same UI surface as spawned work, satisfying the user's contract "conversation stream visible in visualizer" (memory: `feedback_conversation_stream_visible.md`).

1	<!-- GSD-T:START — Do not remove this marker. Content between START/END is managed by gsd-t update. -->
2	# Prime Directives
3	
4	1. SIMPLICITY ABOVE ALL. Every change should be minimal and impact as little code as possible. No massive refactors.
5	2. ALWAYS check for unwanted downstream effects before writing any new code or changing existing code.
6	3. ALWAYS check for completeness that any code creation/change/deletion is implemented thoroughly in every relevant file.
7	4. ALWAYS work autonomously. ONLY ask for user input when truly blocked.
8	
9	
10	# GSD-T: Contract-Driven Development
11	
12	## Work Hierarchy
13	
14	```
15	PROJECT or FEATURE or SCAN
16	  └── MILESTONE (major deliverable)
17	      └── PARTITION → DISCUSS → PLAN → IMPACT → EXECUTE → TEST-SYNC → INTEGRATE → VERIFY → COMPLETE
18	```
19	
20	- **Project**: Full greenfield project → decomposed into milestones
21	- **Feature**: Major new feature for existing codebase → impact analysis → milestones
22	- **Scan**: Deep codebase analysis → techdebt.md → promotable to milestones
23	- **Milestone**: A significant deliverable (e.g., "User Authentication Complete")
24	- **Domain**: An independent area of responsibility within a milestone, with its own scope, tasks, and file boundaries
25	- **Contract**: The documented interface between domains — API shapes, schemas, component props
26	
27	## Commands Reference
28	
29	See `/gsd-t-help` for the complete command list.
30	
31	
32	# Living Documents
33	
34	These documents MUST be maintained and referenced throughout development:
35	
36	| Document | Location | Purpose |
37	|----------|----------|---------|
38	| **Requirements** | `docs/requirements.md` | Functional and technical requirements |
39	| **Architecture** | `docs/architecture.md` | System design, components, data flow, decisions |
40	| **Workflows** | `docs/workflows.md` | User journeys and technical process flows |
41	| **Infrastructure** | `docs/infrastructure.md` | Commands, DB setup, server access, creds |
42	| **README** | `README.md` | Project overview, setup, features |
43	| **Progress** | `.gsd-t/progress.md` | Current milestone/phase state + version |
44	| **Contracts** | `.gsd-t/contracts/` | Interfaces between domains |
45	| **Tech Debt** | `.gsd-t/techdebt.md` | Debt register from scans |
46	
47	## The "No Re-Research" Rule
48	
49	**BEFORE researching how something works, CHECK THE DOCS FIRST.**
50	
51	```
52	NEED TO UNDERSTAND SOMETHING?
53	  ├── Is it about system structure/components? → Read docs/architecture.md
54	  ├── Is it about how a process flows? → Read docs/workflows.md
55	  ├── Is it about what to build? → Read docs/requirements.md
56	  ├── Is it about how to deploy/operate? → Read docs/infrastructure.md
57	  ├── Is it about domain interfaces? → Read .gsd-t/contracts/
58	  └── Not documented? → Research, then DOCUMENT IT
59	```
60	
61	
62	# Versioning
63	
64	GSD-T tracks project version in `.gsd-t/progress.md` using semantic versioning: `Major.Minor.Patch`
65	
66	| Segment | Bumped When | Example |
67	|---------|-------------|---------|
68	| **Major** | Breaking changes, major rework, v1 launch | 1.0.10 → 2.0.10 |
69	| **Minor** | New features, completed feature milestones | 1.10.10 → 1.11.10 |
70	| **Patch** | Bug fixes, minor improvements, cleanup | 1.1.10 → 1.1.11 |
71	
72	**Patch convention**: Patch numbers are always 2 digits (≥10). When resetting after a minor or major bump, start at **10** (not 0). This keeps patches always 2 characters without leading zeros, so semver stays valid.
73	
74	- Version is set during `gsd-t-init`:
75	  - **New project** (no existing manifest version): starts at `0.1.00` — first `complete-milestone` resets patch to `0.1.10`
76	  - **Existing repo** (has `package.json`, `pyproject.toml`, `Cargo.toml`, etc. with a version): use that version as the starting point
77	- Version is bumped during `gsd-t-complete-milestone` based on milestone scope
78	- Version is reflected in: `progress.md`, `README.md`, package manifest (if any), and git tags (`v{version}`)
79	
80	
81	# Destructive Action Guard (MANDATORY)
82	
83	**NEVER perform destructive or structural changes without explicit user approval.** This applies at ALL autonomy levels, including Level 3.
84	
85	```
86	BEFORE any of these actions, STOP and ask the user:
87	  ├── DROP TABLE, DROP COLUMN, DROP INDEX, TRUNCATE, DELETE without WHERE
88	  ├── Renaming or removing database tables or columns
89	  ├── Schema migrations that lose data or break existing queries
90	  ├── Replacing an existing architecture pattern (e.g., normalized → denormalized)
91	  ├── Removing or replacing existing files/modules that contain working functionality
92	  ├── Changing ORM models in ways that conflict with the existing database schema
93	  ├── Removing API endpoints or changing response shapes that existing clients depend on
94	  ├── Replacing a dependency or framework with a different one
95	  └── Any change that would require other parts of the system to be rewritten
96	```
97	
98	### How to handle schema/architecture mismatches:
99	1. **READ the existing schema/code first** — understand what exists before proposing changes
100	2. **Adapt new code to match existing structures** — not the other way around
101	3. **If restructuring is truly needed**, present the case to the user with:
102	   - What exists today and why it might have been designed that way
103	   - What you want to change and why
104	   - What will break if you make the change
105	   - What data or functionality will be lost
106	   - A migration path that preserves existing data
107	4. **Wait for explicit approval** before proceeding
108	
109	### Why this matters:
110	Even in development, the user may have:
111	- Working functionality they've tested and rely on
112	- Data they've carefully set up (seed data, test accounts, configuration)
113	- Other code that depends on the current structure
114	- Design decisions made for reasons not documented
115	
116	**"Adapt to what exists" is always safer than "replace what exists."**
117	
118	
119	# Autonomous Execution Rules
120	
121	## Update Notices
122	
123	On session start, a version check hook auto-updates GSD-T and outputs a status message. Show the result to the user at the **beginning** of your first response:
124	
125	- If `[GSD-T AUTO-UPDATE]` appears → GSD-T was just auto-updated. Show:
126	  ```
127	  ✅ GSD-T auto-updated: v{old} → v{new}
128	     Changelog: https://github.com/Tekyz-Inc/get-stuff-done-teams/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md
129	  ```
130	
131	- If `[GSD-T UPDATE]` appears → update available but auto-update failed. Show:
132	  ```
133	  ⬆️  GSD-T update available: v{installed} → v{latest} (auto-update failed)
134	     Run: /gsd-t-version-update-all
135	     Changelog: https://github.com/Tekyz-Inc/get-stuff-done-teams/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md
136	  ```
137	  Also repeat at the **end** of your first response.
138	
139	- If `[GSD-T]` appears → up to date. Show:
140	  ```
141	  GSD-T v{version} — up to date
142	  Changelog: https://github.com/Tekyz-Inc/get-stuff-done-teams/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md
143	  ```
144	
145	## Conversation vs. Work
146	
147	Only execute GSD-T workflow behavior when a `/gsd-t-*` command is invoked or when actively mid-phase (resumed via `/gsd-t-resume`). **Plain text messages — especially questions — should be answered conversationally.** Do not launch into workflow execution, file reading, or phase advancement from a question or comment. If the user wants work done, they will invoke a command.
148	
149	**Exception — Auto-Route signal**: When `[GSD-T AUTO-ROUTE]` appears in your context (injected by the UserPromptSubmit hook), the user's plain text message should be treated as a `/gsd {message}` invocation. Execute the `/gsd` smart router with the user's full message as the argument instead of replying conversationally. The hook only fires in GSD-T projects (directories containing `.gsd-t/progress.md`) — it silently passes through in all other directories.
150	
151	## Auto-Init Guard
152	
153	Before executing any GSD-T workflow command, check if **any** of these files are missing in the current project:
154	- `.gsd-t/progress.md`, `.gsd-t/backlog.md`, `.gsd-t/backlog-settings.md`
155	- `.gsd-t/contracts/`, `.gsd-t/domains/`
156	- `CLAUDE.md`, `README.md`
157	- `docs/requirements.md`, `docs/architecture.md`, `docs/workflows.md`, `docs/infrastructure.md`
158	
159	If any are missing:
160	1. Run `gsd-t-init` automatically (it skips files that already exist)
161	2. Then continue with the originally requested command
162	
163	**Exempt commands** (do not trigger auto-init): `gsd-t-init`, `gsd-t-init-scan-setup`, `gsd-t-help`, `gsd-t-version-update`, `gsd-t-version-update-all`.
164	
165	## Playwright Readiness Guard
166	
167	Before any command that involves testing (`gsd-t-execute`, `gsd-t-test-sync`, `gsd-t-verify`, `gsd-t-quick`, `gsd-t-wave`, `gsd-t-milestone`, `gsd-t-complete-milestone`, `gsd-t-debug`), check if `playwright.config.*` exists in the project. If it does not:
168	1. Detect the package manager and install Playwright (`@playwright/test` + chromium)
169	2. Create a basic `playwright.config.ts` with sensible defaults
170	3. Create the E2E test directory with a placeholder spec
171	4. Then continue with the original command
172	
173	Playwright must always be ready before any testing occurs. Do not skip this check. Do not defer setup to "later."
174	
175	### Playwright Cleanup
176	
177	After Playwright tests finish (pass or fail), **kill any app/server processes that were started for the tests**. Playwright often launches a dev server (via `webServer` config or manually). These processes must not be left running:
178	1. Check for any dev server processes spawned during the test run
179	2. Kill them (e.g., `npx kill-port`, or terminate the process directly)
180	3. Verify the port is free before proceeding
181	
182	This applies everywhere Playwright tests are executed: execute, test-sync, verify, quick, wave, debug, complete-milestone, and integrate.
183	
184	### E2E Enforcement Rule (MANDATORY)
185	
186	**Running only unit tests when E2E tests exist is a test failure.** This is non-negotiable.
187	
188	```
189	BEFORE reporting "tests pass" for ANY task:
190	  ├── Does playwright.config.* or cypress.config.* exist?
191	  │     YES → You MUST run the full E2E suite. Unit-only results are INCOMPLETE.
192	  │     NO  → Unit/integration tests are sufficient.
193	  ├── Did you run every detected test runner?
194	  │     NO → Run it now. Do not commit until ALL suites pass.
195	  └── Report format MUST include all suites:
196	        "Unit: X/Y pass | E2E: X/Y pass" (or "E2E: N/A — no config")
197	```
198	
199	The conditional "if UI/routes/flows changed" in command files applies to **writing new E2E specs**, not to **running existing ones**. You always run existing E2E specs. Always.
200	
201	### E2E Test Quality Standard (MANDATORY)
202	
203	**E2E tests must be FUNCTIONAL tests, not LAYOUT tests.** This is non-negotiable.
204	
205	A layout test checks that elements exist (`isVisible`, `toBeAttached`, `toBeEnabled`, `toHaveCount`). A functional test checks that features work — actions produce correct outcomes.
206	
207	```
208	LAYOUT TEST (WRONG — passes even if every feature is broken):
209	  await expect(page.locator('#tab-sessions')).toBeVisible();
210	  await page.click('#tab-sessions');
211	  // ← No assertion that the tab's content actually loaded
212	
213	FUNCTIONAL TEST (RIGHT — fails if the feature is broken):
214	  await page.click('#tab-sessions');
215	  await expect(page.locator('.session-list')).toContainText('Session 1');
216	  // ← Proves clicking the tab loaded the session data
217	```
218	
219	Every Playwright assertion must verify one of:
220	- **State changed**: After click/type/submit, the app state is different (new content, updated data, changed status)
221	- **Data flowed**: User input → API call → response rendered (use `page.waitForResponse` or assert on rendered data)
222	- **Content loaded**: Navigation/tab switch → destination content appeared (assert on text/data unique to destination)
223	- **Widget responded**: Terminal accepted keystrokes and produced output, editor saved changes, form submitted and data persisted
224	
225	**If a test would pass on an empty HTML page with the correct element IDs and no JavaScript, it is not a functional test.** Rewrite it.
226	
227	## QA Agent (Mandatory)
228	
229	Every code-producing/validating phase MUST run QA. QA writes ZERO feature code — it generates, runs, and gap-reports tests. Failure (or any shallow E2E test) blocks phase completion.
230	Protocol: `templates/prompts/qa-subagent.md`. Contract: `.gsd-t/contracts/qa-agent-contract.md`.
231	
232	## Design Verification Agent (Mandatory when design contract exists)
233	
234	When `.gsd-t/contracts/design-contract.md` or `.gsd-t/contracts/design/` exists, a dedicated agent opens a browser, compares the build against the design, and writes a structured element-by-element MATCH/DEVIATION table. Writes ZERO feature code. Deviations (or missing verification artifact) block phase completion.
235	Protocol: `templates/prompts/design-verify-subagent.md`.
236	
237	## Red Team — Adversarial QA (Mandatory)
238	
239	After QA + Design Verification pass, every code-producing command spawns an adversarial subagent whose success is measured by bugs found, not tests passed. VERDICT is `FAIL` (bugs — blocks completion) or `GRUDGING PASS` (exhaustive search, nothing found). CRITICAL/HIGH bugs get up to 2 fix cycles before deferral.
240	Protocol: `templates/prompts/red-team-subagent.md`.
241	
242	## Model Display (MANDATORY)
243	
244	**Before every subagent spawn, display the model being used to the user:**
245	`⚙ [{model}] {command} → {brief description}` (e.g., `⚙ [sonnet] gsd-t-execute → domain: auth-service`, `⚙ [haiku] gsd-t-execute → QA validation`)
246	
247	This gives the user real-time visibility into which model is handling each operation.
248	
249	**Model assignments:**
250	- `model: haiku` — strictly mechanical tasks: run test suites and report counts, check file existence, validate JSON structure, branch guard checks
251	- `model: sonnet` — mid-tier reasoning: routine code changes, standard refactors, test writing, QA evaluation, straightforward synthesis
252	- `model: opus` — high-stakes reasoning: architecture decisions, security analysis, complex debugging, cross-module refactors, Red Team adversarial QA, quality judgment on critical paths
253	
254	**Context Meter (M34/M38, v3.12.10+)** — The real context-window measurement feeding the headless-default spawn decision. A PostToolUse hook (`scripts/gsd-t-context-meter.js`) runs after every tool call, uses local token estimation to write the current input-token count into `.gsd-t/.context-meter-state.json`. `getSessionStatus()` reads that state file (fresh window = 5 minutes) with a historical heuristic fallback when the file is missing or stale. Command files consume the signal via a small bash shim (`CTX_PCT=$(node -e "…tb.getSessionStatus('.').pct")`). **Single-band model** (context-meter-contract v1.3.0): there's one threshold (default 85%) and one action — hand off to a detached headless spawn. No three-band routing, no silent downgrades, no MANDATORY STOP prose. The meter exists to inform spawn-time routing, not to pause work in-flight.
255	
256	## Observability Logging (MANDATORY)
257	
258	Every command that spawns a Task subagent, invokes `claude -p`, or calls `spawn('claude', ...)` MUST route the spawn through `bin/gsd-t-token-capture.cjs` so the real token-usage envelope is parsed and recorded. This is the M41 canonical pattern — the pre-M41 bash block that wrote `| N/A |` is retired.
259	
260	### Pattern A — wrap a spawn callable with `captureSpawn`
261	
262	Preferred for new spawn sites. The wrapper owns the before/after timing, model banner, envelope parse, row write, and JSONL record.
263	
264	```
265	node -e "
266	const { captureSpawn } = require('./bin/gsd-t-token-capture.cjs');
267	(async () => {
268	  await captureSpawn({
269	    command: 'gsd-t-execute',
270	    step: 'Step 4',
271	    model: 'sonnet',
272	    description: 'domain: auth-service',
273	    projectDir: '.',
274	    domain: 'auth-service',
275	    task: 'T-3',
276	    spawnFn: async () => { /* actual Task(...) or spawn('claude', ...) call */ },
277	  });
278	})();
279	"
280	```
281	
282	### Pattern B — record after the result envelope is already in hand
283	
284	For command files where the Task subagent already ran and the caller has the result object. Identical row format, no timing wrap.
285	
286	```
287	node -e "
288	const { recordSpawnRow } = require('./bin/gsd-t-token-capture.cjs');
289	recordSpawnRow({
290	  projectDir: '.',
291	  command: 'gsd-t-verify',
292	  step: 'Step 4',
293	  model: 'haiku',
294	  startedAt: '2026-04-21 10:00',
295	  endedAt:   '2026-04-21 10:02',
296	  usage: result.usage, // may be undefined — wrapper handles with '—'
297	  domain: '-', task: '-',
298	  ctxPct: 42,
299	  notes: 'test audit + contract review',
300	});
301	"
302	```
303	
304	### Canonical `.gsd-t/token-log.md` header
305	
306	```
307	| Datetime-start | Datetime-end | Command | Step | Model | Duration(s) | Tokens | Notes | Domain | Task | Ctx% |
308	```
309	
310	The wrapper detects old headers (no `Tokens` column) and upgrades in place, preserving existing rows. The **Tokens** cell renders as `in=N out=N cr=N cc=N $X.XX` when usage is present, or `—` when absent. Never `0`. Never `N/A`. A zero is a measurement; a dash is an acknowledged gap.
311	
312	For QA/validation subagents, append findings to `.gsd-t/qa-issues.md`:
313	```
314	| Date | Command | Step | Model | Duration(s) | Severity | Finding |
315	```
316	
317	## Token Capture Rule (MANDATORY)
318	
319	Every `Task(...)` subagent spawn, every `claude -p` child process, and every `spawn('claude', ...)` call MUST flow through `bin/gsd-t-token-capture.cjs`. Either wrap with `captureSpawn({..., spawnFn})` or record explicitly with `recordSpawnRow({...})` after the call returns.
320	
321	No command file ships a bare `Task(...)` or `claude -p` line outside of a wrapper call. `gsd-t capture-lint` (D5) enforces this mechanically; violations fail the opt-in pre-commit hook.
322	
323	Rationale: the pre-M41 convention silently wrote `N/A` tokens because no caller parsed the `usage` envelope. The wrapper is the single place that parses it. Bypassing the wrapper re-introduces blind spots.
324	
325	## Always-Headless Spawn (M43 D4, v3.16.x+) — Channel Separation
326	
327	Every GSD-T command spawns detached, unconditionally. There is no `--watch`, no `--in-session`, no `--headless` opt-in, no context-meter threshold that reroutes, no low-water-mark bypass. The dialog channel is reserved for human↔Claude conversation; everything else is a detached headless child. Interactive session shows a launch banner + live-transcript URL + event-stream path, then exits. Results surface via the read-back banner on the user's next message.
328	
329	The only in-session surface is the `/gsd` router (`commands/gsd.md`), and only for dialog-only exploratory turns. The moment Step 2.5 classifies a turn as `workflow`, the router hands off to a detached spawn.
330	
331	Legacy `watch` / `inSession` params are accepted-and-ignored with a one-shot stderr deprecation warning (scheduled removal in v3.0.0 of the contract). `shouldSpawnHeadless` is a constant `() => true`.
332	
333	Contract: `.gsd-t/contracts/headless-default-contract.md` v2.0.0 (see also `unattended-event-stream-contract.md`, `unattended-supervisor-contract.md`).
334	
335	## API Documentation Guard (Swagger/OpenAPI)
336	
337	**Every API endpoint MUST be documented in a Swagger/OpenAPI spec. No exceptions.**
338	
339	When any GSD-T command creates or modifies an API endpoint:
340	1. **If no Swagger/OpenAPI spec exists**: Set one up immediately
341	   - Detect the framework (Express, Fastify, Hono, Django, FastAPI, etc.)
342	   - Install the appropriate Swagger integration (e.g., `swagger-jsdoc` + `swagger-ui-express`, `@fastify/swagger`, FastAPI's built-in OpenAPI)
343	   - Create the OpenAPI spec file or configure auto-generation from code
344	   - Add a `/docs` or `/api-docs` route serving the Swagger UI
345	2. **Update the spec**: Every new or changed endpoint must be reflected in the Swagger/OpenAPI spec — routes, request/response schemas, auth requirements, error responses
346	3. **Publish the Swagger URL**: The Swagger/API docs URL MUST appear in:
347	   - `CLAUDE.md` — under Documentation or Infrastructure section
348	   - `README.md` — under API section or Getting Started
349	   - `docs/infrastructure.md` — under API documentation
350	4. **Verify**: After any API change, confirm the Swagger UI loads and reflects the current endpoints
351	
352	This applies during: `gsd-t-execute`, `gsd-t-quick`, `gsd-t-integrate`, `gsd-t-wave`, and any command that touches API code.
353	
354	## Prime Rule
355	KEEP GOING. Only stop for:
356	1. Unrecoverable errors after 2 fix attempts (delegate to `gsd-t headless --debug-loop` first — only stop if exit code 4)
357	2. Ambiguity that fundamentally changes project direction
358	3. Milestone completion (checkpoint for user review)
359	4. Destructive actions (see Destructive Action Guard above — ALWAYS stop)
360	
361	## Pre-Commit Gate (MANDATORY)
362	
363	NEVER commit code without running this checklist. This is not optional.
364	
365	```
366	BEFORE EVERY COMMIT:
367	  ├── Am I on the correct branch?
368	  │     CHECK → Run `git branch --show-current`
369	  │     Compare against "Expected branch" in project CLAUDE.md
370	  │     WRONG BRANCH → STOP. Do NOT commit. Switch to the correct branch first.
371	  │     No guard set → Proceed (but warn user to set one)
372	  ├── Did I create or change an API endpoint or response shape?
373	  │     YES → Update .gsd-t/contracts/api-contract.md
374	  │     YES → Update Swagger/OpenAPI spec (see API Documentation Guard below)
375	  │     YES → Verify Swagger URL is in CLAUDE.md and README.md
376	  ├── Did I change the database schema?
377	  │     YES → Update .gsd-t/contracts/schema-contract.md AND docs/schema.md
378	  ├── Did I add/change a UI component interface?
379	  │     YES → Update .gsd-t/contracts/component-contract.md
380	  ├── Did I add new files or directories?
381	  │     YES → Update the owning domain's scope.md
382	  ├── Did I implement or change a requirement?
383	  │     YES → Update docs/requirements.md (mark complete or revise)
384	  ├── Did I add/change/remove a component or change data flow?
385	  │     YES → Update docs/architecture.md
386	  ├── Did I modify any document, script, or code file?
387	  │     YES → Add timestamped entry to .gsd-t/progress.md Decision Log
388	  │     Format: `- YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM: {what was done} — {brief context or result}`
389	  │     This includes ALL file-modifying activities:
390	  │       project, feature, scan, gap-analysis, milestone, partition, discuss,
391	  │       plan, impact, execute, test-sync, integrate, verify, complete-milestone,
392	  │       wave, quick, debug, promote-debt, populate, setup, init, init-scan-setup,
393	  │       backlog-add/edit/move/remove/promote/settings, and any manual code changes
394	  ├── Did I make an architectural or design decision?
395	  │     YES → Also include decision rationale in the progress.md entry
396	  ├── Did I discover or fix tech debt?
397	  │     YES → Update .gsd-t/techdebt.md
398	  ├── Did I establish a pattern future work should follow?
399	  │     YES → Update CLAUDE.md or domain constraints.md
400	  ├── Did I add/change tests?
401	  │     YES → Verify test names and paths are referenced in requirements
402	  ├── Did I change UI, routes, or user flows?
403	  │     YES → Update affected E2E test specs (Playwright/Cypress)
404	  └── Did I run the affected tests?
405	        YES → Verify they pass. NO → Run them now.
406	```
407	
408	If ANY answer is YES and the doc is NOT updated, update it BEFORE committing. No exceptions.
409	
410	## Document Ripple Completion Gate (MANDATORY)
411	
412	**NEVER report a task as "done" or present a summary until ALL downstream documents are updated.** This is not optional.
413	
414	When a change affects multiple files (e.g., a new standard that applies across command files, a renamed API, a new convention), you MUST:
415	
416	1. **Identify the full blast radius BEFORE starting**: List every file that needs the change
417	2. **Complete ALL updates in one pass**: Do not update 3 of 8 files and then present a summary
418	3. **Run the Pre-Commit Gate on the COMPLETE changeset**: Not on a partial subset
419	4. **Only THEN report completion**
420	
421	```
422	BEFORE reporting "done" or presenting a summary:
423	  ├── Did this change establish a new standard, rule, or convention?
424	  │     YES → Grep for every file that should enforce it. Update ALL of them.
425	  ├── Did this change modify a pattern used in multiple command files?
426	  │     YES → Find and update EVERY command file that uses that pattern.
427	  ├── Did this change affect a template (CLAUDE-global, CLAUDE-project, etc.)?
428	  │     YES → The template AND the live equivalent (~/.claude/CLAUDE.md) must match.
429	  ├── Did this change add a new requirement?
430	  │     YES → Add to docs/requirements.md in the same pass.
431	  ├── Have I checked EVERY file in the blast radius?
432	  │     NO → Keep going. Do not present partial work.
433	  └── Am I about to say "want me to also update X?" or "should I check Y?"
434	        YES → STOP. Just update X and check Y. Then report done.
435	```
436	
437	**The test for this gate**: If the user asks "did you update all the documents?" and the answer would be "no, I missed some" — you failed this gate. The user should never need to ask.
438	
439	## Execution Behavior
440	- ALWAYS check docs/architecture.md before adding or modifying components.
441	- ALWAYS check docs/workflows.md before changing any multi-step process.
442	- ALWAYS update docs as part of completing work — not as an afterthought.
443	- ALWAYS self-verify work by running tests and verification commands.
444	- NEVER re-research how something works if you built it — it should be documented.
445	- NEVER pause to show verification steps — execute them.
446	- NEVER ask "should I continue?" — just continue.
447	- NEVER summarize what you're "about to do" — just do it.
448	- IF a test fails, fix it immediately (up to 2 attempts) before reporting. If both attempts fail, delegate to `gsd-t headless --debug-loop` before stopping.
449	
450	## Autonomy Levels
451	
452	Projects can specify an autonomy level in their project CLAUDE.md:
453	
454	| Level | Behavior |
455	|-------|----------|
456	| **Level 1: Supervised** | Pause at each phase for confirmation |
457	| **Level 2: Standard** | Pause only at milestones |
458	| **Level 3: Full Auto** | Only pause for blockers or project completion (default) |
459	
460	If not specified, use Level 3.
461	
462	## Workflow Preferences (Defaults — override in project CLAUDE.md)
463	
464	### Research Policy
465	Before planning a phase, evaluate whether research is needed:
466	
467	**Run research when:**
468	- Phase involves unfamiliar libraries, APIs, or services
469	- Architectural decisions are required
470	- Integrating external systems
471	- Phase scope is ambiguous or complex
472	
473	**Skip research when:**
474	- Patterns are already established from earlier phases
475	- Straightforward CRUD, UI, or config work
476	- Domain is well understood
477	- Phase builds directly on existing code patterns
478	
479	If in doubt, skip research and proceed — research if execution reveals gaps.
480	
481	### Phase Flow
482	- Upon completing a phase, automatically proceed to the next phase
483	- ONLY run Discussion phase if truly required (clear path → skip to Plan)
484	- ALWAYS self-verify work by running verification commands
485	- NEVER pause to show verification steps — execute them
486	
487	### Next Command Hint
488	
489	When a GSD-T command completes (and does NOT auto-advance to the next phase), display a "Next Up" block at the very end of your response. This format is designed to trigger Claude Code's prompt suggestion engine — making the next command appear as ghost text in the user's input field.
490	
491	**MANDATORY format** — use this exact structure:
492	
493	```
494	───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
495	
496	## ▶ Next Up
497	
498	**{Phase Name}** — {one-line description of what happens next}
499	
500	`/gsd-t-{command}`
501	
502	───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
503	```
504	
505	If there are alternative commands that also make sense, add them:
506	
507	```
508	**Also available:**
509	- `/gsd-t-{alt-1}` — {description}
510	- `/gsd-t-{alt-2}` — {description}
511	```
512	
513	Successor mapping:
514	| Completed | Next | Also available |
515	|-----------|------|----------------|
516	| `project` | `milestone` | |
517	| `feature` | `milestone` | |
518	| `milestone` | `partition` | |
519	| `partition` | `plan` | `discuss` (if complex) |
520	| `discuss` | `plan` | |
521	| `plan` | `execute` | `impact` (if risky) |
522	| `impact` | `execute` | |
523	| `execute` | `test-sync` | |
524	| `test-sync` | `verify` | `integrate` (if multi-domain) |
525	| `integrate` | `verify` | |
526	| `verify` | *(auto-invokes complete-milestone)* | |
527	| `complete-milestone` | `status` | |
528	| `scan` | `promote-debt` | `milestone` |
529	| `init` | `scan` | `milestone` |
530	| `init-scan-setup` | `milestone` | |
531	| `gap-analysis` | `milestone` | `feature` |
532	| `populate` | `status` | |
533	| `setup` | `status` | |
534	| `design-decompose` | `design-build` | `partition` (if domains needed first) |
535	
536	Commands with no successor (standalone): `quick`, `debug`, `brainstorm`, `status`, `help`, `resume`, `prompt`, `log`, `health`, `pause`, backlog commands.
537	
538	Skip the hint if auto-advancing (Level 3 mid-wave) — only show when the user needs to manually invoke the next step.
539	
540	
541	# Don't Do These Things
542	
543	- NEVER perform destructive or structural changes without explicit user approval (see Destructive Action Guard above).
544	- NEVER drop database tables, remove columns, or run destructive SQL on an existing database — adapt new code to the existing schema.
545	- NEVER replace existing architecture patterns (e.g., normalized → denormalized) without user approval — even if you think the new way is better.
546	- NEVER commit code without running the Pre-Commit Gate checklist. EVERY commit.
547	- NEVER batch doc updates for later — update docs as part of the same commit as the code change.
548	- NEVER start a phase without reading contracts and relevant docs first.
549	- NEVER complete a phase without running document ripple on affected docs.
550	- NEVER re-research how a component works — read architecture.md and contracts.
551	- NEVER let code and contract disagree — fix one or the other immediately.
552	- NEVER make changes that touch more than 3 files without pausing to confirm approach.
553	
554	
555	# Code Standards (Defaults — override in project CLAUDE.md)
556	
557	## Patterns
558	- Type hints required on all function signatures
559	- Dataclasses/interfaces for data models, not raw dicts
560	- Functions under 30 lines — split if longer
561	- Files under 200 lines — create new modules if needed
562	- Enums for state management and fixed option sets
563	
564	## Naming
565	```
566	files:      snake_case        (user_service.py)
567	classes:    PascalCase        (UserService)
568	functions:  snake_case        (get_user)
569	constants:  UPPER_SNAKE_CASE  (MAX_RETRIES)
570	private:    _underscore       (_internal_method)
571	```
572	
573	## Markdown Tables
574	
575	Markdown table emoji-padding rules live in `templates/stacks/_markdown.md` (auto-injected via Stack Rules Engine).
576	
577	
578	## Stack Rules Engine
579	
580	GSD-T auto-detects project tech stack at subagent spawn time and injects mandatory best-practice rules into the subagent prompt.
581	
582	**Detection sources**: `package.json` (React, TypeScript, Node API), `requirements.txt`/`pyproject.toml` (Python), `go.mod` (Go), `Cargo.toml` (Rust).
583	
584	**Universal rules**: Templates prefixed with `_` (e.g., `_security.md`) are **always** injected, regardless of stack.
585	
586	**Stack-specific rules**: Injected only when the matching stack is detected (e.g., `react.md` when `"react"` is in `package.json`).
587	
588	**Design-to-code**: Activated when `.gsd-t/contracts/design-contract.md` (flat), `.gsd-t/contracts/design/` (hierarchical element/widget/page contracts — bootstrap via `/gsd-t-design-decompose`), `design-tokens.json`, `design-tokens/`, `.figmarc`, or `figma.config.json` exists, OR when Figma MCP is configured in `~/.claude/settings.json`. Auto-bootstrapped during partition when Figma URLs or design references are detected in requirements. Enforces pixel-perfect frontend implementation from designs with: Figma MCP auto-detection, design token extraction protocol, stack capability evaluation (recommends alternatives if stack can't achieve the design), component decomposition, responsive breakpoint strategy, and a mandatory visual verification loop — every implemented screen must be rendered in a real browser, screenshotted at mobile/tablet/desktop breakpoints, and compared pixel-by-pixel against the Figma design. Visual deviations block task completion.
589	
590	**Enforcement**: Stack rule violations have the same weight as contract violations — they are task failures, not warnings.
591	
592	**Extensible**: Drop a `.md` file into `templates/stacks/` in the GSD-T package to add rules for a new stack. If the directory is missing, detection skips silently.
593	
594	**Commands that inject stack rules**: `gsd-t-execute`, `gsd-t-quick`, `gsd-t-integrate`, `gsd-t-wave`, `gsd-t-debug`.
595	
596	
597	# Recovery After Interruption
598	
599	When resuming work (new session or after /clear):
600	1. Read `.gsd-t/progress.md` for current state
601	2. Read `docs/requirements.md` for what's left to build
602	3. Read `docs/architecture.md` for how the system is structured
603	4. Read `.gsd-t/contracts/` for domain interfaces
604	5. Verify last task's work is intact (files exist, tests pass)
605	6. Continue from current task — don't restart the phase
606	
607	**CRITICAL: Do NOT research how the system works. The docs tell you. Read them.**
608	<!-- GSD-T:END — Do not remove this marker. -->

1	# Compaction Events Contract
2	
3	**Status**: v1.1.0 — M44 D7 (calibration event added 2026-04-22)
4	**Sink**: `.gsd-t/metrics/compactions.jsonl`
5	**Producers**:
6	- Live: `scripts/gsd-t-compact-detector.js` (SessionStart hook, `source=compact`) — emits the `compact` row defined in v1.0.0.
7	- Live: `scripts/gsd-t-calibration-hook.js` (SessionStart hook, `source=compact`) — emits the `compaction_post_spawn` calibration event defined in v1.1.0. Runs alongside the detector; both are independent listeners on the same hook payload.
8	- Backfill: `scripts/gsd-t-compaction-scanner.js` (historical scan of `~/.claude/projects/<slug>/*.jsonl`, `source=compact-backfill`) — emits the `compact-backfill` row defined in v1.0.0.
9	
10	## Versions
11	
12	- **v1.1.0** (2026-04-22, M44 D7) — adds `compaction_post_spawn` calibration event type appended to the same sink. Pairs `estimatedCwPct` (D6's pre-spawn prediction) with `actualCwPct` (derived from the live compaction event) so D6's estimator can self-calibrate. Backward-compatible: every v1.0.0 row is a valid v1.1.0 row; consumers MUST treat `type` as optional and default missing values to `"compact"`.
13	- **v1.0.0** (2026-04-22, M44 pre-req) — initial schema for the `compact` and `compact-backfill` rows.
14	
15	## Why this exists
16	
17	The canonical token-measurement hierarchy is:
18	
19	```
20	Run → Iter → Context Window → Turn → Tool call
21	```
22	
23	A **Context Window** (CW) is bounded by compactions. Without compaction
24	events recorded, every iter looks like a single CW regardless of how many
25	times Claude Code actually auto-compacted mid-run — so per-CW accounting,
26	drift analysis, and the M44 parallel orchestrator's CW-scoped attribution
27	all quietly decay to iter-level. Neither the transcript NDJSON nor
28	`token-usage.jsonl` surfaces these transitions today.
29	
30	Claude Code **already** fires a `SessionStart` hook after an auto-compaction
31	with `source: "compact"` (vs `"startup"` or `"resume"`). Nothing consumed
32	the signal until now.
33	
34	## Row schema (v1)
35	
36	Each row is one NDJSON line in `compactions.jsonl`:
37	
38	```json
39	{
40	  "ts": "2026-04-22T12:34:56.000Z",
41	  "schemaVersion": 1,
42	  "session_id": "new-abc",
43	  "prior_session_id": "old-xyz",
44	  "source": "compact",
45	  "cwd": "/Users/david/projects/GSD-T",
46	  "hook": "SessionStart"
47	}
48	```
49	
50	| Field | Type | Notes |
51	|-------|------|-------|
52	| `ts` | ISO-8601 string | When the row was written (detector wall-clock; for backfill, the compact_boundary's own `timestamp`). |
53	| `schemaVersion` | integer | `1`. Bump on breaking change. |
54	| `session_id` | string \| null | New session id after compaction. |
55	| `prior_session_id` | string \| null | Previous session id if Claude Code supplies one (`prior_session_id` or `previous_session_id` accepted). Often null for in-place compactions. |
56	| `source` | string | `"compact"` (live hook) or `"compact-backfill"` (scanner). Never anything else. |
57	| `cwd` | string | Absolute working directory at the time of the event. |
58	| `hook` | string | Always `"SessionStart"` (for provenance; backfill rows carry the same tag so joiners treat them uniformly). |
59	
60	Backfill may also include two optional fields when they can be recovered
61	from the historical `compact_boundary` record:
62	
63	| Field | Type | Notes |
64	|-------|------|-------|
65	| `preTokens` | integer | Input tokens just before compaction. |
66	| `postTokens` | integer | Input tokens just after compaction. |
67	| `trigger` | string | `"auto"` or `"manual"` from `compactMetadata.trigger`. |
68	| `durationMs` | integer | `compactMetadata.durationMs`. |
69	
70	Consumers MUST treat unknown fields as ignorable. Consumers MUST NOT fail
71	when optional fields are absent.
72	
73	## Hook lifecycle
74	
75	1. Claude Code fires `SessionStart` with a JSON payload on stdin.
76	2. The hook reads stdin (1 MiB cap, UTF-8).
77	3. If `source !== "compact"` → no-op, exit 0.
78	4. If `<cwd>/.gsd-t/` does not exist → no-op, exit 0 (off-switch).
79	5. Append one row to `<cwd>/.gsd-t/metrics/compactions.jsonl`.
80	6. **Exit 0, always.** Throwing or non-zero exit breaks Claude Code.
81	
82	### Guardrails
83	
84	- **Fail-open**: any exception is swallowed. No row is better than a broken
85	  session start.
86	- **1 MiB stdin cap**: abort (no write) when stdin exceeds cap.
87	- **Path-traversal guard**: resolved output path MUST stay under
88	  `<cwd>/.gsd-t/metrics/`. Otherwise no-op.
89	- **Strict cwd**: when `payload.cwd` is present but not an absolute path (or
90	  is a non-string), the hook silently no-ops rather than falling back to
91	  `process.cwd()`. Only a wholly-missing `cwd` defaults to `process.cwd()`.
92	- **Off-switch**: deleting `.gsd-t/` in a project disables recording in that
93	  project. Deleting the hook matcher from `~/.claude/settings.json` disables
94	  it globally.
95	
96	## Backfill semantics
97	
98	`scripts/gsd-t-compaction-scanner.js` scans
99	`~/.claude/projects/<cwd-slug>/*.jsonl` session transcripts. Each session
100	JSONL contains a sequence of records; compaction boundaries are emitted by
101	Claude Code itself as:
102	
103	```json
104	{ "type": "system", "subtype": "compact_boundary",
105	  "timestamp": "…", "sessionId": "…", "cwd": "…",
106	  "compactMetadata": { "trigger": "auto", "preTokens": …,
107	                       "postTokens": …, "durationMs": … } }
108	```
109	
110	The scanner walks every file, finds these rows, deduplicates against the
111	existing `compactions.jsonl` by `(ts, session_id)`, and — only when
112	`--write` is passed — appends new rows with `source: "compact-backfill"`.
113	
114	Default is dry-run: summary counts + sample rows to stdout, no mutation.
115	
116	**Empirical note (2026-04-22 initial run)**: 72 historical compactions
117	found across 112 GSD-T session files spanning Apr 4–21. Older Claude Code
118	archives do not consistently include `postTokens`; backfilled rows omit
119	the field rather than guessing. Consumers MUST handle missing optional
120	fields without error.
121	
122	**Prior-session-id on backfill**: live detector rows carry Claude Code's
123	own `prior_session_id` (when present). Backfill rows map the session
124	JSONL's `logicalParentUuid` into this field — it's the UUID of the last
125	message in the pre-compact window, NOT a true Claude Code session id, but
126	it's the closest stable boundary anchor the archive exposes for in-place
127	compactions. Consumers treating this field as "anything that uniquely
128	identifies the boundary" are correct; consumers assuming it's always a
129	session id are not.
130	
131	## Wiring
132	
133	`~/.claude/settings.json` → `hooks.SessionStart[]` includes **all three**:
134	
135	1. The existing version-check hook (`gsd-t-update-check.js`). **Never remove.**
136	2. The compact detector (`gsd-t-compact-detector.js`). Added by v1.0.0.
137	3. The calibration hook (`gsd-t-calibration-hook.js`). Added by v1.1.0 (M44 D7).
138	
139	Matchers are separate entries so any one can be disabled without touching
140	the others. The calibration hook is independent of the detector — both
141	listen for `source=compact` payloads on stdin and write to the same sink,
142	but neither reads or writes the other's rows.
143	
144	## Calibration event row schema (v1.1.0)
145	
146	The calibration hook appends one NDJSON line per recognized post-spawn
147	compaction event:
148	
149	```json
150	{
151	  "type": "compaction_post_spawn",
152	  "schemaVersion": 1,
153	  "ts": "2026-04-22T12:34:56.000Z",
154	  "cw_id": "spawn-d3b0-...",
155	  "task_id": "M44-D7-T3",
156	  "spawn_id": "spawn-d3b0-...",
157	  "estimatedCwPct": 0.85,
158	  "actualCwPct": 0.97
159	}
160	```
161	
162	| Field            | Type           | Notes |
163	|------------------|----------------|-------|
164	| `type`           | string         | Always `"compaction_post_spawn"`. Distinguishes calibration rows from v1.0.0 `compact` rows in the same sink. v1.0.0 rows have NO `type` field (or default to `"compact"` when consumers normalize). |
165	| `schemaVersion`  | integer        | `1` (the calibration event schema is independent of the v1.0.0 row schema; both happen to start at `1`). |
166	| `ts`             | ISO-8601 string | When the calibration row was written (hook wall-clock). |
167	| `cw_id`          | string \| null | Per-CW attribution key (matches the value the orchestrator wrote into `token-usage.jsonl` rows for this spawn). For unattended workers, equals `spawn_id`. |
168	| `task_id`        | string \| null | Task identifier from the supervisor's active spawn correlation (e.g. `"M44-D7-T3"`). Null when the supervisor did not record a task at spawn time. |
169	| `spawn_id`       | string \| null | Stable spawn identifier (matches `cw_id` for unattended workers). |
170	| `estimatedCwPct` | number \| null | D6's pre-spawn prediction (0.0–1.0 fraction of the CW ceiling). Null when no estimate was recorded for this spawn. |
171	| `actualCwPct`    | number         | Observed CW utilization at the moment of compaction (0.0–1.0 fraction of the CW ceiling, derived from the compaction event's `input_tokens` divided by the configured CW ceiling). |
172	
173	### Hook lifecycle (v1.1.0 calibration hook)
174	
175	1. Claude Code fires `SessionStart` with a JSON payload on stdin.
176	2. The hook reads stdin (1 MiB cap, UTF-8).
177	3. If `payload.source !== "compact"` → no-op, exit 0.
178	4. If `<cwd>/.gsd-t/.unattended/state.json` is missing/unreadable, OR
179	   parsing fails, OR `state.status !== "running"`, OR no active spawn
180	   correlation can be derived → no-op (silent), exit 0.
181	5. Derive `actualCwPct` from the compaction payload's `input_tokens` (or
182	   nested `compactMetadata.preTokens`) divided by the configured CW
183	   ceiling (default `200000` input tokens). Clamp to `[0.0, 2.0]`.
184	6. Append one calibration row to `<cwd>/.gsd-t/metrics/compactions.jsonl`.
185	7. **Exit 0, always.** Throwing or non-zero exit breaks Claude Code.
186	
187	### Calibration hook guardrails
188	
189	- **Fail-open**: any exception is swallowed. No row is better than a
190	  broken session start.
191	- **Silent no-op when no active spawn**: the supervisor may not be running
192	  when a manual session compacts. Accepted.
193	- **Append-only, same sink**: calibration rows go into
194	  `compactions.jsonl` next to v1.0.0 rows. Consumers distinguish them by
195	  the `type` field (`"compaction_post_spawn"` vs absent / `"compact"`).
196	- **Independent of detector**: removing the calibration hook from
197	  settings.json disables only the calibration row; v1.0.0 detector rows
198	  continue to flow.
199	- **CW ceiling override**: if `state.json` carries an explicit CW ceiling
200	  (`cwCeilingTokens` or similar), the hook uses it; otherwise it falls
201	  back to a built-in default.
202	
203	## Consumers (future, not required by this contract)
204	
205	- `gsd-t metrics` — per-CW rollups.
206	- M44 orchestrator — CW-scoped attribution.
207	- `gsd-t tokens --regenerate-log` — timeline annotation.
208	
209	None of these are dependencies; this contract only defines the producer
210	shape.
211

Root cause: when the hook is `require()`d by a test file (as `test/m44-cw-attribution.test.js` does to exercise the `handle()` export directly), the module-level `process.stdin.setEncoding('utf8')` + `stdin.on('data'/'end')` listeners attach to the *test process's* stdin and keep the Node event loop alive indefinitely. All 19 D7 assertions pass in ~400ms isolated, but in the `npm test` full-suite run the file hangs on the unclosed stdin handle until the test runner kills it at its internal timeout (54885ms observed), which flagged the file as `✖` even though every sub-test was green. The `require.main === module` guard is the standard Node pattern for "only run top-level code when invoked as a script" and is functionally invisible in production (Claude Code invokes hooks via `node scripts/…` = `require.main === module` is true). After fix: 69/69 pass across D1 + D7 + m41-token-capture + compact-detector in 566ms combined. **Wave 1 gate status**: D1 DAG + D7 `cw_id` field + calibration hook all land; `.gsd-t/metrics/token-usage.jsonl` new rows carry `cw_id` when supplied; `.gsd-t/metrics/compactions.jsonl` can now receive `compaction_post_spawn` calibration rows. On `payload.source === "compact"` it reads `.gsd-t/.unattended/state.json`, derives `spawn_id` (sessionId fallback chain), `cw_id` (= spawn_id for unattended workers), `task_id` (activeTask / task_id / currentTask fallback chain), `estimatedCwPct` (D6's pre-spawn prediction if recorded), `cwCeilingTokens` (defaults to 200000), then derives `actualCwPct` from `payload.input_tokens` (or nested `compactMetadata.preTokens`) divided by the ceiling, clamped to [0, 2.0]. Appends one `{type: "compaction_post_spawn", schemaVersion: 1, ts, cw_id, task_id, spawn_id, estimatedCwPct, actualCwPct}` row to `.gsd-t/metrics/compactions.jsonl`. **Silent no-op** when payload isn't a compact source, when `.gsd-t/` is missing, when `state.json` is missing/unparseable/`status !== "running"`, when no `spawn_id` derivable, when no `input_tokens` derivable, or when `cwd` is non-absolute. consumers distinguish via `type` field (`"compaction_post_spawn"` vs absent). **Contracts bumped** in this domain (per constraints — must land in D7's commit, not Wave 2): `metrics-schema-contract.md` v2 → v2.1.0 (adds optional `cw_id` field with derivation rules — unattended: `cw_id == spawn_id`; in-session: `session_id + ":" + compaction_index` per the calibration hook's compact-fire counter), `compaction-events-contract.md` v1.0.0 → v1.1.0 (adds `compaction_post_spawn` event type, calibration hook lifecycle, guardrails, three-hook wiring matrix). **Test suite**: `test/m44-cw-attribution.test.js` — 19 tests, all green isolated: cw_id present → field serialized, cw_id numeric → coerced to string, cw_id absent/null/empty → field OMITTED (not null, not ""), captureSpawn forwards cw_id, `_buildJsonlRecord` cw_id branch matches session_id pattern, calibration hook with active spawn → calibration row written with correct task_id + estimatedCwPct + actualCwPct ~0.97 for 194000/200000, fallback to compactMetadata.preTokens, cwCeilingTokens override (95000/100000 = 0.95), no state.json → no row written, status !== running → no-op, malformed json → silent, source !== compact → no-op, missing input_tokens → no-op, empty/non-json stdin → no-op, non-absolute cwd → no-op, calibration row appends alongside v1.0.0 detector rows in same sink, zero ext deps. **Hard constraints all met** (per `.gsd-t/domains/m44-d7-per-cw-attribution/constraints.md`): backward-compatible cw_id (existing callers unchanged, absent ≠ null), silent no-op when no active spawn, zero ext deps, calibration event includes BOTH estimatedCwPct AND actualCwPct, contract bumps in D7's commit, `scripts/gsd-t-compact-detector.js` NOT modified. **Out of scope** (deferred): backfilling pre-D7 token-usage.jsonl rows with cw_id (525 historical rows; D6 falls back to per-iter median); economics logic (D6); parallel dispatch (D2); CW boundary detection algorithm beyond the spawn_id == cw_id rule. **D1 ran in parallel** (also Wave 1 Foundation per partition map: D1 + D7 both no-dep). User feedback during M44 unattended run: *"Each message and tool call should include a time stamp so I can easily see from the stream if something got stuck."* Added a small monospace HH:MM:SS pill (with ISO `title` tooltip) prepended to every frame in `scripts/gsd-t-transcript.html`. Single chokepoint: `appendFrame(el, arrivedAt)` now inserts a `<span class="ts">` as the first child of every frame div. SSE `onmessage` captures `arrivedAt = new Date()` once per message and threads it through `renderFrame` → all 9 per-type render helpers (`renderSystem`, `renderUserMessage`, `renderAssistantText`, `renderThinking`, `renderToolUse`, `renderToolResult`, `renderBoundary`, `renderRaw`, `renderCompactMarker`). Stuck-stream detection is now visual: two adjacent frames with identical HH:MM:SS = nothing arrived between them; a long gap before a new timestamp = the stream paused. Tests: `test/m44-transcript-timestamp.test.js` — 7 asserts (CSS rule present + appendFrame signature + fmtTs/pad2 helpers + SSE captures + renderFrame threading + script syntactically valid + sandboxed-eval unit test of `fmtTs(Date)` → `"09:05:07"`). All 14 transcript renderer tests pass (7 new + 7 prior). Renderer-only change — no contract, domain, or server impact.
170	
171	- 2026-04-22 23:30: [quick] supervisor self-healing — stream-json spawn args + JSON-or-integer pid parser. Two bugs surfaced during the M44 unattended launch: (1) supervisor's explicit `args:` to `claude -p` omitted `--output-format stream-json --verbose`, so the worker buffered all output until exit and the M42 transcript NDJSON stayed at 0 bytes mid-iter; (2) `supervisor.pid` is now JSON `{pid, projectDir, startedAt}` (per `bin/supervisor-pid-fingerprint.cjs writePidFile`) but three command-file readers parsed it with `parseInt(...trim(), 10)` — getting `NaN`, treating the supervisor as dead while it was running fine. Fix scope: spawn-args added to both the supervisor's explicit args (`bin/gsd-t-unattended.cjs:1381`) and the platform default fallback (`bin/gsd-t-unattended-platform.cjs:154`); PID readers in `commands/gsd-t-unattended-watch.md` (Step 2 PID branch), `commands/gsd-t-unattended.md` (Step 1b pre-flight + Step 3 liveness loop) now require the canonical `readPidFile()` from `bin/supervisor-pid-fingerprint.cjs` which transparently handles both JSON and legacy bare-integer forms. `commands/gsd-t-resume.md` already used `readPidFile()` — no change needed. Two new regression tests: `test/m44-supervisor-stream-json-args.test.js` (3 asserts: explicit-args site + default fallback both contain `--output-format stream-json --verbose --dangerously-skip-permissions`) and `test/m44-pid-parser-canonical.test.js` (6 asserts across three command files: no `parseInt(readFileSync(PID_FILE))` pattern + each uses `readPidFile`; plus a unit test that `readPidFile` returns `{form:"json"}` and `{form:"legacy"}` correctly). Full suite **1767/1769** — exactly the prior 1758/1760 baseline plus 9 new tests. Same 2 unrelated pre-existing failures (flaky dashboard port test + M43-progress-md hardcoded expectation now stale post-M44 promotion). This commit is shipped per the user's standing self-healing directive: *"if you see a problem and you've identified a fix for it, just stop unattended, implement your fix, and restart it without asking me."* Files changed: `bin/gsd-t-unattended.cjs`, `bin/gsd-t-unattended-platform.cjs`, `commands/gsd-t-unattended-watch.md`, `commands/gsd-t-unattended.md`, `test/m44-supervisor-stream-json-args.test.js` (new), `test/m44-pid-parser-canonical.test.js` (new). Next: relaunch `/gsd-t-unattended M44`.
172	
173	- 2026-04-22 22:50: [bootstrap] Promoted M44 from backlog #14 to `## Current Milestone` (status IN PROGRESS, target v3.18.10) ahead of `/gsd-t-unattended` launch. Status header flipped from `M43 COMPLETED` to `M44 IN PROGRESS`. Added M44 row to Milestones table above the M43 COMPLETED row. M43 reference DEFINED block dropped from Current Milestone (full M43 record preserved in the Completed Milestones table at line 70). Body summary lifted from backlog #14 (mode contracts NON-NEGOTIABLE, three invariants, two delivery layers, pre-reqs landed status, calibration corpus pointers, 7 proposed domains, success criteria, recommended flow → `partition` → `discuss` → `plan` → `execute` → `integrate` → `verify` → `complete-milestone` → tag v3.18.10). Bootstrap done because progress.md showed M43 COMPLETED but no successor; supervisor's resume worker keys off `## Current Milestone` so the supervisor would have had no target.
174	
175	- 2026-04-22 22:30: [quick] Q2b — compact_marker frame in transcript NDJSON + visualizer timeline badge. Extended `scripts/gsd-t-compact-detector.js` with a second write path: after appending the compactions.jsonl row, the hook now also appends a `{type:"compact_marker", ts, source, session_id, prior_session_id, trigger?, preTokens?, postTokens?}` frame to the most-recently-modified `.ndjson` in `<cwd>/.gsd-t/transcripts/`. Active transcript discovery (`findActiveTranscript`) reads the transcripts dir, filters `*.ndjson`, picks the file with the highest mtime — no-ops silently when the dir is absent or empty. Identical guardrails as the Q2a write: path-traversal guard (resolved transcript path must stay under `.gsd-t/transcripts/`), off-switch (no `.gsd-t/` → silent no-op), fail-open (transcript append failure swallowed so compactions.jsonl write always completes). Updated `scripts/gsd-t-transcript.html`: new CSS `.frame.compact-marker` (orange/yellow horizontal rule with centered badge), new `renderCompactMarker(frame)` function (exports as `window.__gsdtRenderCompactMarker`), badge label "⚡ CW boundary", tooltip = `ts + trigger + prior_session_id + session_id + preTokens + postTokens` — only fields present in the frame. `renderFrame()` dispatches `type === "compact_marker"` before all other frame types. New `test/m44-compact-marker-frame.test.js` — **11/11 pass**: schema required fields; optional fields included/omitted correctly; appends without overwriting prior content; most-recently-modified transcript chosen; no-op when `.gsd-t/` absent; no-op when transcripts dir absent; no-op when dir has no `.ndjson`; hook exits 0 even when transcript write fails (compactions.jsonl still written); renderer exports `renderCompactMarker`; badge has `cm-badge` class + "CW boundary" text + tooltip with trigger/tokens; no throw on legacy frame. Full suite **1758/1760** — same 2 pre-existing unrelated fails. Files changed: `scripts/gsd-t-compact-detector.js` (+76 lines: `findActiveTranscript` + `writeTranscriptMarker` + inline call), `scripts/gsd-t-transcript.html` (+39 lines: CSS + `renderCompactMarker` + `renderFrame` dispatch), `test/m44-compact-marker-frame.test.js` (new, 235 lines).

input.content:
# Conversation Capture Contract

**Status**: v1.0.0 — M45 D2 (initial release 2026-04-23)
**Sink**: `.gsd-t/transcripts/in-session-{sessionId}.ndjson`
**Producer**: `scripts/hooks/gsd-t-conversation-capture.js` — Claude Code hook script registered for `SessionStart`, `UserPromptSubmit`, `Stop`, and (opt-in) `PostToolUse`.

## Versions

- **v1.0.0** (2026-04-23, M45 D2) — initial schema. Frames: `session_start`, `user_turn`, `assistant_turn`, `tool_use`. File-name prefix `in-session-` is the viewer-left-rail discriminator.

## Why this exists

The orchestrator session's user↔assistant dialog has been invisible in the
visualizer. Only spawned agents show up in the transcript left rail (because
only spawns produce NDJSON via `bin/gsd-t-transcript-tee.cjs`). That means
mid-session compactions, user prompts, and assistant turns never appear in
the same surface as spawned work — satisfying this user-level contract:
"conversation stream visible in visualizer" (see `memory:
feedback_conversation_stream_visible.md`).

M43 D1 already captures per-turn *token usage* for the dialog channel via
`gsd-t-in-session-usage-hook.js`. That hook writes numeric rows into
`.gsd-t/metrics/token-usage.jsonl`. The conversation-capture hook is a
**parallel, independent** writer — it captures *content frames* for the
transcript viewer. Both hooks can (and should) be wired at the same time.

## File naming

```
.gsd-t/transcripts/in-session-{sessionId}.ndjson
```

- `{sessionId}` comes from the Claude Code hook payload's `session_id`. When
  the payload omits a session id, the hook falls back to a deterministic
  per-process id of the form `pid-{sha1(pid:startedAt)[:12]}` so the filename
  is always non-empty.
- The `in-session-` prefix is a **contract with the viewer**. The viewer's
  left rail decides label rendering by checking whether the NDJSON stem starts
  with `in-session-` — no server-side `type` field is required.
- The same prefix is a **contract with the compact-detector**. When the
  detector falls back to in-session target selection (no fresh spawn NDJSON
  exists), it writes the locked `compact_marker` frame into the active
  in-session NDJSON.

## Frame schema (v1.0.0)

Each frame is a JSON object on a single line, UTF-8, newline-terminated.

All frames share these fields:

| Field        | Type         | Required | Notes |
|--------------|--------------|----------|-------|
| `type`       | string enum  | yes      | `session_start` \| `user_turn` \| `assistant_turn` \| `tool_use` \| `compact_marker` (last emitted by the compact-detector, not this hook) |
| `ts`         | ISO-8601 str | yes      | When the hook wrote the frame (hook wall-clock). |
| `session_id` | string       | yes      | Claude Code session id, or the `pid-…` fallback. |

Per-type additive fields:

### `session_start`
Optional marker emitted on `SessionStart`. Exists so the left-rail
discovers the in-session NDJSON immediately, before the first user turn.

No additional fields.

### `user_turn`

| Field        | Type    | Required | Notes |
|--------------|---------|----------|-------|
| `content`    | string  | no       | The user's prompt text. Capped at 16 KB per frame. Absent if the hook payload did not carry the prompt. |
| `truncated`  | boolean | no       | `true` when `content` was truncated to fit the 16 KB cap. Absent means not truncated. |
| `message_id` | string  | no       | Claude Code's message id, when the hook payload carries it. |

### `assistant_turn`

| Field        | Type    | Required | Notes |
|--------------|---------|----------|-------|
| `content`    | string  | no       | The assistant's final message text. Capped at 16 KB. Absent if the hook payload did not carry it (Stop hooks sometimes don't). |
| `truncated`  | boolean | no       | As above. |
| `message_id` | string  | no       | As above. |

A `Stop` event with no assistant content still writes a stub frame with
only `type` + `ts` + `session_id`. The stub matters — it tells the viewer
the turn completed.

### `tool_use` (opt-in)

Written only when the env var `GSD_T_CAPTURE_TOOL_USES=1` is set at hook
invocation time. Default is OFF — full tool payloads would bloat the
NDJSON and the `events/*.jsonl` sink already records them in structured
form.

| Field          | Type   | Required | Notes |
|----------------|--------|----------|-------|
| `name`         | string | no       | Tool name from the hook payload. |
| `tool_use_id`  | string | no       | Correlates with `events/*.jsonl` rows. |
| `duration_ms`  | number | no       | Tool execution duration in milliseconds. |

### `compact_marker`

This hook does NOT emit `compact_marker`. It is emitted by
`scripts/gsd-t-compact-detector.js` (contract:
`compaction-events-contract.md` v1.1.0). The detector's fallback
target-selection may target an `in-session-*.ndjson` when no spawn NDJSON
is fresher — but the frame shape is locked by `compaction-events-contract.md`
and is NOT a v1.0.0 addition here.

## Hook entry points

Registered in `~/.claude/settings.json`:

| Hook event         | Produces frame      | Required |
|--------------------|---------------------|----------|
| `SessionStart`     | `session_start`     | yes      |
| `UserPromptSubmit` | `user_turn`         | yes      |
| `Stop`             | `assistant_turn`    | yes      |
| `PostToolUse`      | `tool_use`          | opt-in (`GSD_T_CAPTURE_TOOL_USES=1`) |

See `templates/CLAUDE-global.md` § "In-Session Conversation Capture" for
the literal settings.json block.

## Session-id resolution

1. **Primary**: `payload.session_id` from the Claude Code hook stdin.
2. **Fallback**: `pid-{sha1(pid + ':' + startedAt)[:12]}` — stable within one
   hook-script invocation, sufficient to keep the filename non-empty. This
   fallback should never fire in practice; it exists so a malformed payload
   does not crash the hook or produce `in-session-.ndjson`.

## Project-dir resolution

The hook writes to `<projectDir>/.gsd-t/transcripts/…`. `projectDir` is
resolved in order:

1. `GSD_T_PROJECT_DIR` env var, if set and `<dir>/.gsd-t` exists.
2. `payload.cwd`, if absolute and `<cwd>/.gsd-t` exists.
3. Walk up from `process.cwd()` (up to 10 levels) looking for
   `.gsd-t/progress.md`.
4. If none: **silent no-op**. The hook must never interrupt a non-GSD-T
   Claude Code session.

## Contract with the viewer

The viewer at `scripts/gsd-t-transcript.html` consumes this producer by:

1. Polling `/transcripts` (or `/api/spawns-index`) for spawn-index rows.
2. For each row, checking whether `spawnId.startsWith('in-session-')`.
3. When true, labeling the left-rail node with `💬 conversation` instead of
   the default `▶ spawn`. CSS class `label-in-session` is applied for
   optional per-theme styling.

No server-side `type` field is required. The filename prefix is the sole
discriminator.

## Contract with the compact-detector

`scripts/gsd-t-compact-detector.js` owns target-selection for the
`compact_marker` frame. M45 D2 adds a **fallback** branch:

1. Existing behavior (unchanged): pick the most-recently-modified NDJSON.
2. New fallback: when no non-`in-session-*` NDJSON has been modified in
   the last 30 seconds AND a fresh `in-session-*.ndjson` exists, target
   that file instead.
3. Log the fallback decision to stderr
   (`compact-detector: targeting in-session-{sessionId}.ndjson (fallback)`)
   so regressions are visible during debugging.

The `compact_marker` frame shape itself is **locked** by
`compaction-events-contract.md` v1.1.0 — M45 D2 does not change it.

## Guardrails

- **Fail-open**: every error is caught, logged to stderr, exit 0. A broken
  capture never kills a Claude Code session.
- **Append-only**: the hook never truncates or rewrites an existing
  in-session NDJSON.
- **Content cap**: 16 KB per frame; over-cap writes carry
  `"truncated": true`.
- **Stdin cap**: 1 MiB defense-in-depth; over-cap drops the write.
- **Path-traversal guard**: resolved output path must stay under
  `<projectDir>/.gsd-t/transcripts/`.
- **Off-switch**: deleting `.gsd-t/` in a project disables the hook for
  that project. Removing the entries from `~/.claude/settings.json`
  disables it globally.

## Consumers (future, not required by this contract)

- The dashboard server may one day emit a `type: "in-session"` field on
  `/api/spawns-index` rows. When it does, the viewer MAY consume that in
  preference to the filename prefix — but the filename prefix remains the
  canonical discriminator.
- Replay / export tooling can treat `in-session-*.ndjson` frames as
  first-class transcript rows alongside spawn-NDJSON frames.

## M45 D1 Report — viewer-route-fix complete

**Files changed** (all absolute):
- `/Users/david/projects/GSD-T/scripts/gsd-t-dashboard-server.js` — `handleTranscriptsList` text/html branch refactored to serve `gsd-t-transcript.html` with empty `__SPAWN_ID__`; `renderTranscriptsHtml` (76 lines) deleted; exports updated; `startServer` route now threads `tHtmlPath` into the handler.
- `/Users/david/projects/GSD-T/test/transcripts-html-page.test.js` — rewritten for new contract.
- `/Users/david/projects/GSD-T/test/m45-d1-transcripts-route-viewer.test.js` — NEW.
- `/Users/david/projects/GSD-T/.gsd-t/domains/m45-d1-viewer-route-fix/tasks.md` — T-1..T-4 marked `[x]`; T-5 deferred with note.
- `/Users/david/projects/GSD-T/.gsd-t/progress.md` — Decision Log entry appended.

**Tests added / refactored**:
- Added `test/m45-d1-transcripts-route-viewer.test.js` with 4 tests: viewer served at `/transcripts`, identical HTML skeleton to `/transcript/:id`, JSON back-compat for `application/json` and `*/*`.
- Refactored `test/transcripts-html-page.test.js` down to 3 tests: viewer DOM markers on text/html branch, JSON for `*/*`, JSON for `application/json`.

**Test results**:
- Targeted: `7/7 pass` (`transcripts-html-page.test.js` + `m45-d1-transcripts-route-viewer.test.js`).
- Full suite: `Unit: 1912/1914 pass | E2E: N/A — no playwright config`. The 2 failures are pre-existing, unrelated to D1 (`test/event-stream.test.js:341` expects a null command field but sees `gsd-t-resume`; `test/watch-progress-writer.test.js:222` expects `shell-*` filename but sees `supervisor-iter-*`). Baseline tolerance per task brief was 4 pre-existing fails; I'm well inside it with zero new fails.

**Commit SHA**: `9e5d95567ca1ede2fc96ad7e90fb347df6b9c43a`.

**Deviations**:
- T-5 manual smoke deferred to user (no browser in unattended worker). Recorded in tasks.md.
- T-4 (renderTranscriptsHtml delete) was executed since T-3 dropped every test that referenced it — repo-wide grep confirms no other consumers. The optional cleanup collapsed into this commit as the task brief permits.

**For verify phase**: D1 is self-contained and file-disjoint from D2 per the partition. 254	**Context Meter (M34/M38, v3.12.10+)** — The real context-window measurement feeding the headless-default spawn decision. A PostToolUse hook (`scripts/gsd-t-context-meter.js`) runs after every tool call, uses local token estimation to write the current input-token count into `.gsd-t/.context-meter-state.json`. `getSessionStatus()` reads that state file (fresh window = 5 minutes) with a historical heuristic fallback when the file is missing or stale. Command files consume the signal via a small bash shim (`CTX_PCT=$(node -e "…tb.getSessionStatus('.').pct")`). **Single-band model** (context-meter-contract v1.3.0): there's one threshold (default 85%) and one action — hand off to a detached headless spawn. No three-band routing, no silent downgrades, no MANDATORY STOP prose. The meter exists to inform spawn-time routing, not to pause work in-flight.

Command files consume the signal via a small bash shim (`CTX_PCT=$(node -e "…tb.getSessionStatus('.').pct")`). **Single-band model** (context-meter-contract v1.3.0): there's one threshold (default 85%) and one action — hand off to a detached headless spawn. **Context Meter (M34/M38, v3.12.10+)** — The real context-window measurement feeding the headless-default spawn decision. A PostToolUse hook (`scripts/gsd-t-context-meter.js`) runs after every tool call, uses local token estimation to write the current input-token count into `.gsd-t/.context-meter-state.json`. `getSessionStatus()` reads that state file (fresh window = 5 minutes) with a historical heuristic fallback when the file is missing or stale. Command files consume the signal via a small bash shim (`CTX_PCT=$(node -e "…tb.getSessionStatus('.').pct")`). **Single-band model** (context-meter-contract v1.3.0): there's one threshold (default 85%) and one action — hand off to a detached headless spawn. No three-band routing, no silent downgrades, no MANDATORY STOP prose. The meter exists to inform spawn-time routing, not to pause work in-flight.

## In-Session Conversation Capture (M45 D2)

The orchestrator session's user↔assistant dialog is captured into
`.gsd-t/transcripts/in-session-{sessionId}.ndjson` via a dedicated hook
script (`scripts/hooks/gsd-t-conversation-capture.js`). The viewer's left
rail labels these entries `💬 conversation` (front-end-only discriminator
— the `in-session-` filename prefix is the contract).

This hook captures **content** (user prompts + assistant replies). It is
complementary to `scripts/hooks/gsd-t-in-session-usage-hook.js` (M43 D1),
which captures per-turn **token usage** into
`.gsd-t/metrics/token-usage.jsonl`. Both hooks coexist on the same events.

**Install block** (append to `~/.claude/settings.json` alongside the existing
context-meter, version-check, compact-detector, and in-session-usage hooks):

```json
{
  "hooks": {
    "SessionStart": [
      { "matcher": "",
        "hooks": [{ "type": "command",
                    "command": "node \"$HOME/.claude/scripts/hooks/gsd-t-conversation-capture.js\"",
                    "async": true }] }
    ],
    "UserPromptSubmit": [
      { "matcher": "",
        "hooks": [{ "type": "command",
                    "command": "node \"$HOME/.claude/scripts/hooks/gsd-t-conversation-capture.js\"",
                    "async": true }] }
    ],
    "Stop": [
      { "matcher": "",
        "hooks": [{ "type": "command",
                    "command": "node \"$HOME/.claude/scripts/hooks/gsd-t-conversation-capture.js\"",
                    "async": true }] }
    ],
    "PostToolUse": [
      { "matcher": "",
        "hooks": [{ "type": "command",
                    "command": "GSD_T_CAPTURE_TOOL_USES=1 node \"$HOME/.claude/scripts/hooks/gsd-t-conversation-capture.js\"",
                    "async": true }] }
    ]
  }
}
```

The `PostToolUse` entry is **opt-in** via `GSD_T_CAPTURE_TOOL_USES=1`. Leave it
unset unless you want per-tool frames in the NDJSON (full tool payloads are
already recorded in `events/*.jsonl`).

Contract: `.gsd-t/contracts/conversation-capture-contract.md` v1.0.0. Frame
schema, file-naming, and session-id resolution rules are locked there.

## Observability Logging (MANDATORY)

**T-1**: Create `scripts/hooks/gsd-t-conversation-capture.js`.
