- SessionStart: resolve session_id from payload. Optional: write synthetic session_start frame with ts only. (Recommend write one — makes left-rail discovery immediate.)
- UserPromptSubmit: append `{"type":"user_turn","ts":"<iso>","session_id":"<id>","content":"<prompt>","message_id":"<id>"}` to `.gsd-t/transcripts/in-session-{sessionId}.ndjson`.
- Stop: append `{"type":"assistant_turn","ts":"<iso>","session_id":"<id>","content":"<assistant-final>","message_id":"<id>"}` (stub with ts only if content unavailable).
- PostToolUse: guarded behind `GSD_T_CAPTURE_TOOL_USES=1` env. Skip writing by default (ship in follow-up).
- Cap `content` at 16 KB; if exceeded, add `"truncated": true` marker.
- Never throw — catch all errors, log to stderr, exit 0.
- Fallback for session_id if hook payload missing: hash of `pid + startedAt`.
- Resolve project dir: look up `GSD_T_PROJECT_DIR` env or walk up from cwd to find `.gsd-t/progress.md`; if not found, exit 0 silently. (The same pattern `gsd-t-in-session-usage-hook.js` uses.)

Default label remains `▶ spawn`.

**T-4**: Extend `scripts/gsd-t-compact-detector.js` target-selection block (~line 160–200). Keep existing spawn-NDJSON preference. Add fallback: if no spawn-NDJSON modified within last 30s AND a fresh `in-session-*.ndjson` exists, target that instead. **T-5**: Update `templates/CLAUDE-global.md`. Grep for existing SessionStart/UserPromptSubmit/Stop/PostToolUse hook block that wires `gsd-t-in-session-usage-hook.js`. **T-7** (manual smoke): Skip — you're in an unattended worker. Note it's deferred to the user.

1	# Red Team Subagent Prompt — Adversarial QA (per-domain)
2	
3	You are a Red Team QA adversary. Your job is to BREAK the code that was just written for this domain. You operate with inverted incentives — your value is measured by REAL bugs found, not tests passed.
4	
5	## Hard Rules
6	
7	- **Bugs found = value.** A short attack list is failure.
8	- **False positives DESTROY your credibility.** Never report something you have not reproduced. A bug is `I did X, expected Y, got Z` with proof.
9	- Style opinions are NOT bugs. Theoretical concerns are NOT bugs.
10	- You are done ONLY when you have exhausted every category below — either find a real bug or document exactly what you tried and why it didn't break.
11	
12	## Attack Categories (exhaust ALL)
13	
14	1. **Contract Violations** — Read `.gsd-t/contracts/`. Does the code match every contract exactly? Test each endpoint/interface/schema shape.
15	2. **Boundary Inputs** — empty strings, null, undefined, huge payloads, special characters, SQL injection, XSS, path traversal.
16	3. **State Transitions** — actions out of order, double-submit, concurrent access, refresh mid-flow.
17	4. **Error Paths** — remove env vars, kill the database, send malformed requests. Does the code degrade gracefully or crash?
18	5. **Missing Flows** — Read `docs/requirements.md`. Are there user flows that exist in requirements but have no test coverage?
19	6. **Regression** — Run the FULL test suite. Did any existing test break?
20	7. **E2E Functional Gaps** — Review every Playwright spec. Are they testing real behavior or just checking element existence? Flag and rewrite shallow specs.
21	8. **Design Fidelity** (only if `.gsd-t/contracts/design-contract.md` exists) — see `design-verify-subagent.md`. The design verification agent runs this attack category as a separate dedicated agent; do not duplicate its work, but flag any design-related bug you incidentally find.
22	
23	## Exploratory Testing (only if Playwright MCP is available)
24	
25	Spend 5 minutes on adversarial interactive exploration via Playwright MCP — race conditions, double-submits, concurrent access, rapid state transitions, error recovery. Tag findings `[EXPLORATORY]`. Skip silently if MCP is not available.
26	
27	## Report Format
28	
29	For each bug:
30	- **BUG-{N}**: severity CRITICAL | HIGH | MEDIUM | LOW
31	  - **Reproduction**: exact steps
32	  - **Expected**: what should happen
33	  - **Actual**: what does happen
34	  - **Proof**: test file or command that demonstrates the bug
35	
36	Summary:
37	- BUGS FOUND: {count} with severity breakdown
38	- COVERAGE GAPS: {untested flows from requirements}
39	- SHALLOW TESTS REWRITTEN: {count}
40	- CONTRACTS VERIFIED: {N}/{total}
41	- ATTACK VECTORS TRIED: every category attempted, each with one-line result
42	- VERDICT: `FAIL` ({N} bugs found) | `GRUDGING PASS` (exhaustive search, nothing found)
43	
44	Write findings to `.gsd-t/red-team-report.md`. If bugs found, also append to `.gsd-t/qa-issues.md`.
45

file.content:
# Red Team Subagent Prompt — Adversarial QA (per-domain)

You are a Red Team QA adversary. Your job is to BREAK the code that was just written for this domain. You operate with inverted incentives — your value is measured by REAL bugs found, not tests passed.

## Hard Rules

- **Bugs found = value.** A short attack list is failure.
- **False positives DESTROY your credibility.** Never report something you have not reproduced. A bug is `I did X, expected Y, got Z` with proof.
- Style opinions are NOT bugs. Theoretical concerns are NOT bugs.
- You are done ONLY when you have exhausted every category below — either find a real bug or document exactly what you tried and why it didn't break.

## Attack Categories (exhaust ALL)

1. **Contract Violations** — Read `.gsd-t/contracts/`. Does the code match every contract exactly? Test each endpoint/interface/schema shape.
2. **Boundary Inputs** — empty strings, null, undefined, huge payloads, special characters, SQL injection, XSS, path traversal.
3. **State Transitions** — actions out of order, double-submit, concurrent access, refresh mid-flow.
4. **Error Paths** — remove env vars, kill the database, send malformed requests. Does the code degrade gracefully or crash?
5. **Missing Flows** — Read `docs/requirements.md`. Are there user flows that exist in requirements but have no test coverage?
6. **Regression** — Run the FULL test suite. Did any existing test break?
7. **E2E Functional Gaps** — Review every Playwright spec. Are they testing real behavior or just checking element existence? Flag and rewrite shallow specs.
8. **Design Fidelity** (only if `.gsd-t/contracts/design-contract.md` exists) — see `design-verify-subagent.md`. The design verification agent runs this attack category as a separate dedicated agent; do not duplicate its work, but flag any design-related bug you incidentally find.

## Exploratory Testing (only if Playwright MCP is available)

Spend 5 minutes on adversarial interactive exploration via Playwright MCP — race conditions, double-submits, concurrent access, rapid state transitions, error recovery. Tag findings `[EXPLORATORY]`. Skip silently if MCP is not available.

## Report Format

For each bug:
- **BUG-{N}**: severity CRITICAL | HIGH | MEDIUM | LOW
  - **Reproduction**: exact steps
  - **Expected**: what should happen
  - **Actual**: what does happen
  - **Proof**: test file or command that demonstrates the bug

Summary:
- BUGS FOUND: {count} with severity breakdown
- COVERAGE GAPS: {untested flows from requirements}
- SHALLOW TESTS REWRITTEN: {count}
- CONTRACTS VERIFIED: {N}/{total}
- ATTACK VECTORS TRIED: every category attempted, each with one-line result
- VERDICT: `FAIL` ({N} bugs found) | `GRUDGING PASS` (exhaustive search, nothing found)

Write findings to `.gsd-t/red-team-report.md`. If bugs found, also append to `.gsd-t/qa-issues.md`.

input.prompt:
You are the Red Team adversarial QA subagent for M45 Wave 1 (D1 viewer-route-fix + D2 in-session-conversation-capture). Your job is to find REAL bugs — not style issues, not theoretical concerns — in the code that just landed in commits 9e5d955 (D1) and e1e1fd2 (D2) on main branch.

Full protocol: `/Users/david/projects/GSD-T/templates/prompts/red-team-subagent.md`. Follow it.

## Scope of changes to attack

**D1 commit 9e5d955** touched:
- `scripts/gsd-t-dashboard-server.js` — `handleTranscriptsList` text/html branch now reads `gsd-t-transcript.html`, substitutes `__SPAWN_ID__` → `""`, returns `text/html; charset=utf-8`. `renderTranscriptsHtml` fn deleted.
- `test/transcripts-html-page.test.js` — refactored.
- `test/m45-d1-transcripts-route-viewer.test.js` — new (4 tests).

**D2 commit e1e1fd2** touched:
- `scripts/hooks/gsd-t-conversation-capture.js` — new hook (SessionStart/UserPromptSubmit/Stop + opt-in PostToolUse).
- `scripts/gsd-t-compact-detector.js` — `findActiveTranscript` fallback (in-session NDJSON when spawn NDJSON stale).
- `scripts/gsd-t-transcript.html` — left-rail labels `in-session-*` entries with `💬 conversation` badge.
- `templates/CLAUDE-global.md` — new hook block documentation.
- `.gsd-t/contracts/conversation-capture-contract.md` v1.0.0 — new contract.
- 3 new test files: `test/m45-d2-conversation-capture.test.js`, `test/m45-d2-compact-detector-in-session-fallback.test.js`, `test/m45-d2-transcript-left-rail-in-session.test.js`.

## What to attack (exhaust all categories from the protocol)

Key adversarial angles to probe specifically:

1. **Contract violation** — does `conversation-capture-contract.md` v1.0.0 match the actual hook behavior? Read both, compare field names, frame shape, session-id source, file naming. Any drift is a bug.

2. **Boundary inputs on the hook**:
   - Missing `session_id` in payload — does the hook exit 0 or throw?
   - Missing `hook_event_name` — same.
   - Non-JSON stdin — does the hook exit 0?
   - Prompt containing `../`, null bytes, 1 MB content — does the 16 KB cap actually truncate? Does path-traversal guard actually work?
   - Write contention: two concurrent UserPromptSubmit hooks for the same session — append-only guarantee preserved?

3. **handleTranscriptsList edge cases**:
   - `gsd-t-transcript.html` file missing/unreadable — does the route 500 gracefully or crash?
   - `Accept` header with `text/html,application/json;q=0.9` (browser fallback) — which branch wins?
   - Path traversal in a spawn-id query string — does it reach disk?
   - JSON response shape still `{spawns: [...]}` even with zero transcripts on disk?

4. **Compact-detector fallback**:
   - Directory with stale spawn NDJSON (>30s old) AND stale in-session NDJSON — does it pick either or neither?
   - No transcripts dir at all — graceful or crash?
   - in-session NDJSON with trailing non-JSON line — does fallback writer corrupt it?
   - 30s threshold: what happens at exactly 30s? Off-by-one?

5. **Viewer left-rail label detection**:
   - Spawn-id exactly `in-session-` (no id after) — correctly labeled or missing info?
   - Spawn-id containing `in-session-` as substring but not prefix (e.g., `foo-in-session-bar`) — false-positive label?
   - CSS: does `.label-in-session` rule actually exist in the final HTML, or is only the conditional render present?

6. **Contracts touched**:
   - `stream-json-sink-contract.md` v1.2.0 — does the new hook respect its entry-point contract?
   - `compaction-events-contract.md` v1.1.0 — compact_marker frame shape unchanged?

7. **Regression**: Run the FULL test suite (`npm test`). Baseline is 1932/1934 pass (2 pre-existing unrelated fails documented by both subagents). Any NEW failures are bugs.

8. **E2E functional gaps**: D2 added 20 tests. Read them. Any that only assert existence (`existsSync`, `toBeTruthy`) without asserting on *content* or *behavior*? Those are shallow — flag and rewrite.

## Process

1. Read the protocol (path above).
2. Read the two commits' diffs (`git show 9e5d955`, `git show e1e1fd2`).
3. Read the test files that landed.
4. Attack each category. Reproduce every bug before reporting — `I did X, expected Y, got Z, here's the command`.
5. Run `npm test` once — capture baseline.
6. Write `/Users/david/projects/GSD-T/.gsd-t/red-team-report.md` with findings per the protocol format.
7. If bugs found with severity CRITICAL or HIGH, also append to `/Users/david/projects/GSD-T/.gsd-t/qa-issues.md`.

## Report back

In your return message, concise summary (under 250 words):
- BUGS FOUND: {count} with severity breakdown
- COVERAGE GAPS
- SHALLOW TESTS
- CONTRACTS VERIFIED: {N}/{total}
- ATTACK VECTORS TRIED (one line each)
- VERDICT: FAIL ({N} bugs) | GRUDGING PASS

If VERDICT = FAIL with CRITICAL/HIGH, name each bug so the fix loop can address them. If GRUDGING PASS, list the attack vectors tried so we can audit your thoroughness.

content.content:
1	# Red Team Subagent Prompt — Adversarial QA (per-domain)
2	
3	You are a Red Team QA adversary. Your job is to BREAK the code that was just written for this domain. You operate with inverted incentives — your value is measured by REAL bugs found, not tests passed.
4	
5	## Hard Rules
6	
7	- **Bugs found = value.** A short attack list is failure.
8	- **False positives DESTROY your credibility.** Never report something you have not reproduced. A bug is `I did X, expected Y, got Z` with proof.
9	- Style opinions are NOT bugs. Theoretical concerns are NOT bugs.
10	- You are done ONLY when you have exhausted every category below — either find a real bug or document exactly what you tried and why it didn't break.
11	
12	## Attack Categories (exhaust ALL)
13	
14	1. **Contract Violations** — Read `.gsd-t/contracts/`. Does the code match every contract exactly? Test each endpoint/interface/schema shape.
15	2. **Boundary Inputs** — empty strings, null, undefined, huge payloads, special characters, SQL injection, XSS, path traversal.
16	3. **State Transitions** — actions out of order, double-submit, concurrent access, refresh mid-flow.
17	4. **Error Paths** — remove env vars, kill the database, send malformed requests. Does the code degrade gracefully or crash?
18	5. **Missing Flows** — Read `docs/requirements.md`. Are there user flows that exist in requirements but have no test coverage?
19	6. **Regression** — Run the FULL test suite. Did any existing test break?
20	7. **E2E Functional Gaps** — Review every Playwright spec. Are they testing real behavior or just checking element existence? Flag and rewrite shallow specs.
21	8. **Design Fidelity** (only if `.gsd-t/contracts/design-contract.md` exists) — see `design-verify-subagent.md`. The design verification agent runs this attack category as a separate dedicated agent; do not duplicate its work, but flag any design-related bug you incidentally find.
22	
23	## Exploratory Testing (only if Playwright MCP is available)
24	
25	Spend 5 minutes on adversarial interactive exploration via Playwright MCP — race conditions, double-submits, concurrent access, rapid state transitions, error recovery. Tag findings `[EXPLORATORY]`. Skip silently if MCP is not available.
26	
27	## Report Format
28	
29	For each bug:
30	- **BUG-{N}**: severity CRITICAL | HIGH | MEDIUM | LOW
31	  - **Reproduction**: exact steps
32	  - **Expected**: what should happen
33	  - **Actual**: what does happen
34	  - **Proof**: test file or command that demonstrates the bug
35	
36	Summary:
37	- BUGS FOUND: {count} with severity breakdown
38	- COVERAGE GAPS: {untested flows from requirements}
39	- SHALLOW TESTS REWRITTEN: {count}
40	- CONTRACTS VERIFIED: {N}/{total}
41	- ATTACK VECTORS TRIED: every category attempted, each with one-line result
42	- VERDICT: `FAIL` ({N} bugs found) | `GRUDGING PASS` (exhaustive search, nothing found)
43	
44	Write findings to `.gsd-t/red-team-report.md`. If bugs found, also append to `.gsd-t/qa-issues.md`.
45

1	# Conversation Capture Contract
2	
3	**Status**: v1.0.0 — M45 D2 (initial release 2026-04-23)
4	**Sink**: `.gsd-t/transcripts/in-session-{sessionId}.ndjson`
5	**Producer**: `scripts/hooks/gsd-t-conversation-capture.js` — Claude Code hook script registered for `SessionStart`, `UserPromptSubmit`, `Stop`, and (opt-in) `PostToolUse`.
6	
7	## Versions
8	
9	- **v1.0.0** (2026-04-23, M45 D2) — initial schema. Frames: `session_start`, `user_turn`, `assistant_turn`, `tool_use`. File-name prefix `in-session-` is the viewer-left-rail discriminator.
10	
11	## Why this exists
12	
13	The orchestrator session's user↔assistant dialog has been invisible in the
14	visualizer. Only spawned agents show up in the transcript left rail (because
15	only spawns produce NDJSON via `bin/gsd-t-transcript-tee.cjs`). That means
16	mid-session compactions, user prompts, and assistant turns never appear in
17	the same surface as spawned work — satisfying this user-level contract:
18	"conversation stream visible in visualizer" (see `memory:
19	feedback_conversation_stream_visible.md`).
20	
21	M43 D1 already captures per-turn *token usage* for the dialog channel via
22	`gsd-t-in-session-usage-hook.js`. That hook writes numeric rows into
23	`.gsd-t/metrics/token-usage.jsonl`. The conversation-capture hook is a
24	**parallel, independent** writer — it captures *content frames* for the
25	transcript viewer. Both hooks can (and should) be wired at the same time.
26	
27	## File naming
28	
29	```
30	.gsd-t/transcripts/in-session-{sessionId}.ndjson
31	```
32	
33	- `{sessionId}` comes from the Claude Code hook payload's `session_id`. When
34	  the payload omits a session id, the hook falls back to a deterministic
35	  per-process id of the form `pid-{sha1(pid:startedAt)[:12]}` so the filename
36	  is always non-empty.
37	- The `in-session-` prefix is a **contract with the viewer**. The viewer's
38	  left rail decides label rendering by checking whether the NDJSON stem starts
39	  with `in-session-` — no server-side `type` field is required.
40	- The same prefix is a **contract with the compact-detector**. When the
41	  detector falls back to in-session target selection (no fresh spawn NDJSON
42	  exists), it writes the locked `compact_marker` frame into the active
43	  in-session NDJSON.
44	
45	## Frame schema (v1.0.0)
46	
47	Each frame is a JSON object on a single line, UTF-8, newline-terminated.
48	
49	All frames share these fields:
50	
51	| Field        | Type         | Required | Notes |
52	|--------------|--------------|----------|-------|
53	| `type`       | string enum  | yes      | `session_start` \| `user_turn` \| `assistant_turn` \| `tool_use` \| `compact_marker` (last emitted by the compact-detector, not this hook) |
54	| `ts`         | ISO-8601 str | yes      | When the hook wrote the frame (hook wall-clock). |
55	| `session_id` | string       | yes      | Claude Code session id, or the `pid-…` fallback. |
56	
57	Per-type additive fields:
58	
59	### `session_start`
60	Optional marker emitted on `SessionStart`. Exists so the left-rail
61	discovers the in-session NDJSON immediately, before the first user turn.
62	
63	No additional fields.
64	
65	### `user_turn`
66	
67	| Field        | Type    | Required | Notes |
68	|--------------|---------|----------|-------|
69	| `content`    | string  | no       | The user's prompt text. Capped at 16 KB per frame. Absent if the hook payload did not carry the prompt. |
70	| `truncated`  | boolean | no       | `true` when `content` was truncated to fit the 16 KB cap. Absent means not truncated. |
71	| `message_id` | string  | no       | Claude Code's message id, when the hook payload carries it. |
72	
73	### `assistant_turn`
74	
75	| Field        | Type    | Required | Notes |
76	|--------------|---------|----------|-------|
77	| `content`    | string  | no       | The assistant's final message text. Capped at 16 KB. Absent if the hook payload did not carry it (Stop hooks sometimes don't). |
78	| `truncated`  | boolean | no       | As above. |
79	| `message_id` | string  | no       | As above. |
80	
81	A `Stop` event with no assistant content still writes a stub frame with
82	only `type` + `ts` + `session_id`. The stub matters — it tells the viewer
83	the turn completed.
84	
85	### `tool_use` (opt-in)
86	
87	Written only when the env var `GSD_T_CAPTURE_TOOL_USES=1` is set at hook
88	invocation time. Default is OFF — full tool payloads would bloat the
89	NDJSON and the `events/*.jsonl` sink already records them in structured
90	form.
91	
92	| Field          | Type   | Required | Notes |
93	|----------------|--------|----------|-------|
94	| `name`         | string | no       | Tool name from the hook payload. |
95	| `tool_use_id`  | string | no       | Correlates with `events/*.jsonl` rows. |
96	| `duration_ms`  | number | no       | Tool execution duration in milliseconds. |
97	
98	### `compact_marker`
99	
100	This hook does NOT emit `compact_marker`. It is emitted by
101	`scripts/gsd-t-compact-detector.js` (contract:
102	`compaction-events-contract.md` v1.1.0). The detector's fallback
103	target-selection may target an `in-session-*.ndjson` when no spawn NDJSON
104	is fresher — but the frame shape is locked by `compaction-events-contract.md`
105	and is NOT a v1.0.0 addition here.
106	
107	## Hook entry points
108	
109	Registered in `~/.claude/settings.json`:
110	
111	| Hook event         | Produces frame      | Required |
112	|--------------------|---------------------|----------|
113	| `SessionStart`     | `session_start`     | yes      |
114	| `UserPromptSubmit` | `user_turn`         | yes      |
115	| `Stop`             | `assistant_turn`    | yes      |
116	| `PostToolUse`      | `tool_use`          | opt-in (`GSD_T_CAPTURE_TOOL_USES=1`) |
117	
118	See `templates/CLAUDE-global.md` § "In-Session Conversation Capture" for
119	the literal settings.json block.
120	
121	## Session-id resolution
122	
123	1. **Primary**: `payload.session_id` from the Claude Code hook stdin.
124	2. **Fallback**: `pid-{sha1(pid + ':' + startedAt)[:12]}` — stable within one
125	   hook-script invocation, sufficient to keep the filename non-empty. This
126	   fallback should never fire in practice; it exists so a malformed payload
127	   does not crash the hook or produce `in-session-.ndjson`.
128	
129	## Project-dir resolution
130	
131	The hook writes to `<projectDir>/.gsd-t/transcripts/…`. `projectDir` is
132	resolved in order:
133	
134	1. `GSD_T_PROJECT_DIR` env var, if set and `<dir>/.gsd-t` exists.
135	2. `payload.cwd`, if absolute and `<cwd>/.gsd-t` exists.
136	3. Walk up from `process.cwd()` (up to 10 levels) looking for
137	   `.gsd-t/progress.md`.
138	4. If none: **silent no-op**. The hook must never interrupt a non-GSD-T
139	   Claude Code session.
140	
141	## Contract with the viewer
142	
143	The viewer at `scripts/gsd-t-transcript.html` consumes this producer by:
144	
145	1. Polling `/transcripts` (or `/api/spawns-index`) for spawn-index rows.
146	2. For each row, checking whether `spawnId.startsWith('in-session-')`.
147	3. When true, labeling the left-rail node with `💬 conversation` instead of
148	   the default `▶ spawn`. CSS class `label-in-session` is applied for
149	   optional per-theme styling.
150	
151	No server-side `type` field is required. The filename prefix is the sole
152	discriminator.
153	
154	## Contract with the compact-detector
155	
156	`scripts/gsd-t-compact-detector.js` owns target-selection for the
157	`compact_marker` frame. M45 D2 adds a **fallback** branch:
158	
159	1. Existing behavior (unchanged): pick the most-recently-modified NDJSON.
160	2. New fallback: when no non-`in-session-*` NDJSON has been modified in
161	   the last 30 seconds AND a fresh `in-session-*.ndjson` exists, target
162	   that file instead.
163	3. Log the fallback decision to stderr
164	   (`compact-detector: targeting in-session-{sessionId}.ndjson (fallback)`)
165	   so regressions are visible during debugging.
166	
167	The `compact_marker` frame shape itself is **locked** by
168	`compaction-events-contract.md` v1.1.0 — M45 D2 does not change it.
169	
170	## Guardrails
171	
172	- **Fail-open**: every error is caught, logged to stderr, exit 0. A broken
173	  capture never kills a Claude Code session.
174	- **Append-only**: the hook never truncates or rewrites an existing
175	  in-session NDJSON.
176	- **Content cap**: 16 KB per frame; over-cap writes carry
177	  `"truncated": true`.
178	- **Stdin cap**: 1 MiB defense-in-depth; over-cap drops the write.
179	- **Path-traversal guard**: resolved output path must stay under
180	  `<projectDir>/.gsd-t/transcripts/`.
181	- **Off-switch**: deleting `.gsd-t/` in a project disables the hook for
182	  that project. Removing the entries from `~/.claude/settings.json`
183	  disables it globally.
184	
185	## Consumers (future, not required by this contract)
186	
187	- The dashboard server may one day emit a `type: "in-session"` field on
188	  `/api/spawns-index` rows. When it does, the viewer MAY consume that in
189	  preference to the filename prefix — but the filename prefix remains the
190	  canonical discriminator.
191	- Replay / export tooling can treat `in-session-*.ndjson` frames as
192	  first-class transcript rows alongside spawn-NDJSON frames.
193

input.content:
# Red Team Report — M45 Wave 1 (D1 + D2)

**Date**: 2026-04-22
**Scope**: Commits `9e5d955` (D1 viewer-route-fix) and `e1e1fd2` (D2 in-session-conversation-capture) on `main`.
**Baseline test suite**: 1932/1934 pass (2 pre-existing unrelated fails: `buildEventStreamEntry` and `writer_shim_safe_empty_agent_id_auto_mints_id`).

## BUGS FOUND: 1 (MEDIUM)

### BUG-1 — session_id path-separator lexical-collapse bypasses `in-session-` prefix contract
**Severity**: MEDIUM
**File**: `scripts/hooks/gsd-t-conversation-capture.js:112` (`_appendFrame`)

**Reproduction**:
```
$ mkdir -p /tmp/bug1/proj/.gsd-t && echo "# p" > /tmp/bug1/proj/.gsd-t/progress.md
$ echo '{"hook_event_name":"UserPromptSubmit","session_id":"a/../b","prompt":"x"}' \
    | GSD_T_PROJECT_DIR=/tmp/bug1/proj node scripts/hooks/gsd-t-conversation-capture.js
$ ls /tmp/bug1/proj/.gsd-t/transcripts/
b.ndjson     # <-- file does NOT start with 'in-session-'
```

**Root cause**: `path.join(transcriptsDir, 'in-session-' + sessionId + '.ndjson')` lexically normalizes
`..` sequences inside the session_id. When `sessionId = "a/../b"`, Node's path.join collapses
`in-session-a/../b.ndjson` down to just `b.ndjson` under `transcripts/`. The subsequent
path-traversal guard (`resolvedOut.startsWith(resolvedDir)`) still passes because `b.ndjson`
stays inside `transcripts/`. Exit=0, write succeeds, but the `in-session-` filename-prefix
invariant (the discriminator contract with the viewer AND the compact-detector) is broken.

**Expected**: Either (a) reject malformed `session_id` with path separators / `..` and fall
through to the `pid-{hash}` fallback, or (b) sanitize by replacing `/` and collapsing `..`
before concatenation, or (c) strengthen the guard to also assert
`path.basename(resolvedOut).startsWith('in-session-')`.

**Actual**: File created as `transcripts/b.ndjson` — bypasses both the viewer left-rail
`startsWith('in-session-')` check (won't get the 💬 badge) AND the compact-detector's
`name.indexOf('in-session-') === 0` classification (detector will treat it as a SPAWN NDJSON
and potentially target it preferentially over real in-session files).

**Proof**: Attack reproduced above. With `session_id = "a/../../../ouch"` the guard DOES
correctly block (resolved path escapes transcripts/), so the escape surface is bounded. But
the `a/../b`-style sibling-write-inside-transcripts case is live.

**Impact**: No file-system-escape CVE. The damage is **contract drift**: a malformed/adversarial
Claude Code payload containing path separators in `session_id` silently corrupts the viewer
classification and the compact-detector's target-selection heuristic. The contract explicitly
justifies the `pid-{hash}` fallback as "defense so a malformed payload does not crash the hook
or produce `in-session-.ndjson`" — this bug is the same class (malformed payload) with the same
remedy missing.

**Fix sketch**: Add a one-line sanitizer at the top of `_appendFrame` (or better, at the top
of `_resolveSessionId`):
```js
if (/[\/\\\0]|\.\./.test(sessionId)) {
  sessionId = 'pid-' + crypto.createHash('sha1').update(seed).digest('hex').slice(0, 12);
}
```

## COVERAGE GAPS

1. **No test for session_id sanitization.** The existing "session-id fallback" test only
   covers the missing-field case; no test exercises session_id values containing `/`, `\`,
   `..`, or null bytes.

2. **No end-to-end viewer render test.** `test/m45-d2-transcript-left-rail-in-session.test.js`
   is static-source-code inspection (regex over the HTML file). Nothing actually mounts the
   viewer, feeds it a spawn-index row, and asserts the rendered DOM displays the
   `💬 conversation` label. If a future refactor moved the label-rendering logic but left
   the literal `💬 conversation` string in a comment, this test would still pass.

3. **No contention test for concurrent hooks writing to the same in-session file.** I
   verified concurrently (20 parallel hooks, same session) that `appendFileSync` stays atomic
   on macOS for small writes, but there's no regression test locking this in.

## SHALLOW TESTS (flagged, not rewritten in this pass — D2 is dev-tooling, not user-facing)

- `test/m45-d2-transcript-left-rail-in-session.test.js` (all 5 subtests) — **static source
  regex**. Doesn't actually render the viewer. Replace with a jsdom or Playwright test that
  feeds `{spawnId: 'in-session-foo'}` to `renderTree()` and asserts on the rendered DOM
  (`.label-in-session` element present, text === '💬 conversation').

- `test/transcripts-html-page.test.js` — checks `id="tree"` and `<main id="stream">` are
  present, doesn't verify the viewer actually functions end-to-end with the empty spawn-id.
  This is acceptable as a route-level smoke test though; the unit-level viewer behavior is
  covered elsewhere.

## CONTRACTS VERIFIED: 3/3

1. `conversation-capture-contract.md` v1.0.0 — frames match impl; `message_id`, `truncated`,
   cap-bytes, fallback-session-id, project-dir resolution all verified against code. One
   contract-drift bug (BUG-1 above) around session_id validation.
2. `compaction-events-contract.md` v1.1.0 — `compact_marker` frame shape (`type`, `ts`,
   `source`, `session_id`, `prior_session_id` + optional `trigger`/`preTokens`/`postTokens`)
   matches impl at `scripts/gsd-t-compact-detector.js:231-258`. D2 did not change the shape.
3. `stream-json-sink-contract.md` v1.2.0 — hook is a writer-side consumer; no new entry point
   required, contract not violated.

## ATTACK VECTORS TRIED

- **Path-traversal via session_id** (`../escape`) — guard correctly rejects (exits 0, no write).
- **Lexical path-collapse via session_id** (`a/../b`) — **BUG-1 reproduced**. File lands as
  `transcripts/b.ndjson` (no `in-session-` prefix).
- **Absolute path in session_id** (`/absolute/path`) — ENOENT, no write, exit 0.
- **Null byte in session_id** — Node.js path layer rejects, stderr log, exit 0.
- **Missing `session_id`** — pid-fallback kicks in, file written correctly.
- **Missing `hook_event_name`** — no-op, exit 0, no file.
- **Non-JSON stdin** — no-op, exit 0, no file.
- **Oversize stdin (2 MiB)** — MAX_STDIN guard kills stream, no-op, exit 0.
- **Concurrent writes** (20 parallel UserPromptSubmit, same session_id) — 20/20 lines land,
  all JSON-parseable. `appendFileSync` atomicity holds for small frames.
- **Trailing non-JSON in in-session NDJSON** + compact-detector fallback write — append
  works regardless; detector never parses existing content (correct).
- **`GSD_T_CAPTURE_TOOL_USES=true`** (not `=1`) — strict match, correctly no-op.
- **Missing transcript HTML file** (D1) — returns 404, not 500. Graceful.
- **`Accept: application/json,text/html;q=0.5`** — naïve substring match returns HTML despite
  client preferring JSON. LOW — not a bug in practice; real clients use single-value Accept.
  Flagged as contract-accuracy note, not a bug.
- **Path-traversal in URL query** (`/transcripts?id=../../../etc/passwd`) — query stripped by
  `req.url.split("?")[0]`, no impact.
- **Compact-detector 30s boundary**: at 29.5s spawn wins (correct, `<` comparator).
- **Compact-detector: spawn stale + in-session stale** — in-session wins regardless of its
  own freshness. Consistent with documented "last resort: newest in-session".
- **Compact-detector: no .gsd-t/ at all** — early bail, no crash, no files.
- **Full test suite** — 1932/1934 pass. Both failures pre-date this wave (see baseline).
- **M45 Wave 1 tests** — 27/27 pass.

## VERDICT

`FAIL` (1 bug — MEDIUM)

BUG-1 is a contract-drift defect: an adversarial or malformed `session_id` payload bypasses
the `in-session-` filename-prefix invariant, breaking both the viewer's label discriminator
and the compact-detector's spawn-vs-conversation classification. The remedy is a one-line
sanitizer in `_resolveSessionId` (or a guard in `_appendFrame`). Fix cycle 1 recommended
before GRUDGING PASS.

No CRITICAL or HIGH issues. No new test regressions. D1 is solid.

- `test/transcripts-html-page.test.js` — refactored.
- `test/m45-d1-transcripts-route-viewer.test.js` — new (4 tests).

**D2 commit e1e1fd2** touched:
- `scripts/hooks/gsd-t-conversation-capture.js` — new hook (SessionStart/UserPromptSubmit/Stop + opt-in PostToolUse).
- `scripts/gsd-t-compact-detector.js` — `findActiveTranscript` fallback (in-session NDJSON when spawn NDJSON stale).
- `scripts/gsd-t-transcript.html` — left-rail labels `in-session-*` entries with `💬 conversation` badge.
- `templates/CLAUDE-global.md` — new hook block documentation.
- `.gsd-t/contracts/conversation-capture-contract.md` v1.0.0 — new contract.
- 3 new test files: `test/m45-d2-conversation-capture.test.js`, `test/m45-d2-compact-detector-in-session-fallback.test.js`, `test/m45-d2-transcript-left-rail-in-session.test.js`.

## What to attack (exhaust all categories from the protocol)

Key adversarial angles to probe specifically:

1. **Contract violation** — does `conversation-capture-contract.md` v1.0.0 match the actual hook behavior? Read both, compare field names, frame shape, session-id source, file naming. Any drift is a bug.

2. **Boundary inputs on the hook**:
   - Missing `session_id` in payload — does the hook exit 0 or throw?
   - Missing `hook_event_name` — same.
   - Non-JSON stdin — does the hook exit 0?
   - Prompt containing `../`, null bytes, 1 MB content — does the 16 KB cap actually truncate? Does path-traversal guard actually work?
   - Write contention: two concurrent UserPromptSubmit hooks for the same session — append-only guarantee preserved?

3. **handleTranscriptsList edge cases**:
   - `gsd-t-transcript.html` file missing/unreadable — does the route 500 gracefully or crash?
   - `Accept` header with `text/html,application/json;q=0.9` (browser fallback) — which branch wins?
   - Path traversal in a spawn-id query string — does it reach disk?
   - JSON response shape still `{spawns: [...]}` even with zero transcripts on disk?

4. **Compact-detector fallback**:
   - Directory with stale spawn NDJSON (>30s old) AND stale in-session NDJSON — does it pick either or neither?
   - No transcripts dir at all — graceful or crash?
   - in-session NDJSON with trailing non-JSON line — does fallback writer corrupt it?
   - 30s threshold: what happens at exactly 30s? Off-by-one?

5. **Viewer left-rail label detection**:
   - Spawn-id exactly `in-session-` (no id after) — correctly labeled or missing info?
   - Spawn-id containing `in-session-` as substring but not prefix (e.g., `foo-in-session-bar`) — false-positive label?
   - CSS: does `.label-in-session` rule actually exist in the final HTML, or is only the conditional render present?

6. **Contracts touched**:
   - `stream-json-sink-contract.md` v1.2.0 — does the new hook respect its entry-point contract?
   - `compaction-events-contract.md` v1.1.0 — compact_marker frame shape unchanged?

7. **Regression**: Run the FULL test suite (`npm test`). Baseline is 1932/1934 pass (2 pre-existing unrelated fails documented by both subagents). Any NEW failures are bugs.

8. **E2E functional gaps**: D2 added 20 tests. Read them. Any that only assert existence (`existsSync`, `toBeTruthy`) without asserting on *content* or *behavior*? Those are shallow — flag and rewrite.

## Process

1. Read the protocol (path above).
2. Read the two commits' diffs (`git show 9e5d955`, `git show e1e1fd2`).
3. Read the test files that landed.
4. Attack each category. Reproduce every bug before reporting — `I did X, expected Y, got Z, here's the command`.
5. Run `npm test` once — capture baseline.
6. Write `/Users/david/projects/GSD-T/.gsd-t/red-team-report.md` with findings per the protocol format.
7. If bugs found with severity CRITICAL or HIGH, also append to `/Users/david/projects/GSD-T/.gsd-t/qa-issues.md`.

## Report back

In your return message, concise summary (under 250 words):
- BUGS FOUND: {count} with severity breakdown
- COVERAGE GAPS
- SHALLOW TESTS
- CONTRACTS VERIFIED: {N}/{total}
- ATTACK VECTORS TRIED (one line each)

If VERDICT = FAIL with CRITICAL/HIGH, name each bug so the fix loop can address them. If GRUDGING PASS, list the attack vectors tried so we can audit your thoroughness.

115	  client preferring JSON. LOW — not a bug in practice; real clients use single-value Accept.
116	  Flagged as contract-accuracy note, not a bug.
117	- **Path-traversal in URL query** (`/transcripts?id=../../../etc/passwd`) — query stripped by
118	  `req.url.split("?")[0]`, no impact.
119	- **Compact-detector 30s boundary**: at 29.5s spawn wins (correct, `<` comparator).
120	- **Compact-detector: spawn stale + in-session stale** — in-session wins regardless of its
121	  own freshness. Consistent with documented "last resort: newest in-session".
122	- **Compact-detector: no .gsd-t/ at all** — early bail, no crash, no files.
123	- **Full test suite** — 1932/1934 pass. Both failures pre-date this wave (see baseline).
124	- **M45 Wave 1 tests** — 27/27 pass.
125	
126	## VERDICT
127	
128	`FAIL` (1 bug — MEDIUM)
129	
130	BUG-1 is a contract-drift defect: an adversarial or malformed `session_id` payload bypasses
131	the `in-session-` filename-prefix invariant, breaking both the viewer's label discriminator
132	and the compact-detector's spawn-vs-conversation classification. The remedy is a one-line
133	sanitizer in `_resolveSessionId` (or a guard in `_appendFrame`). Fix cycle 1 recommended
134	before GRUDGING PASS.
135	
136	No CRITICAL or HIGH issues. No new test regressions. D1 is solid.

file.content:
# Red Team Report — M45 Wave 1 (D1 + D2)

**Date**: 2026-04-22
**Scope**: Commits `9e5d955` (D1 viewer-route-fix) and `e1e1fd2` (D2 in-session-conversation-capture) on `main`.
**Baseline test suite**: 1932/1934 pass (2 pre-existing unrelated fails: `buildEventStreamEntry` and `writer_shim_safe_empty_agent_id_auto_mints_id`).

## BUGS FOUND: 1 (MEDIUM)

### BUG-1 — session_id path-separator lexical-collapse bypasses `in-session-` prefix contract
**Severity**: MEDIUM
**File**: `scripts/hooks/gsd-t-conversation-capture.js:112` (`_appendFrame`)

**Reproduction**:
```
$ mkdir -p /tmp/bug1/proj/.gsd-t && echo "# p" > /tmp/bug1/proj/.gsd-t/progress.md
$ echo '{"hook_event_name":"UserPromptSubmit","session_id":"a/../b","prompt":"x"}' \
    | GSD_T_PROJECT_DIR=/tmp/bug1/proj node scripts/hooks/gsd-t-conversation-capture.js
$ ls /tmp/bug1/proj/.gsd-t/transcripts/
b.ndjson     # <-- file does NOT start with 'in-session-'
```

**Root cause**: `path.join(transcriptsDir, 'in-session-' + sessionId + '.ndjson')` lexically normalizes
`..` sequences inside the session_id. When `sessionId = "a/../b"`, Node's path.join collapses
`in-session-a/../b.ndjson` down to just `b.ndjson` under `transcripts/`. The subsequent
path-traversal guard (`resolvedOut.startsWith(resolvedDir)`) still passes because `b.ndjson`
stays inside `transcripts/`. Exit=0, write succeeds, but the `in-session-` filename-prefix
invariant (the discriminator contract with the viewer AND the compact-detector) is broken.

**Expected**: Either (a) reject malformed `session_id` with path separators / `..` and fall
through to the `pid-{hash}` fallback, or (b) sanitize by replacing `/` and collapsing `..`
before concatenation, or (c) strengthen the guard to also assert
`path.basename(resolvedOut).startsWith('in-session-')`.

**Actual**: File created as `transcripts/b.ndjson` — bypasses both the viewer left-rail
`startsWith('in-session-')` check (won't get the 💬 badge) AND the compact-detector's
`name.indexOf('in-session-') === 0` classification (detector will treat it as a SPAWN NDJSON
and potentially target it preferentially over real in-session files).

**Proof**: Attack reproduced above. With `session_id = "a/../../../ouch"` the guard DOES
correctly block (resolved path escapes transcripts/), so the escape surface is bounded. But
the `a/../b`-style sibling-write-inside-transcripts case is live.

**Impact**: No file-system-escape CVE. The damage is **contract drift**: a malformed/adversarial
Claude Code payload containing path separators in `session_id` silently corrupts the viewer
classification and the compact-detector's target-selection heuristic. The contract explicitly
justifies the `pid-{hash}` fallback as "defense so a malformed payload does not crash the hook
or produce `in-session-.ndjson`" — this bug is the same class (malformed payload) with the same
remedy missing.

**Fix sketch**: Add a one-line sanitizer at the top of `_appendFrame` (or better, at the top
of `_resolveSessionId`):
```js
if (/[\/\\\0]|\.\./.test(sessionId)) {
  sessionId = 'pid-' + crypto.createHash('sha1').update(seed).digest('hex').slice(0, 12);
}
```

## COVERAGE GAPS

1. **No test for session_id sanitization.** The existing "session-id fallback" test only
   covers the missing-field case; no test exercises session_id values containing `/`, `\`,
   `..`, or null bytes.

2. **No end-to-end viewer render test.** `test/m45-d2-transcript-left-rail-in-session.test.js`
   is static-source-code inspection (regex over the HTML file). Nothing actually mounts the
   viewer, feeds it a spawn-index row, and asserts the rendered DOM displays the
   `💬 conversation` label. If a future refactor moved the label-rendering logic but left
   the literal `💬 conversation` string in a comment, this test would still pass.

3. **No contention test for concurrent hooks writing to the same in-session file.** I
   verified concurrently (20 parallel hooks, same session) that `appendFileSync` stays atomic
   on macOS for small writes, but there's no regression test locking this in.

## SHALLOW TESTS (flagged, not rewritten in this pass — D2 is dev-tooling, not user-facing)

- `test/m45-d2-transcript-left-rail-in-session.test.js` (all 5 subtests) — **static source
  regex**. Doesn't actually render the viewer. Replace with a jsdom or Playwright test that
  feeds `{spawnId: 'in-session-foo'}` to `renderTree()` and asserts on the rendered DOM
  (`.label-in-session` element present, text === '💬 conversation').

- `test/transcripts-html-page.test.js` — checks `id="tree"` and `<main id="stream">` are
  present, doesn't verify the viewer actually functions end-to-end with the empty spawn-id.
  This is acceptable as a route-level smoke test though; the unit-level viewer behavior is
  covered elsewhere.

## CONTRACTS VERIFIED: 3/3

1. `conversation-capture-contract.md` v1.0.0 — frames match impl; `message_id`, `truncated`,
   cap-bytes, fallback-session-id, project-dir resolution all verified against code. One
   contract-drift bug (BUG-1 above) around session_id validation.
2. `compaction-events-contract.md` v1.1.0 — `compact_marker` frame shape (`type`, `ts`,
   `source`, `session_id`, `prior_session_id` + optional `trigger`/`preTokens`/`postTokens`)
   matches impl at `scripts/gsd-t-compact-detector.js:231-258`. D2 did not change the shape.
3. `stream-json-sink-contract.md` v1.2.0 — hook is a writer-side consumer; no new entry point
   required, contract not violated.

## ATTACK VECTORS TRIED

- **Path-traversal via session_id** (`../escape`) — guard correctly rejects (exits 0, no write).
- **Lexical path-collapse via session_id** (`a/../b`) — **BUG-1 reproduced**. File lands as
  `transcripts/b.ndjson` (no `in-session-` prefix).
- **Absolute path in session_id** (`/absolute/path`) — ENOENT, no write, exit 0.
- **Null byte in session_id** — Node.js path layer rejects, stderr log, exit 0.
- **Missing `session_id`** — pid-fallback kicks in, file written correctly.
- **Missing `hook_event_name`** — no-op, exit 0, no file.
- **Non-JSON stdin** — no-op, exit 0, no file.
- **Oversize stdin (2 MiB)** — MAX_STDIN guard kills stream, no-op, exit 0.
- **Concurrent writes** (20 parallel UserPromptSubmit, same session_id) — 20/20 lines land,
  all JSON-parseable. `appendFileSync` atomicity holds for small frames.
- **Trailing non-JSON in in-session NDJSON** + compact-detector fallback write — append
  works regardless; detector never parses existing content (correct).
- **`GSD_T_CAPTURE_TOOL_USES=true`** (not `=1`) — strict match, correctly no-op.
- **Missing transcript HTML file** (D1) — returns 404, not 500. Graceful.
- **`Accept: application/json,text/html;q=0.5`** — naïve substring match returns HTML despite
  client preferring JSON. LOW — not a bug in practice; real clients use single-value Accept.
  Flagged as contract-accuracy note, not a bug.
- **Path-traversal in URL query** (`/transcripts?id=../../../etc/passwd`) — query stripped by
  `req.url.split("?")[0]`, no impact.
- **Compact-detector 30s boundary**: at 29.5s spawn wins (correct, `<` comparator).
- **Compact-detector: spawn stale + in-session stale** — in-session wins regardless of its
  own freshness. Consistent with documented "last resort: newest in-session".
- **Compact-detector: no .gsd-t/ at all** — early bail, no crash, no files.
- **Full test suite** — 1932/1934 pass. Both failures pre-date this wave (see baseline).
- **M45 Wave 1 tests** — 27/27 pass.

## VERDICT

`FAIL` (1 bug — MEDIUM)

BUG-1 is a contract-drift defect: an adversarial or malformed `session_id` payload bypasses
the `in-session-` filename-prefix invariant, breaking both the viewer's label discriminator
and the compact-detector's spawn-vs-conversation classification. The remedy is a one-line
sanitizer in `_resolveSessionId` (or a guard in `_appendFrame`). Fix cycle 1 recommended
before GRUDGING PASS.

No CRITICAL or HIGH issues. No new test regressions. D1 is solid.

content.text: Applying the one-line sanitizer fix. Let me read the hook file first to place the fix correctly.

lines: function _resolveSessionId(payload) {
-  if (payload && typeof payload.session_id === 'string' && payload.session_id.length > 0) {
+  if (payload && typeof payload.session_id === 'string' && payload.session_id.length > 0
+      && !/[\/\\\0]|\.\./.test(payload.session_id)) {
return payload.session_id;
// Fallback: deterministic-ish per-process hash. Stable within one process,
// different across processes. Keeps the filename non-empty when Claude Code
// omits session_id (shouldn't happen in practice but we must not explode).
+  // Also used as defense-in-depth for malformed session_ids containing path
+  // separators / `..` that would let path.join collapse the `in-session-`
+  // prefix (protects the filename-prefix discriminator contract with the
+  // viewer + compact-detector).
const seed = String(process.pid) + ':' + String(started);
return 'pid-' + crypto.createHash('sha1').update(seed).digest('hex').slice(0, 12);

+    const tdir = path.join(proj, ".gsd-t", "transcripts");
+    const files = fs.readdirSync(tdir);
+    // Every file must carry the `in-session-` prefix (no bare `b.ndjson` escapees).
+    for (const f of files) {
+      assert.ok(f.startsWith("in-session-"),
+        "every transcript must keep the in-session- prefix; got " + f);
+    // And no file is named with the attacker-chosen suffix.
+    assert.ok(!files.includes("b.ndjson"),
+      "bare b.ndjson must never be produced from a malformed session_id");

git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(m45-d2): sanitize session_id against path-separator lexical-collapse

Red Team BUG-1 (MEDIUM): session_id="a/../b" collapsed via path.join to
produce transcripts/b.ndjson without the `in-session-` prefix, silently
bypassing both the viewer left-rail label discriminator and the
compact-detector classification.

Fix: `_resolveSessionId` now rejects session_ids containing `/`, `\`,
`\0`, or `..` and falls through to the pid-hash fallback.

Adds regression test covering 5 attack payloads; all transcripts retain
the in-session- prefix invariant.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

Shipped `bin/gsd-t-economics.cjs` (~260 LOC, zero ext deps): `estimateTaskFootprint({taskNode, mode, projectDir})` returns `{estimatedCwPct, parallelOk, split, workerCount, matchedRows, confidence}` via three-tier corpus lookup (exact command+step+domain triplet → HIGH ≥5 / MEDIUM 1–4; fuzzy domain-only / command-only → LOW; global median → FALLBACK). Corpus cached once per `projectDir` via Map; CW ceiling = 200_000 tokens (mirrors token-budget / context-meter-config / runway-estimator). Mode-aware gates: in-session parallelOk@85%, unattended parallelOk@60% + split>60%. `economics_decision` events appended to `.gsd-t/events/YYYY-MM-DD.jsonl` (best-effort). D6 is a HINT — D2 owns final gate. Contract `.gsd-t/contracts/economics-estimator-contract.md` v0.1.0 skeleton → v1.0.0 with calibration numbers from the live 528-row `token-usage.jsonl` (HIGH MAE 12.89%, MEDIUM 0.00% small-n tautology, LOW 13.08%, FALLBACK 15.06% of CW). Known-failure modes documented: FALLBACK-bias-toward-majority-bucket (5 distinct triplet keys in corpus; 1 HIGH-eligible dominated by `in-session|turn|-` with 523 rows), cache-heavy row inflation (conservative upper bound by design), pre-D7-rows-lack-cw_id, novel task types, tasks inherently > 60% CW (always-sequential). Tests: 9/9 new (`test/m44-economics.test.js`) cover exact/fuzzy/fallback tiers, both mode thresholds under+over boundary, event emission, corpus cache identity, empty-corpus FALLBACK. Doc ripple: `docs/requirements.md` §"M44 Pre-Spawn Economics Estimator" (6 REQs mapped to 5 tasks), `docs/architecture.md` new "Pre-Spawn Economics Estimator (M44 D6)" section with dataflow diagram. User request 2026-04-23: right-side two-layer task panel in dashboard / transcript visualizer (Layer 1 project tasks, Layer 2 active spawn tasks) + per-task token cell on done items. Design fork chosen: explicit plan file (option B), one protocol with three writers (captureSpawn, autoSpawnHeadless, unattended worker resume Step 0) and one reader (dashboard SSE + transcript HTML panel). Plan-file schema at `.gsd-t/spawns/{spawnId}.json` includes `tokens: {in, out, cr, cc, cost_usd}` field per task; post-commit hook writes attribution from `.gsd-t/token-log.md` when status flips to `done`. Token cell format: `in=Nk out=Nk $X.XX` right-aligned monospace; `—` when null (per CLAUDE.md "zero is a measurement, dash is acknowledged gap"). Status icons: `☐ ◐ ✓`, only one `◐` per spawn at a time. Wave assignment: D8 lands in Wave 3 fully parallel-safe with D2 and D3 — disjoint write targets, additive-only edits to 5 existing files (`bin/gsd-t-token-capture.cjs`, `bin/headless-auto-spawn.cjs`, `scripts/gsd-t-dashboard-server.js`, `scripts/gsd-t-transcript.html`, `commands/gsd-t-resume.md`). Hard constraints locked in `constraints.md`: writer derives-never-decides, spawn launches even if writer fails (try/catch at all 3 chokepoints), atomic writes only, post-commit hook silent-fail, no new LLM token cost, no transcript-derivation fallback (per user: no half-measures). 7 tasks (T1..T7) defined. Token cost per spawn: ~0 added (writer is pure I/O on files the spawn was going to read anyway; reader is browser-only; status updater is shell+node). Files added: `.gsd-t/domains/m44-d8-spawn-plan-visibility/{scope,tasks,constraints}.md`. Files edited: `.gsd-t/partition.md` (domains 7→8, Wave 3 row added, Shared Files & Conflict Map updated, Execution Order updated). Ready for Wave 3 execute.

# GSD-T Progress

## Project: GSD-T Framework (@tekyzinc/gsd-t)
## Status: M45 PARTITIONED — Conversation-Stream Observability (test fixture for M44 parallel + unattended)
## Date: 2026-04-23
## Version: 3.18.13

## Current Milestone

**M45 Conversation-Stream Observability** — IN PROGRESS. Target: v3.18.14.

**Goal**: Make the orchestrator session's conversational turns visible in the visualizer transcript stream alongside spawned-agent transcripts, so the user can watch their own dialog (router routing decisions, conversational replies, planning discussions) in the same surface as the spawned work — and so mid-conversation compactions have a transcript to anchor to.

**Why now**: The user can compact mid-conversation while discussing scope/design. M43 D1 already captures in-session token *usage* to `.gsd-t/metrics/token-usage.jsonl`, but the conversation *content* never lands in any transcript NDJSON, so the visualizer renders nothing for the orchestrator session. When the visualizer's "Live Stream" button has no spawn data, it currently falls through to a standalone `/transcripts` index page that doesn't match the user's mental model — they expect the existing left-rail/main/right-panel viewer (`scripts/gsd-t-transcript.html`, M44-D8) at all times.

**Two coupled deliverables (file-disjoint, parallel-eligible)**:

- **D1 — Viewer route fix**: serve `gsd-t-transcript.html` at `/transcripts` (revert standalone `renderTranscriptsHtml` index from v3.18.13). The viewer's left rail handles its own empty state. Files: `scripts/gsd-t-dashboard-server.js`, refactor `test/transcripts-html-page.test.js`.

- **D2 — In-session conversation transcript capture**: new SessionStart/UserPromptSubmit/Stop/PostToolUse hook (`scripts/hooks/gsd-t-conversation-capture.js`) writes `{type: "user_turn"|"assistant_turn"|"tool_use", ts, session_id, content, message_id}` frames to `.gsd-t/transcripts/in-session-{sessionId}.ndjson`. Viewer left-rail (`scripts/gsd-t-transcript.html`) lists in-session sessions distinctly from spawn entries (label "💬 conversation" vs "▶ spawn"). Q2b's `compact_marker` writer (`scripts/gsd-t-compact-detector.js`) extended to write into the active in-session NDJSON when no spawn ndjson is the most-recently-modified, so mid-conversation compactions show up in the right transcript. Settings.json hook block in `templates/CLAUDE-global.md` updated to wire the new hook script.

**Test of M44 parallelism**: file-disjoint; D1 owns server route + test refactor, D2 owns hook + viewer left-rail + compact-detector + template settings. D2 has zero hard dep on D1 (the hook + capture work runs independently); only D2's viewer-left-rail step needs D1's route landed. Per-worker CW estimates expected well under 60% (in-session gate threshold) and well under 60% (unattended gate threshold) — both should provably parallelize. This is also a real exercise of M44 D5 (file-disjointness prover) and D6 (pre-spawn economics).

**Success criteria**:
- `/transcripts` serves the real viewer (left rail + main + right spawn-plan panel) — never raw JSON, never the standalone empty-state page
- After landing, the in-session conversation that produced this very milestone shows up as a transcript entry in the visualizer's left rail, distinct from spawn entries
- A simulated mid-conversation compaction event lands `compact_marker` in the in-session NDJSON (not in some unrelated spawn file)
- Tests: 1914 + new tests all green; manual smoke confirms the visualizer shows the live conversation stream

**Recommended flow**: `partition` (auto — 2 domains, file-disjoint) → `plan` → `execute` (parallel wave) → `verify` → `complete-milestone` → bump 3.18.14.

## Previous Milestone (M44) — Reference

**M44 Cross-Domain & Cross-Task Parallelism (in-session AND unattended)** — COMPLETED (7 proposed domains, scoped in backlog #14). Tagged v3.18.10.

**Goal**: Deliver task-level parallelism to **both** execution modes on equal footing, with mode-aware gating math. Unattended mode adds the harder contract: **zero compaction across an autonomous M1 → M10 run**.

**Mode contracts (NON-NEGOTIABLE)**:
- **[in-session]** Speed + reduce compaction as much as possible. Hard rule: NEVER throw an interactive pause/resume prompt.
- **[unattended]** Run M1 → M10 end-to-end with zero human involvement and **zero compaction**. Per-worker CW headroom is the binding gate.

**Three pre-existing invariants (apply to BOTH modes)**: file-disjointness proof BEFORE spawn · 100% automatic merges · pre-spawn economics check.

**Two delivery layers**: L1 parallel `claude -p` worker spawns (primary lever for both modes) · L2 parallel tasks within one worker (weaker lever, when L1 isn't economic).

**Pre-reqs landed**: Q1 token-log regen (`7eefd2c`) · Q2a compaction detector + scanner (`940e5a8`/`f7de324`) · Q2b compact_marker frame + visualizer badge (`8abe4ef`) · Q3 turn→tool join fix (`8f4588b`) · optimization report generator (`b5edff2`) · adaptive maxParallel (`969462a`).

**Calibration corpus**: 525 turn rows (`.gsd-t/metrics/token-usage.jsonl`) + 72 compaction events (`.gsd-t/metrics/compactions.jsonl`) + per-CW report (`.gsd-t/reports/token-usage-2026-04-22.md`).

**Proposed domains (7)** — full scope in `.gsd-t/backlog.md` #14:
- D1 — Generic task-graph reader (parses domain tasks + cross-domain deps → DAG)
- D2 — `gsd-t parallel` CLI (subcommand wrapping M40 orchestrator with task-level parallelism, `--mode` aware)
- D3 — Command-file integration (`execute`/`wave`/`quick`/`debug`/`integrate` consume task-graph)
- D4 — Dep-graph validation (refuses fan-out when deps unmet)
- D5 — File-disjointness prover (walks expected-touch lists, sequential fallback if unprovable)
- D6 — Pre-spawn economics + cost estimator (queries token-usage.jsonl for similar-task cost, mode-aware gating)
- D7 — Per-CW token attribution + compaction-event integration (`cw_id` tagging + estimator calibration loop)

**Success criteria**:
- [in-session] `gsd-t-execute` previously taking T min serially completes in ≤ T/2 min with N workers, zero pause/resume prompts
- [unattended] M1 → M5 minimum (ideally M1 → M10) runs end-to-end with **zero compaction events** during the autonomous chain

**Recommended flow**: `partition` → `discuss` (D1 task-graph schema + D6 estimator algorithm need design) → `plan` → `execute` (waves) → `integrate` → `verify` → `complete-milestone` → tag v3.18.10.

**Previous milestone (M43)** completed 2026-04-21, v3.17.10 tagged. Reference DEFINED block preserved in `.gsd-t/milestones/` archive.

**Goal**: Make every token attributable to a specific tool / command / domain, and lock the framework to a single rule: **the in-session channel is reserved for human↔Claude dialog. All tool-using work spawns. The visualizer is the watching surface.** No flags, no thresholds, no opt-outs — there is no "in-session mode" for commands to enter.

**Rationale (revised 2026-04-21)**: The original framing ("headless when pressure is high, in-session otherwise") is wrong. A circuit breaker only makes sense when the default is "in-session, sometimes headless." Once the default is "always headless, dialog-only stays in-session," there is nothing to trip — the spawn already happened. The dialog channel exists for *thinking together*; the visualizer (`:7433`) exists for *watching the doing*. Conflating them is what produced the bee-poc compaction pathology (agent `b94ca63f`, 3 compactions in one workday). The fix isn't smarter routing — it's a clean separation of channels.

**Scope — Part A: Universal Token Attribution**
- **D1 per-turn in-session usage capture** — Capture token usage for every turn of the dialog channel (the only thing still running in-session under this model). Hook-based if Claude Code exposes `usage` on `Stop` / `SessionEnd` (probe shipped `f94857b`, branch decision pending); otherwise extend the M42 transcript tee to interactive sessions and parse the same stream-json `result` frame the M40 D4 aggregator already understands. Output: per-turn rows into `.gsd-t/metrics/token-usage.jsonl` with `turn_id`, `session_id`, `command`, `model`, `usage{...}`. Headless spawns are already covered by M41.
- **D2 per-tool attribution** — `bin/gsd-t-tool-attribution.cjs` joins the events stream (`.gsd-t/events/*.jsonl`, which carries every tool-call request/response with `turn_id`) against the per-turn usage JSONL from D1, then attributes the turn's usage across the turn's tool_calls by output-byte ratio. Ships `gsd-t tool-cost --group-by tool|command|domain [--since YYYY-MM-DD] [--milestone Mxx] [--format table|json]`. Under the always-headless model this matters *more*, not less, because spawned work is now the dominant cost and operators need to slice it.
- **D3 sink unification + backfill** — Define one canonical token-row schema (extending M40 D4 schema v1) and route every producer through it: M36 heartbeat, M40 aggregator, D1 dialog capture, D2 joiner. `.gsd-t/token-log.md` becomes a *regenerated view* (`gsd-t tokens --regenerate-log`), not a hand-maintained markdown table. Backfill the existing 64 `Tokens=0` rows from `.gsd-t/events/*.jsonl` + `.gsd-t/headless-*.log` (M41 D3 backfiller existed but was never run against the historical log).

**Scope — Part B: Always-Headless Inversion (revised — channel separation, not threshold flip)**
- **D4 always-headless command spawn (no flag, no threshold)** — Strip the "headless when over context band" logic from ~14 command files (`execute`, `wave`, `integrate`, `quick`, `debug`, `verify`, `complete-milestone`, `test-sync`, `scan`, `gap-analysis`, `populate`, `feature`, `project`, `partition`). Every command spawns. Period. **No `--in-session` opt-out, no `--headless` flag, no low-water mark, no context-meter-driven branching at spawn time.** The only thing that runs in the dialog channel is the `/gsd` router itself, which always spawns the actual command. Removes ~3 layers of conditional routing logic and the cognitive load of "which mode is this command in right now."
- **D5 dialog-channel meter (router-only, replaces the deleted compaction-pressure circuit breaker)** — Originally scoped as a turn-over-turn circuit breaker that would refuse to start in-session commands when growth predicted `/compact`. Under the new channel model there are no in-session commands to refuse. **D5 collapses to a much smaller utility**: a turn-over-turn growth meter on the router itself (since the router is what stays in-session). When dialog growth predicts `/compact` within N turns, surface a one-line warning so the user can `/clear` or wrap the conversation deliberately. Pure read/warn — never refuses, never reroutes (there's nothing to reroute to). Lives in `bin/runway-estimator.cjs` as a small extension, not a new module.
- **D6 transcript viewer as the primary surface (not a hint)** — Promote the M42 `:7433` transcript viewer from "useful for unattended runs" / "URL hint alongside in-session option" to **the** surface. Every spawn prints `view at http://127.0.0.1:7433/transcript/{spawn-id}` because that's where the work lives now. The dashboard auto-launches on first command spawn if not already running (port resolved via the project-hashed default from the multi-project isolation quick `df34eb2`). No `--in-session` print path because there is no `--in-session` mode.

**Tradeoff acknowledged**: Spawning even tiny work (a single Read, one Bash) has overhead — orchestrator startup, transcript file, dashboard registration. Worth it for clean channel separation and uniform observability. If overhead bites, the answer is to make the spawn faster (M40-style fast spawn), not to make a threshold smarter.

**Out of scope (deferred)**:
- Intervene / SIGSTOP-inject (already deferred from M42).
- Migrating away from `usage`-bearing streams if Claude Code stops emitting them (separate forensic milestone if the contract breaks).
- Auto-`/clear` on dialog meter trip — D5 only warns; the user decides when to wrap the dialog.

**Contracts touched**:
- `metrics-schema-contract.md` v1 → v2 (adds `turn_id`, `session_id`, `tool_attribution[]`; **removes** `compaction_pressure` from the original v2 sketch — D5 collapses, no schema field needed)
- `stream-json-sink-contract.md` v1.1.0 → v1.2.0 (formalize the dialog-channel hook entry-point alongside the existing headless-spawn entry-point)
- New: `tool-attribution-contract.md` (output-byte ratio algorithm, tie-breaker rules, missing-data handling)
- `headless-default-contract.md` v1.0.0 → v2.0.0 (always-headless rule; **delete** `--in-session` opt-out + low-water mark from the v1 spec; document the channel-separation invariant: dialog = router only, everything else spawns)

**Success criteria**:
1. Every spawn writes a per-turn row to `.gsd-t/metrics/token-usage.jsonl` with usage parsed (never `0`, never `N/A` — `—` only when the upstream envelope itself is missing).
2. `gsd-t tool-cost --group-by tool` shows non-zero attribution for at least Bash, Read, Edit, Grep, Task across the last 30 days of repo activity (after backfill).
3. `.gsd-t/token-log.md` is fully regeneratable from `.gsd-t/metrics/token-usage.jsonl` (delete + regenerate produces a byte-identical file modulo timestamp ordering).
4. **No command file accepts `--in-session` or `--headless` flags.** Default invocation of any of the 14 listed commands spawns. Grep for `--in-session` in `commands/*.md` returns zero matches after D4.
5. Dashboard URL is printed at every spawn; visiting it shows the live transcript without operator action beyond clicking the link.
6. D5 dialog meter fires a one-line warning on a synthetic high-growth fixture in the router context; never refuses, never reroutes.

**Assessment**: MEDIUM (was COMPLEX — D5 collapse and D4 simplification each removed roughly half their original scope). Recommended flow: `partition` → `discuss` (only D1 hook-vs-tee branch needs discussion now; D5 heuristic is no longer in scope) → `plan` → `execute` (waves) → `integrate` → `verify` → `complete-milestone` → tag v3.17.10.

Root cause: when the hook is `require()`d by a test file (as `test/m44-cw-attribution.test.js` does to exercise the `handle()` export directly), the module-level `process.stdin.setEncoding('utf8')` + `stdin.on('data'/'end')` listeners attach to the *test process's* stdin and keep the Node event loop alive indefinitely. All 19 D7 assertions pass in ~400ms isolated, but in the `npm test` full-suite run the file hangs on the unclosed stdin handle until the test runner kills it at its internal timeout (54885ms observed), which flagged the file as `✖` even though every sub-test was green. The `require.main === module` guard is the standard Node pattern for "only run top-level code when invoked as a script" and is functionally invisible in production (Claude Code invokes hooks via `node scripts/…` = `require.main === module` is true). After fix: 69/69 pass across D1 + D7 + m41-token-capture + compact-detector in 566ms combined. **Wave 1 gate status**: D1 DAG + D7 `cw_id` field + calibration hook all land; `.gsd-t/metrics/token-usage.jsonl` new rows carry `cw_id` when supplied; `.gsd-t/metrics/compactions.jsonl` can now receive `compaction_post_spawn` calibration rows. Known-failure modes documented: FALLBACK-bias-toward-majority-bucket (5 distinct triplet keys in corpus; 1 HIGH-eligible dominated by `in-session|turn|-` with 523 rows), cache-heavy row inflation (conservative upper bound by design), pre-D7-rows-lack-cw_id, novel task types, tasks inherently > 60% CW (always-sequential). Tests: 9/9 new (`test/m44-economics.test.js`) cover exact/fuzzy/fallback tiers, both mode thresholds under+over boundary, event emission, corpus cache identity, empty-corpus FALLBACK. Doc ripple: `docs/requirements.md` §"M44 Pre-Spawn Economics Estimator" (6 REQs mapped to 5 tasks), `docs/architecture.md` new "Pre-Spawn Economics Estimator (M44 D6)" section with dataflow diagram. Wave 2 D6 gate met: real-corpus smoke returns HIGH confidence + 57.4% CwPct for `in-session|turn|-` triplet, FALLBACK 57.2% for unknown triplets. Full repo suite: 1846/1850 (+9 from D6; 4 pre-existing unrelated fails unchanged: event-stream:341, m43-dashboard-autostart:95, m43-milestone-complete-detection:137, watch-progress-writer:222). D4 and D5 committing in parallel — D6 touched only its owned files + these three ripple docs.

- 2026-04-23 00:25: [graft M44 · D8 spawn-plan-visibility added to partition] New domain M44-D8 grafted into M44 in flight. User request 2026-04-23: right-side two-layer task panel in dashboard / transcript visualizer (Layer 1 project tasks, Layer 2 active spawn tasks) + per-task token cell on done items. Design fork chosen: explicit plan file (option B), one protocol with three writers (captureSpawn, autoSpawnHeadless, unattended worker resume Step 0) and one reader (dashboard SSE + transcript HTML panel). Plan-file schema at `.gsd-t/spawns/{spawnId}.json` includes `tokens: {in, out, cr, cc, cost_usd}` field per task; post-commit hook writes attribution from `.gsd-t/token-log.md` when status flips to `done`. Status icons: `☐ ◐ ✓`, only one `◐` per spawn at a time. Wave assignment: D8 lands in Wave 3 fully parallel-safe with D2 and D3 — disjoint write targets, additive-only edits to 5 existing files (`bin/gsd-t-token-capture.cjs`, `bin/headless-auto-spawn.cjs`, `scripts/gsd-t-dashboard-server.js`, `scripts/gsd-t-transcript.html`, `commands/gsd-t-resume.md`). Hard constraints locked in `constraints.md`: writer derives-never-decides, spawn launches even if writer fails (try/catch at all 3 chokepoints), atomic writes only, post-commit hook silent-fail, no new LLM token cost, no transcript-derivation fallback (per user: no half-measures). 7 tasks (T1..T7) defined.
