# GSD-T Progress

## Project: GSD-T Framework (@tekyzinc/gsd-t)
## Status: M45 COMPLETE — Conversation-Stream Observability (v3.18.14)
## Date: 2026-04-23
## Version: 3.18.14

## Current Milestone

**M45 Conversation-Stream Observability** — COMPLETE 2026-04-23. Shipped v3.18.14. See Completed Milestones below.

**Goal**: Make the orchestrator session's conversational turns visible in the visualizer transcript stream alongside spawned-agent transcripts, so the user can watch their own dialog (router routing decisions, conversational replies, planning discussions) in the same surface as the spawned work — and so mid-conversation compactions have a transcript to anchor to.

**Why now**: The user can compact mid-conversation while discussing scope/design. M43 D1 already captures in-session token *usage* to `.gsd-t/metrics/token-usage.jsonl`, but the conversation *content* never lands in any transcript NDJSON, so the visualizer renders nothing for the orchestrator session. When the visualizer's "Live Stream" button has no spawn data, it currently falls through to a standalone `/transcripts` index page that doesn't match the user's mental model — they expect the existing left-rail/main/right-panel viewer (`scripts/gsd-t-transcript.html`, M44-D8) at all times.

**Two coupled deliverables (file-disjoint, parallel-eligible)**:

- **D1 — Viewer route fix**: serve `gsd-t-transcript.html` at `/transcripts` (revert standalone `renderTranscriptsHtml` index from v3.18.13). The viewer's left rail handles its own empty state. Files: `scripts/gsd-t-dashboard-server.js`, refactor `test/transcripts-html-page.test.js`.

- **D2 — In-session conversation transcript capture**: new SessionStart/UserPromptSubmit/Stop/PostToolUse hook (`scripts/hooks/gsd-t-conversation-capture.js`) writes `{type: "user_turn"|"assistant_turn"|"tool_use", ts, session_id, content, message_id}` frames to `.gsd-t/transcripts/in-session-{sessionId}.ndjson`. Viewer left-rail (`scripts/gsd-t-transcript.html`) lists in-session sessions distinctly from spawn entries (label "💬 conversation" vs "▶ spawn"). Q2b's `compact_marker` writer (`scripts/gsd-t-compact-detector.js`) extended to write into the active in-session NDJSON when no spawn ndjson is the most-recently-modified, so mid-conversation compactions show up in the right transcript. Settings.json hook block in `templates/CLAUDE-global.md` updated to wire the new hook script.

**Test of M44 parallelism**: file-disjoint; D1 owns server route + test refactor, D2 owns hook + viewer left-rail + compact-detector + template settings. D2 has zero hard dep on D1 (the hook + capture work runs independently); only D2's viewer-left-rail step needs D1's route landed. Per-worker CW estimates expected well under 60% (in-session gate threshold) and well under 60% (unattended gate threshold) — both should provably parallelize. This is also a real exercise of M44 D5 (file-disjointness prover) and D6 (pre-spawn economics).

**Success criteria**:
- `/transcripts` serves the real viewer (left rail + main + right spawn-plan panel) — never raw JSON, never the standalone empty-state page
- After landing, the in-session conversation that produced this very milestone shows up as a transcript entry in the visualizer's left rail, distinct from spawn entries
- A simulated mid-conversation compaction event lands `compact_marker` in the in-session NDJSON (not in some unrelated spawn file)
- Tests: 1914 + new tests all green; manual smoke confirms the visualizer shows the live conversation stream

**Recommended flow**: `partition` (auto — 2 domains, file-disjoint) → `plan` → `execute` (parallel wave) → `verify` → `complete-milestone` → bump 3.18.14.

**Previous milestone (M44)** completed 2026-04-23, v3.18.10 → v3.18.13 patches (button fix + content negotiation). Reference DEFINED block preserved below.

---

## Previous Milestone (M44) — Reference

**M44 Cross-Domain & Cross-Task Parallelism (in-session AND unattended)** — COMPLETED (7 proposed domains, scoped in backlog #14). Tagged v3.18.10.

**Goal**: Deliver task-level parallelism to **both** execution modes on equal footing, with mode-aware gating math. Unattended mode adds the harder contract: **zero compaction across an autonomous M1 → M10 run**.

**Mode contracts (NON-NEGOTIABLE)**:
- **[in-session]** Speed + reduce compaction as much as possible. Hard rule: NEVER throw an interactive pause/resume prompt.
- **[unattended]** Run M1 → M10 end-to-end with zero human involvement and **zero compaction**. Per-worker CW headroom is the binding gate.

**Three pre-existing invariants (apply to BOTH modes)**: file-disjointness proof BEFORE spawn · 100% automatic merges · pre-spawn economics check.

**Two delivery layers**: L1 parallel `claude -p` worker spawns (primary lever for both modes) · L2 parallel tasks within one worker (weaker lever, when L1 isn't economic).

**Pre-reqs landed**: Q1 token-log regen (`7eefd2c`) · Q2a compaction detector + scanner (`940e5a8`/`f7de324`) · Q2b compact_marker frame + visualizer badge (`8abe4ef`) · Q3 turn→tool join fix (`8f4588b`) · optimization report generator (`b5edff2`) · adaptive maxParallel (`969462a`).

**Calibration corpus**: 525 turn rows (`.gsd-t/metrics/token-usage.jsonl`) + 72 compaction events (`.gsd-t/metrics/compactions.jsonl`) + per-CW report (`.gsd-t/reports/token-usage-2026-04-22.md`).

**Proposed domains (7)** — full scope in `.gsd-t/backlog.md` #14:
- D1 — Generic task-graph reader (parses domain tasks + cross-domain deps → DAG)
- D2 — `gsd-t parallel` CLI (subcommand wrapping M40 orchestrator with task-level parallelism, `--mode` aware)
- D3 — Command-file integration (`execute`/`wave`/`quick`/`debug`/`integrate` consume task-graph)
- D4 — Dep-graph validation (refuses fan-out when deps unmet)
- D5 — File-disjointness prover (walks expected-touch lists, sequential fallback if unprovable)
- D6 — Pre-spawn economics + cost estimator (queries token-usage.jsonl for similar-task cost, mode-aware gating)
- D7 — Per-CW token attribution + compaction-event integration (`cw_id` tagging + estimator calibration loop)

**Success criteria**:
- [in-session] `gsd-t-execute` previously taking T min serially completes in ≤ T/2 min with N workers, zero pause/resume prompts
- [unattended] M1 → M5 minimum (ideally M1 → M10) runs end-to-end with **zero compaction events** during the autonomous chain

**Recommended flow**: `partition` → `discuss` (D1 task-graph schema + D6 estimator algorithm need design) → `plan` → `execute` (waves) → `integrate` → `verify` → `complete-milestone` → tag v3.18.10.

**Previous milestone (M43)** completed 2026-04-21, v3.17.10 tagged. Reference DEFINED block preserved in `.gsd-t/milestones/` archive.

**Goal**: Make every token attributable to a specific tool / command / domain, and lock the framework to a single rule: **the in-session channel is reserved for human↔Claude dialog. All tool-using work spawns. The visualizer is the watching surface.** No flags, no thresholds, no opt-outs — there is no "in-session mode" for commands to enter.

**Rationale (revised 2026-04-21)**: The original framing ("headless when pressure is high, in-session otherwise") is wrong. A circuit breaker only makes sense when the default is "in-session, sometimes headless." Once the default is "always headless, dialog-only stays in-session," there is nothing to trip — the spawn already happened. The dialog channel exists for *thinking together*; the visualizer (`:7433`) exists for *watching the doing*. Conflating them is what produced the bee-poc compaction pathology (agent `b94ca63f`, 3 compactions in one workday). The fix isn't smarter routing — it's a clean separation of channels.

**Scope — Part A: Universal Token Attribution**
- **D1 per-turn in-session usage capture** — Capture token usage for every turn of the dialog channel (the only thing still running in-session under this model). Hook-based if Claude Code exposes `usage` on `Stop` / `SessionEnd` (probe shipped `f94857b`, branch decision pending); otherwise extend the M42 transcript tee to interactive sessions and parse the same stream-json `result` frame the M40 D4 aggregator already understands. Output: per-turn rows into `.gsd-t/metrics/token-usage.jsonl` with `turn_id`, `session_id`, `command`, `model`, `usage{...}`. Headless spawns are already covered by M41.
- **D2 per-tool attribution** — `bin/gsd-t-tool-attribution.cjs` joins the events stream (`.gsd-t/events/*.jsonl`, which carries every tool-call request/response with `turn_id`) against the per-turn usage JSONL from D1, then attributes the turn's usage across the turn's tool_calls by output-byte ratio. Ships `gsd-t tool-cost --group-by tool|command|domain [--since YYYY-MM-DD] [--milestone Mxx] [--format table|json]`. Under the always-headless model this matters *more*, not less, because spawned work is now the dominant cost and operators need to slice it.
- **D3 sink unification + backfill** — Define one canonical token-row schema (extending M40 D4 schema v1) and route every producer through it: M36 heartbeat, M40 aggregator, D1 dialog capture, D2 joiner. `.gsd-t/token-log.md` becomes a *regenerated view* (`gsd-t tokens --regenerate-log`), not a hand-maintained markdown table. Backfill the existing 64 `Tokens=0` rows from `.gsd-t/events/*.jsonl` + `.gsd-t/headless-*.log` (M41 D3 backfiller existed but was never run against the historical log).

**Scope — Part B: Always-Headless Inversion (revised — channel separation, not threshold flip)**
- **D4 always-headless command spawn (no flag, no threshold)** — Strip the "headless when over context band" logic from ~14 command files (`execute`, `wave`, `integrate`, `quick`, `debug`, `verify`, `complete-milestone`, `test-sync`, `scan`, `gap-analysis`, `populate`, `feature`, `project`, `partition`). Every command spawns. Period. **No `--in-session` opt-out, no `--headless` flag, no low-water mark, no context-meter-driven branching at spawn time.** The only thing that runs in the dialog channel is the `/gsd` router itself, which always spawns the actual command. Removes ~3 layers of conditional routing logic and the cognitive load of "which mode is this command in right now."
- **D5 dialog-channel meter (router-only, replaces the deleted compaction-pressure circuit breaker)** — Originally scoped as a turn-over-turn circuit breaker that would refuse to start in-session commands when growth predicted `/compact`. Under the new channel model there are no in-session commands to refuse. **D5 collapses to a much smaller utility**: a turn-over-turn growth meter on the router itself (since the router is what stays in-session). When dialog growth predicts `/compact` within N turns, surface a one-line warning so the user can `/clear` or wrap the conversation deliberately. Pure read/warn — never refuses, never reroutes (there's nothing to reroute to). Lives in `bin/runway-estimator.cjs` as a small extension, not a new module.
- **D6 transcript viewer as the primary surface (not a hint)** — Promote the M42 `:7433` transcript viewer from "useful for unattended runs" / "URL hint alongside in-session option" to **the** surface. Every spawn prints `view at http://127.0.0.1:7433/transcript/{spawn-id}` because that's where the work lives now. The dashboard auto-launches on first command spawn if not already running (port resolved via the project-hashed default from the multi-project isolation quick `df34eb2`). No `--in-session` print path because there is no `--in-session` mode.

**Tradeoff acknowledged**: Spawning even tiny work (a single Read, one Bash) has overhead — orchestrator startup, transcript file, dashboard registration. Worth it for clean channel separation and uniform observability. If overhead bites, the answer is to make the spawn faster (M40-style fast spawn), not to make a threshold smarter.

**Out of scope (deferred)**:
- Intervene / SIGSTOP-inject (already deferred from M42).
- Migrating away from `usage`-bearing streams if Claude Code stops emitting them (separate forensic milestone if the contract breaks).
- Auto-`/clear` on dialog meter trip — D5 only warns; the user decides when to wrap the dialog.

**Contracts touched**:
- `metrics-schema-contract.md` v1 → v2 (adds `turn_id`, `session_id`, `tool_attribution[]`; **removes** `compaction_pressure` from the original v2 sketch — D5 collapses, no schema field needed)
- `stream-json-sink-contract.md` v1.1.0 → v1.2.0 (formalize the dialog-channel hook entry-point alongside the existing headless-spawn entry-point)
- New: `tool-attribution-contract.md` (output-byte ratio algorithm, tie-breaker rules, missing-data handling)
- `headless-default-contract.md` v1.0.0 → v2.0.0 (always-headless rule; **delete** `--in-session` opt-out + low-water mark from the v1 spec; document the channel-separation invariant: dialog = router only, everything else spawns)

**Success criteria**:
1. Every spawn writes a per-turn row to `.gsd-t/metrics/token-usage.jsonl` with usage parsed (never `0`, never `N/A` — `—` only when the upstream envelope itself is missing).
2. `gsd-t tool-cost --group-by tool` shows non-zero attribution for at least Bash, Read, Edit, Grep, Task across the last 30 days of repo activity (after backfill).
3. `.gsd-t/token-log.md` is fully regeneratable from `.gsd-t/metrics/token-usage.jsonl` (delete + regenerate produces a byte-identical file modulo timestamp ordering).
4. **No command file accepts `--in-session` or `--headless` flags.** Default invocation of any of the 14 listed commands spawns. Grep for `--in-session` in `commands/*.md` returns zero matches after D4.
5. Dashboard URL is printed at every spawn; visiting it shows the live transcript without operator action beyond clicking the link.
6. D5 dialog meter fires a one-line warning on a synthetic high-growth fixture in the router context; never refuses, never reroutes.

**Assessment**: MEDIUM (was COMPLEX — D5 collapse and D4 simplification each removed roughly half their original scope). Recommended flow: `partition` → `discuss` (only D1 hook-vs-tee branch needs discussion now; D5 heuristic is no longer in scope) → `plan` → `execute` (waves) → `integrate` → `verify` → `complete-milestone` → tag v3.17.10.

Root cause: when the hook is `require()`d by a test file (as `test/m44-cw-attribution.test.js` does to exercise the `handle()` export directly), the module-level `process.stdin.setEncoding('utf8')` + `stdin.on('data'/'end')` listeners attach to the *test process's* stdin and keep the Node event loop alive indefinitely. All 19 D7 assertions pass in ~400ms isolated, but in the `npm test` full-suite run the file hangs on the unclosed stdin handle until the test runner kills it at its internal timeout (54885ms observed), which flagged the file as `✖` even though every sub-test was green. The `require.main === module` guard is the standard Node pattern for "only run top-level code when invoked as a script" and is functionally invisible in production (Claude Code invokes hooks via `node scripts/…` = `require.main === module` is true). After fix: 69/69 pass across D1 + D7 + m41-token-capture + compact-detector in 566ms combined. **Wave 1 gate status**: D1 DAG + D7 `cw_id` field + calibration hook all land; `.gsd-t/metrics/token-usage.jsonl` new rows carry `cw_id` when supplied; `.gsd-t/metrics/compactions.jsonl` can now receive `compaction_post_spawn` calibration rows. Objectives per mode: [in-session] speed + minimize-compaction (hard rule: NEVER throw interactive pause/resume prompts); [unattended] zero compaction across autonomous M1→M10 run. Three pre-existing invariants (file-disjointness, 100% auto-merges, pre-spawn economics) apply to both modes identically — they're correctness, not mode. Mode-specific gating math: [in-session] orchestrator-CW headroom (worker summaries land in user-facing CW; bound fan-out width AND result-envelope size) vs [unattended] per-worker-CW headroom (supervisor is Node process with no CW; bind per-`claude -p` worker CW ceiling). Two delivery layers (L1: parallel `claude -p` worker spawns, L2: parallel tasks within one worker) are mode-agnostic. **Token-cost honesty in the entry**: parallel is approximately token-neutral in steady state (total input/output same, wall-clock is the primary win; `max` instead of `sum`), and token-positive only in the case where sequential would have compacted (compaction-induced re-input drops to zero). Rate-limit pressure is explicitly NOT a con — hitting the 5-hour ceiling because 20× more work got done is the goal. 7 proposed domains (D1 task-graph reader, D2 `gsd-t parallel` CLI, D3 command-file integration, D4 dep-graph validation, D5 file-disjointness prover, D6 pre-spawn economics + cost estimator consuming the 525-row calibration corpus, D7 per-CW attribution + compaction-event integration). Pre-reqs already landed this session: Q1 token-log regen (`7eefd2c`), Q2a compaction detector + scanner (`940e5a8` + `f7de324`), Q3 turn→tool join (`8f4588b`), optimization report (`b5edff2`), adaptive maxParallel (`969462a`). Companion memory `feedback_parallelism_two_modes.md` written; prior `feedback_unattended_overnight_only.md` superseded — time-of-day framing retired, mode is now chosen by *intent* (interrupt-ability vs zero-touch autonomy) not clock. User framing that drove this: "In session, there's two clear objectives, speed and reduce compaction as much as is possible" + "Unattended must run M1 → M10 end-to-end with zero human involvement and zero compaction. This is critical, but not for all builds. Many I will want to run in session because I don't have all the requirements defined or I want to be able to interrupt." Success criteria: [in-session] T/2 wall-clock reduction with N workers and zero pause prompts; [unattended] zero compaction events logged during a measured autonomous chain (baseline: 72 compactions over 18 days of mixed-mode work from Q2a backfill).

Wave assignment: D8 lands in Wave 3 fully parallel-safe with D2 and D3 — disjoint write targets, additive-only edits to 5 existing files (`bin/gsd-t-token-capture.cjs`, `bin/headless-auto-spawn.cjs`, `scripts/gsd-t-dashboard-server.js`, `scripts/gsd-t-transcript.html`, `commands/gsd-t-resume.md`). Hard constraints locked in `constraints.md`: writer derives-never-decides, spawn launches even if writer fails (try/catch at all 3 chokepoints), atomic writes only, post-commit hook silent-fail, no new LLM token cost, no transcript-derivation fallback (per user: no half-measures). 7 tasks (T1..T7) defined. Token cost per spawn: ~0 added (writer is pure I/O on files the spawn was going to read anyway; reader is browser-only; status updater is shell+node). Files added: `.gsd-t/domains/m44-d8-spawn-plan-visibility/{scope,tasks,constraints}.md`. Files edited: `.gsd-t/partition.md` (domains 7→8, Wave 3 row added, Shared Files & Conflict Map updated, Execution Order updated). Ready for Wave 3 execute.

- 2026-04-23 00:50: [graft M44 · D9 parallelism-observability added to partition] New domain M44-D9 grafted into M44. User request 2026-04-23: third dashboard panel showing live parallelism state (active workers, ready tasks, parallelism factor, gate decision tally) with color-coded health (green/yellow/red) — RED when orchestrator failing to fan out OR wave parallelism factor < 50% of D6 estimate. Full Report button dumps markdown post-mortem (per-spawn table, per-gate decisions, Gantt-ish per-worker timeline, token cost vs. D6 estimate). Wave assignment: D9 lands in Wave 3, parallel-safe with D2/D3/D8 — sequenced AFTER D8-T1 (reader requires spawn-plan files to exist). 4 tasks (T1 module+contract, T2 dashboard endpoints, T3 transcript renderer panel, T4 tests+doc ripple). Hard constraints: reader-never-writer, silent-fail on malformed inputs, no new LLM token cost, dashboard endpoints additive (disjoint from D8's /api/spawn-plans), Stop Supervisor button reuses existing /gsd-t-unattended-stop. Token cost per spawn: ~0 added (pure file I/O). Files added: `.gsd-t/domains/m44-d9-parallelism-observability/{scope,tasks,constraints}.md`. Files edited: `.gsd-t/partition.md` (domains 8→9, Wave 3 row, Shared Files & Conflict Map split for `scripts/gsd-t-dashboard-server.js` + `scripts/gsd-t-transcript.html`, new entries for `commands/gsd-t-help.md` and `docs/architecture.md`). Stop sentinel created to drain supervisor before relaunch with D9 in scope.

- 2026-04-23 17:43: [M44-D2-T5] parallel-cli doc ripple — requirements/architecture/README/GSD-T-README updated; Wave 3 D2 gate met. `docs/requirements.md` §"M44 Parallel CLI" lists 10 REQs (REQ-M44-D2-01..10) covering runParallel surface, orchestrator-config extension (`computeInSessionHeadroom`, `computeUnattendedGate`, `IN_SESSION_CW_CEILING_PCT=85`, `UNATTENDED_PER_WORKER_CW_PCT=60`, `DEFAULT_SUMMARY_SIZE_PCT=4`), three-gate wiring (D4→D5→D6), in-session reduction to N=1 floor (never refuses), unattended task_split on >60% per-worker CW, mode selection (`--mode` flag > `GSD_T_UNATTENDED=1` env > default in-session), `--dry-run` 6-col plan table format, and the `wave-join-contract.md` v1.1.0 bump. `docs/architecture.md` §"Parallel CLI (M44 D2, v3.18.10+)" adds the dataflow diagram (D1 buildTaskGraph → D4 validateDepGraph → D5 proveDisjointness → D6 estimateTaskFootprint × N → mode-aware gating math → plan), mode-contract table, event schemas (`gate_veto`, `parallelism_reduced`, `task_split`), and hard invariants (zero ext deps; M40 orchestrator never replaced; 3 pre-existing invariants apply to BOTH modes; in-session never refuses — N=1 floor; adaptive maxParallel untouched). `README.md` adds `gsd-t parallel` block under Headless Mode with 5 usage examples. `docs/GSD-T-README.md` adds "Parallel CLI (M44)" section with flags table, three upstream gates list, and mode-aware final gate table. Wave 3 D2 gate verified: 21/21 tests pass in `test/m44-parallel-cli.test.js`; full repo suite 1868/1871 (3 pre-existing unrelated fails: event-stream:341, m43-milestone-complete-detection:137, watch-progress-writer:222). D3 can now begin. D2 commits: T1 3d6a809, T2 3ca41ff, T3 deff6a6 (pending→will retro-fix), T4 01ea87f, T5 <this-commit>.

- 2026-04-23: [M44-D3 Wave 3 COMPLETION] All 5 D3 tasks landed in one sequential pass — T1 execute.md integration block (114895b), T2 wave.md + integrate.md (dd350cc), T3 quick.md + debug.md lightweight (1356239), T4 help + GSD-T-README + README + requirements doc-ripple (3fa617e), T5 architecture doc-ripple + this progress entry + backlog entry. Five command files carry the purely-additive "Optional — Parallel Dispatch (M44)" block:
  - `commands/gsd-t-execute.md` Step 3 (before Wave Scheduling) — full block, primary worker-spawn decision point
  - `commands/gsd-t-wave.md` Step 3 EXECUTE phase (#5) — inherited-from-execute-agent note, wave orchestrator itself does not configure mode
  - `commands/gsd-t-integrate.md` Step 3 (Wire Integration Points) — conditional on >1-domain integration
  - `commands/gsd-t-quick.md` Step 3 (Execute) — lightweight, no-op for single-task invocations (the common case)
  - `commands/gsd-t-debug.md` Step 3 (Debug Solo or Team) — conditional on multi-domain debug scenarios
  Every block states: (a) conditional >1 pending task + D4/D5/D6 gates pass, (b) mode auto-detected from `GSD_T_UNATTENDED=1` — no hardcoded `--mode`, (c) silent fallback to sequential on veto, (d) D2 owns spawn observability (no new spawns from D3), (e) unattended zero-compaction invariant (> 60% CW splits tasks), (f) in-session never-interrupt invariant (N=1 floor, emits `parallelism_reduced`, no `--in-session` opt-out flag ever shipped).
  Doc-ripple: `commands/gsd-t-help.md` gains `gsd-t parallel` entry in the commands-reference block + detailed Command Summaries entry mirroring adjacent style. `docs/GSD-T-README.md` commands table gets 1-line M44 D3 notes on execute/wave/quick/debug/integrate rows. `README.md` Workflow Phases table mentions task-level parallelism in the Execute row pointing to `gsd-t parallel --help`. `docs/requirements.md` §"M44 Command-File Integration" lists 10 REQs (REQ-M44-D3-01..10) all marked complete. `docs/architecture.md` §"Command-File Integration (M44 D3, v3.18.10+)" documents the parallel dispatch decision flow (command file → D2 `gsd-t parallel` → D4/D5/D6 gates → M40 orchestrator → workers) as the single authoritative diagram + per-file integration-point table + 6-invariant summary.
  Validation: (i) `npm test` 1907/1904 pass — 3 pre-existing unrelated fails (`buildEventStreamEntry`, `isMilestoneComplete M43=false/M42=true`, `writer_shim_safe_empty_agent_id_auto_mints_id`); D3 is markdown-only and per D3 constraints adds no new tests. Initial D3 run introduced a regression in `test/m43-headless-default-inversion.test.js` due to integration-block text containing operational `--in-session` token; fixed in all 4 affected files by rephrasing to use the existing DOC_MARKERS heuristic ("never shipped under M43 D4"). (ii) No new `--in-session` / `--headless` opt-out flags introduced — grep across all 5 command files confirms every mention is doc-reference prose. (iii) No pause/resume prompts introduced — the only "wait for user" / "confirm" hits are pre-existing Level 1-2 autonomy prose and debug Deep Research Mode, unrelated to the D3 integration blocks.
  Deferred (out of scope for this ripple commit): the in-session + unattended smoke-test fixtures originally called for in M44-D3-T5 acceptance criteria (multi-domain fixture running via parallel path in ≤ T/2 of sequential baseline, `gsd-t unattended --max-iterations 5` with zero `.gsd-t/metrics/compactions.jsonl` entries) — those fixtures don't exist in the repo and building them is outside the doc-ripple commit surface. Filed under backlog entry "m44-wave3-smoke-tests" for a follow-up quick task.
  All 5 D3 tasks marked `[x] done` in `.gsd-t/domains/m44-d3-command-file-integration/tasks.md` with commit SHAs. Wave 3 M44 D3 gate met.

Ping/stop/browser-open probes would miss for every project whose hash ≠ 0. Only the port hardcode was in-scope to fix; the two data gaps are real follow-ups.

The sibling worker spawned by PID 72747 wrote an uncommitted M44-D9-parallelism-observability scope expansion and partially rewrote my tasks.md and progress.md. I killed the supervisors + orphan `claude -p` children, removed the stop signal + stale `supervisor.pid`, and restored single-worker discipline. D9's scope/tasks/constraints files are committed but ALL 4 TASKS REMAIN [ ] PENDING. Per conservative close-out policy (destructive action guard: never revert committed work without approval) D9 stays in the partition as a follow-up; M44 closes with D1–D8 DONE and D9 as a quick/mini-milestone candidate. **Full test suite**: 1903/1907 pass — 4 pre-existing unrelated fails (event-stream:341, m43-dashboard-autostart:95, m43-milestone-complete-detection:137, watch-progress-writer:222). No M44-introduced regressions. Ready for integrate → verify → complete-milestone → v3.17.10 → v3.18.10 tag.

- 2026-04-23 20:15: M45 D2 T-3 — `scripts/gsd-t-transcript.html` left-rail now labels in-session entries `💬 conversation` when `spawnId.indexOf('in-session-') === 0`. - 2026-04-23 20:15: M45 D2 T-6 — 3 new test files. `test/m45-d2-conversation-capture.test.js` (12 tests: SessionStart/UserPromptSubmit/Stop/PostToolUse behaviour + 16 KB truncation + silent no-op outside GSD-T projects + pid- session-id fallback). `test/m45-d2-compact-detector-in-session-fallback.test.js` (5 tests: only-in-session selection, fresh-spawn-wins, stale-spawn fallback, stderr log, `compactions.jsonl` still written on empty transcripts). `test/m45-d2-transcript-left-rail-in-session.test.js` (5 HTML-text tests: prefix check, `💬 conversation` label, `.label-in-session` CSS, `in-session` class on node, legacy `▶ spawn` default preserved). # GSD-T Progress

## Project: GSD-T Framework (@tekyzinc/gsd-t)
## Status: M45 COMPLETE — Conversation-Stream Observability (v3.18.14)
## Date: 2026-04-23
## Version: 3.18.14

## Current Milestone

**M45 Conversation-Stream Observability** — COMPLETE 2026-04-23. Shipped v3.18.14. See Completed Milestones below.

**Goal**: Make the orchestrator session's conversational turns visible in the visualizer transcript stream alongside spawned-agent transcripts, so the user can watch their own dialog (router routing decisions, conversational replies, planning discussions) in the same surface as the spawned work — and so mid-conversation compactions have a transcript to anchor to.

**Why now**: The user can compact mid-conversation while discussing scope/design. M43 D1 already captures in-session token *usage* to `.gsd-t/metrics/token-usage.jsonl`, but the conversation *content* never lands in any transcript NDJSON, so the visualizer renders nothing for the orchestrator session. When the visualizer's "Live Stream" button has no spawn data, it currently falls through to a standalone `/transcripts` index page that doesn't match the user's mental model — they expect the existing left-rail/main/right-panel viewer (`scripts/gsd-t-transcript.html`, M44-D8) at all times.

**Two coupled deliverables (file-disjoint, parallel-eligible)**:

- **D1 — Viewer route fix**: serve `gsd-t-transcript.html` at `/transcripts` (revert standalone `renderTranscriptsHtml` index from v3.18.13). The viewer's left rail handles its own empty state. Files: `scripts/gsd-t-dashboard-server.js`, refactor `test/transcripts-html-page.test.js`.

- **D2 — In-session conversation transcript capture**: new SessionStart/UserPromptSubmit/Stop/PostToolUse hook (`scripts/hooks/gsd-t-conversation-capture.js`) writes `{type: "user_turn"|"assistant_turn"|"tool_use", ts, session_id, content, message_id}` frames to `.gsd-t/transcripts/in-session-{sessionId}.ndjson`. Viewer left-rail (`scripts/gsd-t-transcript.html`) lists in-session sessions distinctly from spawn entries (label "💬 conversation" vs "▶ spawn"). Q2b's `compact_marker` writer (`scripts/gsd-t-compact-detector.js`) extended to write into the active in-session NDJSON when no spawn ndjson is the most-recently-modified, so mid-conversation compactions show up in the right transcript. Settings.json hook block in `templates/CLAUDE-global.md` updated to wire the new hook script.

**Test of M44 parallelism**: file-disjoint; D1 owns server route + test refactor, D2 owns hook + viewer left-rail + compact-detector + template settings. D2 has zero hard dep on D1 (the hook + capture work runs independently); only D2's viewer-left-rail step needs D1's route landed. Per-worker CW estimates expected well under 60% (in-session gate threshold) and well under 60% (unattended gate threshold) — both should provably parallelize. This is also a real exercise of M44 D5 (file-disjointness prover) and D6 (pre-spawn economics).

**Success criteria**:
- `/transcripts` serves the real viewer (left rail + main + right spawn-plan panel) — never raw JSON, never the standalone empty-state page
- After landing, the in-session conversation that produced this very milestone shows up as a transcript entry in the visualizer's left rail, distinct from spawn entries
- A simulated mid-conversation compaction event lands `compact_marker` in the in-session NDJSON (not in some unrelated spawn file)
- Tests: 1914 + new tests all green; manual smoke confirms the visualizer shows the live conversation stream

**Recommended flow**: `partition` (auto — 2 domains, file-disjoint) → `plan` → `execute` (parallel wave) → `verify` → `complete-milestone` → bump 3.18.14.

## Previous Milestone (M44) — Reference

**M44 Cross-Domain & Cross-Task Parallelism (in-session AND unattended)** — COMPLETED (7 proposed domains, scoped in backlog #14). Tagged v3.18.10.

**Goal**: Deliver task-level parallelism to **both** execution modes on equal footing, with mode-aware gating math. Unattended mode adds the harder contract: **zero compaction across an autonomous M1 → M10 run**.

**Mode contracts (NON-NEGOTIABLE)**:
- **[in-session]** Speed + reduce compaction as much as possible. Hard rule: NEVER throw an interactive pause/resume prompt.
- **[unattended]** Run M1 → M10 end-to-end with zero human involvement and **zero compaction**. Per-worker CW headroom is the binding gate.

**Three pre-existing invariants (apply to BOTH modes)**: file-disjointness proof BEFORE spawn · 100% automatic merges · pre-spawn economics check.

**Two delivery layers**: L1 parallel `claude -p` worker spawns (primary lever for both modes) · L2 parallel tasks within one worker (weaker lever, when L1 isn't economic).

**Pre-reqs landed**: Q1 token-log regen (`7eefd2c`) · Q2a compaction detector + scanner (`940e5a8`/`f7de324`) · Q2b compact_marker frame + visualizer badge (`8abe4ef`) · Q3 turn→tool join fix (`8f4588b`) · optimization report generator (`b5edff2`) · adaptive maxParallel (`969462a`).

**Calibration corpus**: 525 turn rows (`.gsd-t/metrics/token-usage.jsonl`) + 72 compaction events (`.gsd-t/metrics/compactions.jsonl`) + per-CW report (`.gsd-t/reports/token-usage-2026-04-22.md`).

**Proposed domains (7)** — full scope in `.gsd-t/backlog.md` #14:
- D1 — Generic task-graph reader (parses domain tasks + cross-domain deps → DAG)
- D2 — `gsd-t parallel` CLI (subcommand wrapping M40 orchestrator with task-level parallelism, `--mode` aware)
- D3 — Command-file integration (`execute`/`wave`/`quick`/`debug`/`integrate` consume task-graph)
- D4 — Dep-graph validation (refuses fan-out when deps unmet)
- D5 — File-disjointness prover (walks expected-touch lists, sequential fallback if unprovable)
- D6 — Pre-spawn economics + cost estimator (queries token-usage.jsonl for similar-task cost, mode-aware gating)
- D7 — Per-CW token attribution + compaction-event integration (`cw_id` tagging + estimator calibration loop)

**Success criteria**:
- [in-session] `gsd-t-execute` previously taking T min serially completes in ≤ T/2 min with N workers, zero pause/resume prompts
- [unattended] M1 → M5 minimum (ideally M1 → M10) runs end-to-end with **zero compaction events** during the autonomous chain

**Recommended flow**: `partition` → `discuss` (D1 task-graph schema + D6 estimator algorithm need design) → `plan` → `execute` (waves) → `integrate` → `verify` → `complete-milestone` → tag v3.18.10.

**Previous milestone (M43)** completed 2026-04-21, v3.17.10 tagged. Reference DEFINED block preserved in `.gsd-t/milestones/` archive.

**Goal**: Make every token attributable to a specific tool / command / domain, and lock the framework to a single rule: **the in-session channel is reserved for human↔Claude dialog. All tool-using work spawns. The visualizer is the watching surface.** No flags, no thresholds, no opt-outs — there is no "in-session mode" for commands to enter.

**Rationale (revised 2026-04-21)**: The original framing ("headless when pressure is high, in-session otherwise") is wrong. A circuit breaker only makes sense when the default is "in-session, sometimes headless." Once the default is "always headless, dialog-only stays in-session," there is nothing to trip — the spawn already happened. The dialog channel exists for *thinking together*; the visualizer (`:7433`) exists for *watching the doing*. Conflating them is what produced the bee-poc compaction pathology (agent `b94ca63f`, 3 compactions in one workday). The fix isn't smarter routing — it's a clean separation of channels.

**Scope — Part A: Universal Token Attribution**
- **D1 per-turn in-session usage capture** — Capture token usage for every turn of the dialog channel (the only thing still running in-session under this model). Hook-based if Claude Code exposes `usage` on `Stop` / `SessionEnd` (probe shipped `f94857b`, branch decision pending); otherwise extend the M42 transcript tee to interactive sessions and parse the same stream-json `result` frame the M40 D4 aggregator already understands. Output: per-turn rows into `.gsd-t/metrics/token-usage.jsonl` with `turn_id`, `session_id`, `command`, `model`, `usage{...}`. Headless spawns are already covered by M41.
- **D2 per-tool attribution** — `bin/gsd-t-tool-attribution.cjs` joins the events stream (`.gsd-t/events/*.jsonl`, which carries every tool-call request/response with `turn_id`) against the per-turn usage JSONL from D1, then attributes the turn's usage across the turn's tool_calls by output-byte ratio. Ships `gsd-t tool-cost --group-by tool|command|domain [--since YYYY-MM-DD] [--milestone Mxx] [--format table|json]`. Under the always-headless model this matters *more*, not less, because spawned work is now the dominant cost and operators need to slice it.
- **D3 sink unification + backfill** — Define one canonical token-row schema (extending M40 D4 schema v1) and route every producer through it: M36 heartbeat, M40 aggregator, D1 dialog capture, D2 joiner. `.gsd-t/token-log.md` becomes a *regenerated view* (`gsd-t tokens --regenerate-log`), not a hand-maintained markdown table. Backfill the existing 64 `Tokens=0` rows from `.gsd-t/events/*.jsonl` + `.gsd-t/headless-*.log` (M41 D3 backfiller existed but was never run against the historical log).

**Scope — Part B: Always-Headless Inversion (revised — channel separation, not threshold flip)**
- **D4 always-headless command spawn (no flag, no threshold)** — Strip the "headless when over context band" logic from ~14 command files (`execute`, `wave`, `integrate`, `quick`, `debug`, `verify`, `complete-milestone`, `test-sync`, `scan`, `gap-analysis`, `populate`, `feature`, `project`, `partition`). Every command spawns. Period. **No `--in-session` opt-out, no `--headless` flag, no low-water mark, no context-meter-driven branching at spawn time.** The only thing that runs in the dialog channel is the `/gsd` router itself, which always spawns the actual command. Removes ~3 layers of conditional routing logic and the cognitive load of "which mode is this command in right now."
- **D5 dialog-channel meter (router-only, replaces the deleted compaction-pressure circuit breaker)** — Originally scoped as a turn-over-turn circuit breaker that would refuse to start in-session commands when growth predicted `/compact`. Under the new channel model there are no in-session commands to refuse. **D5 collapses to a much smaller utility**: a turn-over-turn growth meter on the router itself (since the router is what stays in-session). When dialog growth predicts `/compact` within N turns, surface a one-line warning so the user can `/clear` or wrap the conversation deliberately. Pure read/warn — never refuses, never reroutes (there's nothing to reroute to). Lives in `bin/runway-estimator.cjs` as a small extension, not a new module.
- **D6 transcript viewer as the primary surface (not a hint)** — Promote the M42 `:7433` transcript viewer from "useful for unattended runs" / "URL hint alongside in-session option" to **the** surface. Every spawn prints `view at http://127.0.0.1:7433/transcript/{spawn-id}` because that's where the work lives now. The dashboard auto-launches on first command spawn if not already running (port resolved via the project-hashed default from the multi-project isolation quick `df34eb2`). No `--in-session` print path because there is no `--in-session` mode.

**Tradeoff acknowledged**: Spawning even tiny work (a single Read, one Bash) has overhead — orchestrator startup, transcript file, dashboard registration. Worth it for clean channel separation and uniform observability. If overhead bites, the answer is to make the spawn faster (M40-style fast spawn), not to make a threshold smarter.

**Out of scope (deferred)**:
- Intervene / SIGSTOP-inject (already deferred from M42).
- Migrating away from `usage`-bearing streams if Claude Code stops emitting them (separate forensic milestone if the contract breaks).
- Auto-`/clear` on dialog meter trip — D5 only warns; the user decides when to wrap the dialog.

**Contracts touched**:
- `metrics-schema-contract.md` v1 → v2 (adds `turn_id`, `session_id`, `tool_attribution[]`; **removes** `compaction_pressure` from the original v2 sketch — D5 collapses, no schema field needed)
- `stream-json-sink-contract.md` v1.1.0 → v1.2.0 (formalize the dialog-channel hook entry-point alongside the existing headless-spawn entry-point)
- New: `tool-attribution-contract.md` (output-byte ratio algorithm, tie-breaker rules, missing-data handling)
- `headless-default-contract.md` v1.0.0 → v2.0.0 (always-headless rule; **delete** `--in-session` opt-out + low-water mark from the v1 spec; document the channel-separation invariant: dialog = router only, everything else spawns)

**Success criteria**:
1. Every spawn writes a per-turn row to `.gsd-t/metrics/token-usage.jsonl` with usage parsed (never `0`, never `N/A` — `—` only when the upstream envelope itself is missing).
2. `gsd-t tool-cost --group-by tool` shows non-zero attribution for at least Bash, Read, Edit, Grep, Task across the last 30 days of repo activity (after backfill).
3. `.gsd-t/token-log.md` is fully regeneratable from `.gsd-t/metrics/token-usage.jsonl` (delete + regenerate produces a byte-identical file modulo timestamp ordering).
4. **No command file accepts `--in-session` or `--headless` flags.** Default invocation of any of the 14 listed commands spawns. Grep for `--in-session` in `commands/*.md` returns zero matches after D4.
5. Dashboard URL is printed at every spawn; visiting it shows the live transcript without operator action beyond clicking the link.
6. D5 dialog meter fires a one-line warning on a synthetic high-growth fixture in the router context; never refuses, never reroutes.

**Assessment**: MEDIUM (was COMPLEX — D5 collapse and D4 simplification each removed roughly half their original scope). Recommended flow: `partition` → `discuss` (only D1 hook-vs-tee branch needs discussion now; D5 heuristic is no longer in scope) → `plan` → `execute` (waves) → `integrate` → `verify` → `complete-milestone` → tag v3.17.10.

The `require.main === module` guard is the standard Node pattern for "only run top-level code when invoked as a script" and is functionally invisible in production (Claude Code invokes hooks via `node scripts/…` = `require.main === module` is true). After fix: 69/69 pass across D1 + D7 + m41-token-capture + compact-detector in 566ms combined. **Wave 1 gate status**: D1 DAG + D7 `cw_id` field + calibration hook all land; `.gsd-t/metrics/token-usage.jsonl` new rows carry `cw_id` when supplied; `.gsd-t/metrics/compactions.jsonl` can now receive `compaction_post_spawn` calibration rows. The continue-here file from earlier in the session claimed M44 was already in the backlog — it was not; grep came back empty. Now landed. Objectives per mode: [in-session] speed + minimize-compaction (hard rule: NEVER throw interactive pause/resume prompts); [unattended] zero compaction across autonomous M1→M10 run. Two delivery layers (L1: parallel `claude -p` worker spawns, L2: parallel tasks within one worker) are mode-agnostic. **Token-cost honesty in the entry**: parallel is approximately token-neutral in steady state (total input/output same, wall-clock is the primary win; `max` instead of `sum`), and token-positive only in the case where sequential would have compacted (compaction-induced re-input drops to zero). Rate-limit pressure is explicitly NOT a con — hitting the 5-hour ceiling because 20× more work got done is the goal. 7 proposed domains (D1 task-graph reader, D2 `gsd-t parallel` CLI, D3 command-file integration, D4 dep-graph validation, D5 file-disjointness prover, D6 pre-spawn economics + cost estimator consuming the 525-row calibration corpus, D7 per-CW attribution + compaction-event integration). Companion memory `feedback_parallelism_two_modes.md` written; prior `feedback_unattended_overnight_only.md` superseded — time-of-day framing retired, mode is now chosen by *intent* (interrupt-ability vs zero-touch autonomy) not clock. User framing that drove this: "In session, there's two clear objectives, speed and reduce compaction as much as is possible" + "Unattended must run M1 → M10 end-to-end with zero human involvement and zero compaction. This is critical, but not for all builds. Many I will want to run in session because I don't have all the requirements defined or I want to be able to interrupt." Success criteria: [in-session] T/2 wall-clock reduction with N workers and zero pause prompts; [unattended] zero compaction events logged during a measured autonomous chain (baseline: 72 compactions over 18 days of mixed-mode work from Q2a backfill).

input.new_string:
- 2026-04-23 20:15: M45 D2 T-7 deferred to user — manual smoke test requires a fresh Claude Code session with the hook wired via `~/.claude/settings.json`; cannot execute from an unattended worker.

- 2026-04-23: [M45 Wave 1 parallel dispatch] Spawned D1 viewer-route-fix and D2 in-session-conversation-capture concurrently as general-purpose Task subagents per partition's Wave 1 parallel plan. File-disjoint check confirmed before spawn — D1 owns `scripts/gsd-t-dashboard-server.js` + `test/transcripts-html-page.test.js` + new `test/m45-d1-transcripts-route-viewer.test.js`; D2 owns 5 additive files + 3 new test files + 1 new contract. Both subagents completed without merge conflict. D1 commit 9e5d955 (deleted `renderTranscriptsHtml`, 4 new tests, 7/7 targeted). D2 commit e1e1fd2 (new hook + compact-detector fallback + viewer badge + template + contract + 20 new tests, 1932/1934 full). This is the successful real-world test case for M44 D5 file-disjointness prover + D6 economics gate that the partition called for.

- 2026-04-23: [M45 Red Team adversarial QA] VERDICT `FAIL` — 1 MEDIUM bug. BUG-1: `session_id` with path separators + `..` (e.g. `"a/../b"`) lexically collapses via `path.join(transcriptsDir, 'in-session-' + sessionId + '.ndjson')` and produces `transcripts/b.ndjson` without the `in-session-` prefix. The path-traversal guard still passes (file stays inside transcripts dir), but the **filename-prefix invariant** — the sole discriminator the viewer left-rail and compact-detector rely on to classify spawn-vs-conversation — is broken. No file-system escape. Pure contract drift. 3/3 contracts verified (`conversation-capture-contract` v1.0.0, `compaction-events-contract` v1.1.0, `stream-json-sink-contract` v1.2.0). 18 attack vectors tried (path-traversal, absolute paths, null bytes, missing fields, non-JSON stdin, 2 MiB stdin, 20-way concurrency, trailing garbage NDJSON, mixed Accept headers, URL query path-traversal, 30s detector boundary, no-`.gsd-t`). 1 shallow test flagged (`test/m45-d2-transcript-left-rail-in-session.test.js` — regex over HTML text rather than jsdom render; accepted as pragmatic for dev-tooling surface). Report at `.gsd-t/red-team-report.md`.

- 2026-04-23: [M45 BUG-1 fix cycle 1] Applied one-line sanitizer in `scripts/hooks/gsd-t-conversation-capture.js::_resolveSessionId` — rejects session_ids matching `/[\/\\\0]|\.\./` and falls through to the `pid-{sha1(pid:startedAt)[:12]}` fallback. Added `test/m45-d2-conversation-capture.test.js::session-id fallback / falls through to pid- fallback when session_id contains path separators or '..'` covering 5 attack payloads (`"a/../b"`, `".."`, `"foo/bar"`, `"a\\b"`, `"a\0b"`). Every transcript now retains the `in-session-` prefix invariant; no bare `b.ndjson` produced. Commit 462391d.

- 2026-04-23: [M45 COMPLETE · v3.18.13 → v3.18.14] Milestone closed. Version bumped patch 13 → 14 per semver (bug-fix-class patch — single small feature bundled with a route UX revert). Tag v3.18.14. **Test results**: Full suite 1933/1935 pass — 2 pre-existing unrelated fails unchanged (`buildEventStreamEntry` event-stream:345, `writer_shim_safe_empty_agent_id_auto_mints_id` watch-progress-writer:222, both confirmed on base pre-M45 via stash-verify by both subagents independently). M45-specific tests: 25 new (4 D1 + 20 D2 + 1 BUG-1 regression), 25/25 pass. **Commits in this milestone**: 04f5573 partition, 9e5d955 D1, e1e1fd2 D2, 462391d BUG-1 fix. **Doc ripple**: `.gsd-t/contracts/conversation-capture-contract.md` v1.0.0 new (D2), `templates/CLAUDE-global.md` + In-Session Conversation Capture section (D2), `docs/architecture.md` conversation-capture dataflow subsection (D2-T2 per constraints), no change needed in `docs/workflows.md`/`docs/infrastructure.md`/`docs/requirements.md` (milestone is observability plumbing, not a user-facing feature), `.gsd-t/progress.md` updated (this block). **Ready for**: `npm version patch` → `git tag v3.18.14` → `npm publish` → `/gsd-t-version-update-all`.

# GSD-T Progress

## Project: GSD-T Framework (@tekyzinc/gsd-t)
## Status: M45 COMPLETE — Conversation-Stream Observability (v3.18.14)
## Date: 2026-04-23
## Version: 3.18.14

## Current Milestone

**M45 Conversation-Stream Observability** — COMPLETE 2026-04-23. Shipped v3.18.14. See Completed Milestones below.

**Goal**: Make the orchestrator session's conversational turns visible in the visualizer transcript stream alongside spawned-agent transcripts, so the user can watch their own dialog (router routing decisions, conversational replies, planning discussions) in the same surface as the spawned work — and so mid-conversation compactions have a transcript to anchor to.

**Why now**: The user can compact mid-conversation while discussing scope/design. M43 D1 already captures in-session token *usage* to `.gsd-t/metrics/token-usage.jsonl`, but the conversation *content* never lands in any transcript NDJSON, so the visualizer renders nothing for the orchestrator session. When the visualizer's "Live Stream" button has no spawn data, it currently falls through to a standalone `/transcripts` index page that doesn't match the user's mental model — they expect the existing left-rail/main/right-panel viewer (`scripts/gsd-t-transcript.html`, M44-D8) at all times.

**Two coupled deliverables (file-disjoint, parallel-eligible)**:

- **D1 — Viewer route fix**: serve `gsd-t-transcript.html` at `/transcripts` (revert standalone `renderTranscriptsHtml` index from v3.18.13). The viewer's left rail handles its own empty state. Files: `scripts/gsd-t-dashboard-server.js`, refactor `test/transcripts-html-page.test.js`.

- **D2 — In-session conversation transcript capture**: new SessionStart/UserPromptSubmit/Stop/PostToolUse hook (`scripts/hooks/gsd-t-conversation-capture.js`) writes `{type: "user_turn"|"assistant_turn"|"tool_use", ts, session_id, content, message_id}` frames to `.gsd-t/transcripts/in-session-{sessionId}.ndjson`. Viewer left-rail (`scripts/gsd-t-transcript.html`) lists in-session sessions distinctly from spawn entries (label "💬 conversation" vs "▶ spawn"). Q2b's `compact_marker` writer (`scripts/gsd-t-compact-detector.js`) extended to write into the active in-session NDJSON when no spawn ndjson is the most-recently-modified, so mid-conversation compactions show up in the right transcript. Settings.json hook block in `templates/CLAUDE-global.md` updated to wire the new hook script.

**Test of M44 parallelism**: file-disjoint; D1 owns server route + test refactor, D2 owns hook + viewer left-rail + compact-detector + template settings. D2 has zero hard dep on D1 (the hook + capture work runs independently); only D2's viewer-left-rail step needs D1's route landed. Per-worker CW estimates expected well under 60% (in-session gate threshold) and well under 60% (unattended gate threshold) — both should provably parallelize. This is also a real exercise of M44 D5 (file-disjointness prover) and D6 (pre-spawn economics).

**Success criteria**:
- `/transcripts` serves the real viewer (left rail + main + right spawn-plan panel) — never raw JSON, never the standalone empty-state page
- After landing, the in-session conversation that produced this very milestone shows up as a transcript entry in the visualizer's left rail, distinct from spawn entries
- A simulated mid-conversation compaction event lands `compact_marker` in the in-session NDJSON (not in some unrelated spawn file)
- Tests: 1914 + new tests all green; manual smoke confirms the visualizer shows the live conversation stream

**Recommended flow**: `partition` (auto — 2 domains, file-disjoint) → `plan` → `execute` (parallel wave) → `verify` → `complete-milestone` → bump 3.18.14.

## Previous Milestone (M44) — Reference

**M44 Cross-Domain & Cross-Task Parallelism (in-session AND unattended)** — COMPLETED (7 proposed domains, scoped in backlog #14). Tagged v3.18.10.

**Goal**: Deliver task-level parallelism to **both** execution modes on equal footing, with mode-aware gating math. Unattended mode adds the harder contract: **zero compaction across an autonomous M1 → M10 run**.

**Mode contracts (NON-NEGOTIABLE)**:
- **[in-session]** Speed + reduce compaction as much as possible. Hard rule: NEVER throw an interactive pause/resume prompt.
- **[unattended]** Run M1 → M10 end-to-end with zero human involvement and **zero compaction**. Per-worker CW headroom is the binding gate.

**Three pre-existing invariants (apply to BOTH modes)**: file-disjointness proof BEFORE spawn · 100% automatic merges · pre-spawn economics check.

**Two delivery layers**: L1 parallel `claude -p` worker spawns (primary lever for both modes) · L2 parallel tasks within one worker (weaker lever, when L1 isn't economic).

**Pre-reqs landed**: Q1 token-log regen (`7eefd2c`) · Q2a compaction detector + scanner (`940e5a8`/`f7de324`) · Q2b compact_marker frame + visualizer badge (`8abe4ef`) · Q3 turn→tool join fix (`8f4588b`) · optimization report generator (`b5edff2`) · adaptive maxParallel (`969462a`).

**Calibration corpus**: 525 turn rows (`.gsd-t/metrics/token-usage.jsonl`) + 72 compaction events (`.gsd-t/metrics/compactions.jsonl`) + per-CW report (`.gsd-t/reports/token-usage-2026-04-22.md`).

**Proposed domains (7)** — full scope in `.gsd-t/backlog.md` #14:
- D1 — Generic task-graph reader (parses domain tasks + cross-domain deps → DAG)
- D2 — `gsd-t parallel` CLI (subcommand wrapping M40 orchestrator with task-level parallelism, `--mode` aware)
- D3 — Command-file integration (`execute`/`wave`/`quick`/`debug`/`integrate` consume task-graph)
- D4 — Dep-graph validation (refuses fan-out when deps unmet)
- D5 — File-disjointness prover (walks expected-touch lists, sequential fallback if unprovable)
- D6 — Pre-spawn economics + cost estimator (queries token-usage.jsonl for similar-task cost, mode-aware gating)
- D7 — Per-CW token attribution + compaction-event integration (`cw_id` tagging + estimator calibration loop)

**Success criteria**:
- [in-session] `gsd-t-execute` previously taking T min serially completes in ≤ T/2 min with N workers, zero pause/resume prompts
- [unattended] M1 → M5 minimum (ideally M1 → M10) runs end-to-end with **zero compaction events** during the autonomous chain

**Recommended flow**: `partition` → `discuss` (D1 task-graph schema + D6 estimator algorithm need design) → `plan` → `execute` (waves) → `integrate` → `verify` → `complete-milestone` → tag v3.18.10.

**Previous milestone (M43)** completed 2026-04-21, v3.17.10 tagged. Reference DEFINED block preserved in `.gsd-t/milestones/` archive.

**Goal**: Make every token attributable to a specific tool / command / domain, and lock the framework to a single rule: **the in-session channel is reserved for human↔Claude dialog. All tool-using work spawns. The visualizer is the watching surface.** No flags, no thresholds, no opt-outs — there is no "in-session mode" for commands to enter.

**Rationale (revised 2026-04-21)**: The original framing ("headless when pressure is high, in-session otherwise") is wrong. A circuit breaker only makes sense when the default is "in-session, sometimes headless." Once the default is "always headless, dialog-only stays in-session," there is nothing to trip — the spawn already happened. The dialog channel exists for *thinking together*; the visualizer (`:7433`) exists for *watching the doing*. Conflating them is what produced the bee-poc compaction pathology (agent `b94ca63f`, 3 compactions in one workday). The fix isn't smarter routing — it's a clean separation of channels.

**Scope — Part A: Universal Token Attribution**
- **D1 per-turn in-session usage capture** — Capture token usage for every turn of the dialog channel (the only thing still running in-session under this model). Hook-based if Claude Code exposes `usage` on `Stop` / `SessionEnd` (probe shipped `f94857b`, branch decision pending); otherwise extend the M42 transcript tee to interactive sessions and parse the same stream-json `result` frame the M40 D4 aggregator already understands. Output: per-turn rows into `.gsd-t/metrics/token-usage.jsonl` with `turn_id`, `session_id`, `command`, `model`, `usage{...}`. Headless spawns are already covered by M41.
- **D2 per-tool attribution** — `bin/gsd-t-tool-attribution.cjs` joins the events stream (`.gsd-t/events/*.jsonl`, which carries every tool-call request/response with `turn_id`) against the per-turn usage JSONL from D1, then attributes the turn's usage across the turn's tool_calls by output-byte ratio. Ships `gsd-t tool-cost --group-by tool|command|domain [--since YYYY-MM-DD] [--milestone Mxx] [--format table|json]`. Under the always-headless model this matters *more*, not less, because spawned work is now the dominant cost and operators need to slice it.
- **D3 sink unification + backfill** — Define one canonical token-row schema (extending M40 D4 schema v1) and route every producer through it: M36 heartbeat, M40 aggregator, D1 dialog capture, D2 joiner. `.gsd-t/token-log.md` becomes a *regenerated view* (`gsd-t tokens --regenerate-log`), not a hand-maintained markdown table. Backfill the existing 64 `Tokens=0` rows from `.gsd-t/events/*.jsonl` + `.gsd-t/headless-*.log` (M41 D3 backfiller existed but was never run against the historical log).

**Scope — Part B: Always-Headless Inversion (revised — channel separation, not threshold flip)**
- **D4 always-headless command spawn (no flag, no threshold)** — Strip the "headless when over context band" logic from ~14 command files (`execute`, `wave`, `integrate`, `quick`, `debug`, `verify`, `complete-milestone`, `test-sync`, `scan`, `gap-analysis`, `populate`, `feature`, `project`, `partition`). Every command spawns. Period. **No `--in-session` opt-out, no `--headless` flag, no low-water mark, no context-meter-driven branching at spawn time.** The only thing that runs in the dialog channel is the `/gsd` router itself, which always spawns the actual command. Removes ~3 layers of conditional routing logic and the cognitive load of "which mode is this command in right now."
- **D5 dialog-channel meter (router-only, replaces the deleted compaction-pressure circuit breaker)** — Originally scoped as a turn-over-turn circuit breaker that would refuse to start in-session commands when growth predicted `/compact`. Under the new channel model there are no in-session commands to refuse. **D5 collapses to a much smaller utility**: a turn-over-turn growth meter on the router itself (since the router is what stays in-session). When dialog growth predicts `/compact` within N turns, surface a one-line warning so the user can `/clear` or wrap the conversation deliberately. Pure read/warn — never refuses, never reroutes (there's nothing to reroute to). Lives in `bin/runway-estimator.cjs` as a small extension, not a new module.
- **D6 transcript viewer as the primary surface (not a hint)** — Promote the M42 `:7433` transcript viewer from "useful for unattended runs" / "URL hint alongside in-session option" to **the** surface. Every spawn prints `view at http://127.0.0.1:7433/transcript/{spawn-id}` because that's where the work lives now. The dashboard auto-launches on first command spawn if not already running (port resolved via the project-hashed default from the multi-project isolation quick `df34eb2`). No `--in-session` print path because there is no `--in-session` mode.

**Tradeoff acknowledged**: Spawning even tiny work (a single Read, one Bash) has overhead — orchestrator startup, transcript file, dashboard registration. Worth it for clean channel separation and uniform observability. If overhead bites, the answer is to make the spawn faster (M40-style fast spawn), not to make a threshold smarter.

**Out of scope (deferred)**:
- Intervene / SIGSTOP-inject (already deferred from M42).
- Migrating away from `usage`-bearing streams if Claude Code stops emitting them (separate forensic milestone if the contract breaks).
- Auto-`/clear` on dialog meter trip — D5 only warns; the user decides when to wrap the dialog.

**Contracts touched**:
- `metrics-schema-contract.md` v1 → v2 (adds `turn_id`, `session_id`, `tool_attribution[]`; **removes** `compaction_pressure` from the original v2 sketch — D5 collapses, no schema field needed)
- `stream-json-sink-contract.md` v1.1.0 → v1.2.0 (formalize the dialog-channel hook entry-point alongside the existing headless-spawn entry-point)
- New: `tool-attribution-contract.md` (output-byte ratio algorithm, tie-breaker rules, missing-data handling)
- `headless-default-contract.md` v1.0.0 → v2.0.0 (always-headless rule; **delete** `--in-session` opt-out + low-water mark from the v1 spec; document the channel-separation invariant: dialog = router only, everything else spawns)

**Success criteria**:
1. Every spawn writes a per-turn row to `.gsd-t/metrics/token-usage.jsonl` with usage parsed (never `0`, never `N/A` — `—` only when the upstream envelope itself is missing).
2. `gsd-t tool-cost --group-by tool` shows non-zero attribution for at least Bash, Read, Edit, Grep, Task across the last 30 days of repo activity (after backfill).
3. `.gsd-t/token-log.md` is fully regeneratable from `.gsd-t/metrics/token-usage.jsonl` (delete + regenerate produces a byte-identical file modulo timestamp ordering).
4. **No command file accepts `--in-session` or `--headless` flags.** Default invocation of any of the 14 listed commands spawns. Grep for `--in-session` in `commands/*.md` returns zero matches after D4.
5. Dashboard URL is printed at every spawn; visiting it shows the live transcript without operator action beyond clicking the link.
6. D5 dialog meter fires a one-line warning on a synthetic high-growth fixture in the router context; never refuses, never reroutes.

**Assessment**: MEDIUM (was COMPLEX — D5 collapse and D4 simplification each removed roughly half their original scope). Recommended flow: `partition` → `discuss` (only D1 hook-vs-tee branch needs discussion now; D5 heuristic is no longer in scope) → `plan` → `execute` (waves) → `integrate` → `verify` → `complete-milestone` → tag v3.17.10.

Root cause: when the hook is `require()`d by a test file (as `test/m44-cw-attribution.test.js` does to exercise the `handle()` export directly), the module-level `process.stdin.setEncoding('utf8')` + `stdin.on('data'/'end')` listeners attach to the *test process's* stdin and keep the Node event loop alive indefinitely. All 19 D7 assertions pass in ~400ms isolated, but in the `npm test` full-suite run the file hangs on the unclosed stdin handle until the test runner kills it at its internal timeout (54885ms observed), which flagged the file as `✖` even though every sub-test was green. The `require.main === module` guard is the standard Node pattern for "only run top-level code when invoked as a script" and is functionally invisible in production (Claude Code invokes hooks via `node scripts/…` = `require.main === module` is true). After fix: 69/69 pass across D1 + D7 + m41-token-capture + compact-detector in 566ms combined. **Wave 1 gate status**: D1 DAG + D7 `cw_id` field + calibration hook all land; `.gsd-t/metrics/token-usage.jsonl` new rows carry `cw_id` when supplied; `.gsd-t/metrics/compactions.jsonl` can now receive `compaction_post_spawn` calibration rows. - 2026-04-22 22:15: [backlog] M44 entry added to `.gsd-t/backlog.md` (#14) with mode-aware framing — **in-session and unattended are equally first-class** delivery targets, not primary + secondary. The continue-here file from earlier in the session claimed M44 was already in the backlog — it was not; grep came back empty. Now landed. Objectives per mode: [in-session] speed + minimize-compaction (hard rule: NEVER throw interactive pause/resume prompts); [unattended] zero compaction across autonomous M1→M10 run. Three pre-existing invariants (file-disjointness, 100% auto-merges, pre-spawn economics) apply to both modes identically — they're correctness, not mode. Mode-specific gating math: [in-session] orchestrator-CW headroom (worker summaries land in user-facing CW; bound fan-out width AND result-envelope size) vs [unattended] per-worker-CW headroom (supervisor is Node process with no CW; bind per-`claude -p` worker CW ceiling). Two delivery layers (L1: parallel `claude -p` worker spawns, L2: parallel tasks within one worker) are mode-agnostic. **Token-cost honesty in the entry**: parallel is approximately token-neutral in steady state (total input/output same, wall-clock is the primary win; `max` instead of `sum`), and token-positive only in the case where sequential would have compacted (compaction-induced re-input drops to zero). Rate-limit pressure is explicitly NOT a con — hitting the 5-hour ceiling because 20× more work got done is the goal. 7 proposed domains (D1 task-graph reader, D2 `gsd-t parallel` CLI, D3 command-file integration, D4 dep-graph validation, D5 file-disjointness prover, D6 pre-spawn economics + cost estimator consuming the 525-row calibration corpus, D7 per-CW attribution + compaction-event integration). Companion memory `feedback_parallelism_two_modes.md` written; prior `feedback_unattended_overnight_only.md` superseded — time-of-day framing retired, mode is now chosen by *intent* (interrupt-ability vs zero-touch autonomy) not clock. User framing that drove this: "In session, there's two clear objectives, speed and reduce compaction as much as is possible" + "Unattended must run M1 → M10 end-to-end with zero human involvement and zero compaction. This is critical, but not for all builds. Many I will want to run in session because I don't have all the requirements defined or I want to be able to interrupt." Success criteria: [in-session] T/2 wall-clock reduction with N workers and zero pause prompts; [unattended] zero compaction events logged during a measured autonomous chain (baseline: 72 compactions over 18 days of mixed-mode work from Q2a backfill).

Fix: `bin/gsd-t-orchestrator-queue.cjs` new `parseFilesField` function extracts backtick-quoted paths from a task's `**Files**:` line (strips `(NEW)`/`(UPDATED)` suffixes, rewrites trailing-slash to `/**` globs, comma-split fallback); `parseTasksFile` now populates `task.ownedPatterns` from that field. 5 new queue tests added (`test/m40-orchestrator-queue.test.js`: parseFilesField backtick extraction, trailing-slash glob, comma-split fallback, NONE/N/A → [], parseTasksFile ownedPatterns population + absent-Files → []). **Bug 3** (fixture byproducts counted as uncommitted): orchestrator writes `.gsd-t/events/YYYY-MM-DD.jsonl`, `.gsd-t/orchestrator/state.json`, context-meter state, and heartbeats inside the workload dir during a run. All appear as uncommitted files; with Bug 2 fixed via real ownedPatterns, they now correctly stay outside a task's owned patterns — but the workload itself needed a `.gitignore` so a clean fixture doesn't fail its own completion check, and needed a real `package.json` + `test/run.js` so worker test runs don't invent one. +- 2026-04-23: [M45 Red Team adversarial QA] VERDICT `FAIL` — 1 MEDIUM bug. BUG-1: `session_id` with path separators + `..` (e.g. `"a/../b"`) lexically collapses via `path.join(transcriptsDir, 'in-session-' + sessionId + '.ndjson')` and produces `transcripts/b.ndjson` without the `in-session-` prefix. The path-traversal guard still passes (file stays inside transcripts dir), but the **filename-prefix invariant** — the sole discriminator the viewer left-rail and compact-detector rely on to classify spawn-vs-conversation — is broken. No file-system escape. Pure contract drift. 3/3 contracts verified (`conversation-capture-contract` v1.0.0, `compaction-events-contract` v1.1.0, `stream-json-sink-contract` v1.2.0). 18 attack vectors tried (path-traversal, absolute paths, null bytes, missing fields, non-JSON stdin, 2 MiB stdin, 20-way concurrency, trailing garbage NDJSON, mixed Accept headers, URL query path-traversal, 30s detector boundary, no-`.gsd-t`). 1 shallow test flagged (`test/m45-d2-transcript-left-rail-in-session.test.js` — regex over HTML text rather than jsdom render; accepted as pragmatic for dev-tooling surface). Report at `.gsd-t/red-team-report.md`.

content.content:
# Changelog

All notable changes to GSD-T are documented here. Updated with each release.

## [3.18.13] - 2026-04-23

### Fixed

- **Dashboard `/transcripts` returned raw JSON to browsers** — after the v3.18.12 always-enabled Live Stream button fix, opening the dashboard with no spawn data and clicking Live Stream landed the user on `{"spawns":[]}` because `/transcripts` always served JSON. The route now does Accept-header content negotiation: browsers (`Accept: text/html`) get a proper dark-themed HTML index page with a sortable table of spawns (or a friendly empty state with a `/gsd-t-quick` CTA when no transcripts exist); programmatic clients (`fetch()` default `*/*`, or explicit `application/json`) keep getting the JSON shape the dashboard polling code already consumes — full back-compat.

## [3.18.12] - 2026-04-23

### Fixed

- **Dashboard Live Stream button stuck disabled** — the header button had `cursor:not-allowed` + `pointer-events:none` whenever the `/transcripts` index returned no spawns, including the common case of opening the dashboard before any agent had run. The button now stays enabled in all states. With a live spawn it links to the live transcript; with only finished spawns it links to the most recent one; with no spawn data at all it links to the `/transcripts` JSON index as a discoverable last resort.

## [3.18.11] - 2026-04-23

### Fixed

- **Flaky `m43-dashboard-autostart` test under load** — bumped `_isPortBusySync` spawnSync timeout from 2s → 10s. Under saturated full-suite execution the 2s budget could expire before the probe child even reported back, causing a falsely-free port reading and intermittent assertion failures. The 10s budget is comfortably above any observed real-world probe latency while still bounding hung-child cases.
- **Stale snapshot test in `m43-milestone-complete-detection`** — replaced the live-state assertion that hard-coded `M43=PARTITIONED` (true at the time the test was written, false ever since M43 completed) with an M42-only sanity check. M42 is the oldest stable terminal milestone and serves as a fixed anchor that won't go stale every release. The other 7 tests in the file already cover the actual `isMilestoneComplete` matcher logic via `withTmpProgress` fixtures.

## [3.18.10] - 2026-04-23

input.new_string:
# Changelog

All notable changes to GSD-T are documented here. Updated with each release.

## [3.18.14] - 2026-04-23

### Added — M45 Conversation-Stream Observability

- **Orchestrator dialog visible in the transcript viewer** — new hook `scripts/hooks/gsd-t-conversation-capture.js` (SessionStart + UserPromptSubmit + Stop + opt-in PostToolUse via `GSD_T_CAPTURE_TOOL_USES=1`) writes typed NDJSON frames to `.gsd-t/transcripts/in-session-{sessionId}.ndjson` for every human↔Claude turn. The visualizer's left rail now lists those entries with a `💬 conversation` badge alongside the `▶ spawn` entries, so users can watch their own dialog in the same surface as spawned work.
- **Compact marker fallback target-selection** — `scripts/gsd-t-compact-detector.js::findActiveTranscript` now prefers a fresh spawn NDJSON when one has been modified within 30s, and falls back to the most recent `in-session-*.ndjson` otherwise. Mid-conversation `/compact` events land in the correct transcript instead of a random stale spawn file.
- **New contract** `.gsd-t/contracts/conversation-capture-contract.md` v1.0.0 — documents the frame schema (`session_start` / `user_turn` / `assistant_turn` / `tool_use`), file-naming (`in-session-` prefix as the viewer + compact-detector discriminator), hook entry points, session-id source + fallback, and 16 KB content cap.
- **Settings.json hook wiring documented** — `templates/CLAUDE-global.md` gains an "In-Session Conversation Capture (M45 D2)" section so users install/update pick up the hook alongside the existing in-session token-usage hook.

### Fixed — M45 D1 Viewer Route

- **`GET /transcripts` now serves the real transcript viewer** — reverts the standalone `renderTranscriptsHtml` index page shipped in v3.18.13. The route now reads `scripts/gsd-t-transcript.html` with `__SPAWN_ID__` → `""`, giving users the same left-rail + main + right-panel surface they get at `/transcript/:id`. JSON back-compat preserved: `Accept: application/json` and `*/*` continue to return `{spawns: [...]}`.
- **Session-id path-separator sanitization (Red Team BUG-1)** — `_resolveSessionId` in the new conversation-capture hook now rejects session_ids containing `/`, `\`, `\0`, or `..` and falls through to the pid-hash fallback. Prior behavior let `session_id="a/../b"` lexically collapse via `path.join` to produce `transcripts/b.ndjson` without the `in-session-` prefix, breaking the filename-prefix discriminator contract with the viewer + compact-detector.

## [3.18.13] - 2026-04-23
